text.NABRE: 6:8 A priest shall then take from the grain offering a handful of bran flour and oil, together with all the frankincense that is on it, and this he shall burn on the altar as a token of the offering, a sweet aroma to the LORD.

text.NRSV: Command Aaron and his sons, saying: This is the ritual of the burnt offering. The burnt offering itself shall remain on the hearth upon the altar all night until the morning, while the fire on the altar shall be kept burning.
text.Rotherham: Command Aaron and his sons, saying, This is the law of the ascending-sacrifice,—the same, is the ascending-sacrifice, which is upon the hearth, upon the altar, all the night until the morning,—and the fire of the altar, shall be kept burning therein.
text.KJV: 6:9 Command Aaron and his sonnes, saying, This is the law of the burnt offring: (It is the burnt offring, because of the burning vpon the Altar all night vnto the morning, and the fire of the altar shall be burning in it.)
text.NABRE: 6:9 The rest of it Aaron and his sons may eat; but it must be eaten unleavened in a sacred place: in the court of the tent of meeting they shall eat it.

text.NRSV: The priest shall put on his linen vestments after putting on his linen undergarments next to his body; and he shall take up the ashes to which the fire has reduced the burnt offering on the altar, and place them beside the altar.
text.Rotherham: So then the priest shall put on his upper garment of linen, and, breeches of linen, shall he put on over his flesh, then shall he take up the fat-ashes, whereto the fire consumeth the ascending-sacrifice on the altar,—and shall put them beside the altar.
text.KJV: 6:10 And the Priest shal put on his linnen garment, & his linnen breeches shal he put vpon his flesh, and take vp the ashes which the fire hath consumed with the burnt offering on the Altar, and he shall put them besides the Altar.
text.NABRE: 6:10 It shall not be baked with leaven. I have given it to them as their portion from the oblations for the LORD; it is most holy, like the purification offering and the reparation offering.

text.NRSV: Then he shall take off his vestments and put on other garments, and carry the ashes out to a clean place outside the camp.
text.Rotherham: Then shall he put off his garments, and put on other garments,—and shall carry forth the fat-ashes unto the outside of the camp, unto a clean place,
text.KJV: 6:11 And he shal put off his garments, and put on other garments, and carry foorth the ashes without the Campe, vnto a cleane place.
text.NABRE: 6:11 Every male of Aaron’s descendants may eat of it perpetually throughout your generations as their rightful due from the oblations for the LORD. Whatever touches the oblations becomes holy. High Priest’s Daily Grain Offering.

text.NRSV: The fire on the altar shall be kept burning; it shall not go out. Every morning the priest shall add wood to it, lay out the burnt offering upon it, and turn into smoke the fat pieces of the offerings of well-being.
text.Rotherham: And, the fire on the altar, shall be kept burning therein, it shall not be suffered to go out, but the priest shall kindle up thereon pieces of wood, morning by morning,—and shall set in order thereupon the ascending-sacrifice, and shall make a perfume thereon with the fat-pieces of the peace-offerings:
text.KJV: 6:12 And the fire vpon the Altar shall be burning in it: it shall not be put out; And the Priest shall burne wood on it euery morning, and lay the burnt offering in order vpon it, and he shall burne thereon the fatte of the peace offerings.
text.NABRE: 6:12 The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: A perpetual fire shall be kept burning on the altar; it shall not go out.
text.Rotherham: fire, shall continually be kept burning on the altar, it shall not be suffered to go out.
text.KJV: 6:13 The fire shall euer be burning vpon the Altar: it shall neuer goe out.
text.NABRE: 6:13 This is the offering that Aaron and his sons shall present to the LORD on the day he is anointed: one tenth of an ephah of bran flour for the regular grain offering, half of it in the morning and half of it in the evening.

text.NRSV: This is the ritual of the grain offering: The sons of Aaron shall offer it before the Lord, in front of the altar.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the law of the meal-offering,—the sons of Aaron shall bring it near before Yahweh, unto the front of the altar.
text.KJV: 6:14 ¶ And this is the law of the meat offering: the sonnes of Aaron shall offer it before the Lord, before the Altar.
text.NABRE: 6:14 You shall bring it well kneaded and fried in oil on a griddle. Having broken the offering into pieces, you shall present it as a sweet aroma to the LORD.

text.NRSV: You shall take from it a handful of the choice flour of the grain offering, with its oil and all the frankincense that is on the offering, and turn this token portion into smoke on the altar as a pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then shall one lift up therefrom a handful of the fine meal of the meal-offering, and of the oil thereof, and all the frankincense which is upon the meal-offering,—and shall make a perfume at the altar, an altar-flame of a satisfying odour, shall the memorial thereof be, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 6:15 And he shall take of it his handfull, of the flowre of the meat offering, and of the oyle therof, and all the frankincense which is vpon the meat offring, and shall burne it vpon the Altar, for a sweet sauour, euen the memoriall of it vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 6:15 The anointed priest descended from Aaron who succeeds him shall do likewise. This is the LORD’s due forever. The offering shall be wholly burned.

text.NRSV: Aaron and his sons shall eat what is left of it; it shall be eaten as unleavened cakes in a holy place; they shall eat it in the court of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: And the remainder thereof, shall Aaron and his sons eat,—as unleavened cakes, shall it be eaten, in a holy place, within the court of the tent of meeting, shall they eat it.
text.KJV: 6:16 And the remainder thereof shall Aaron and his sonnes eat: with vnleauened bread shall it be eaten in the holy place: in the court of the Tabernacle of the Congregation they shall eat it.
text.NABRE: 6:16 Every grain offering of a priest shall be a whole offering; it may not be eaten. Purification Offerings.

text.NRSV: It shall not be baked with leaven. I have given it as their portion of my offerings by fire; it is most holy, like the sin offering and the guilt offering.
text.Rotherham: It shall not be baked into anything leavened, as their portion, have I given it, from among the altar-flames of Yahweh,—most holy, it is, like the sin-bearer, and like the guilt-bearer,
text.KJV: 6:17 It shall not be baken with leauen: I haue giuen it vnto them for their portion of my offerings made by fire: it is most holy, as is the sin offering, and as the trespasse offering.
text.NABRE: 6:17 The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Every male among the descendants of Aaron shall eat of it, as their due for all time throughout your generations, from the Lord’s offerings by fire; anything that touches them shall become holy.
text.Rotherham: Any male among the sons of Aaron may eat it, an age-abiding statute to your generations from among the altar-flames of Yahweh,—every one that toucheth them shall be hallowed.
text.KJV: 6:18 All the males among the children of Aaron shall eat of it: It shalbe a statute for euer in your generations concerning the offerings of the Lord made by fire: euery one that toucheth them shalbe holy.
text.NABRE: 6:18 Tell Aaron and his sons: This is the ritual for the purification offering. At the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered, there also, before the LORD, shall the purification offering be slaughtered. It is most holy.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying—
text.KJV: 6:19 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 6:19 The priest who offers the purification offering shall eat of it; it shall be eaten in a sacred place, in the court of the tent of meeting.

text.NRSV: This is the offering that Aaron and his sons shall offer to the Lord on the day when he is anointed: one-tenth of an ephah of choice flour as a regular grain offering, half of it in the morning and half in the evening.
text.Rotherham: This, is the oblation of Aaron and his sons, which they shall bring near unto Yahweh in the day when he is anointed, The tenth of an ephah of fine meal, as a continual meal-offering,—half thereof in the morning, and half thereof in the evening;
text.KJV: 6:20 This is the offering of Aaron, and of his sonnes which they shall offer vnto the Lord, in the day when he is anoynted: The tenth part of an Ephah of fine flowre for a meat offering perpetuall, halfe of it in the morning, and halfe thereof at night.
text.NABRE: 6:20 Whatever touches its flesh becomes holy. If any of its blood spatters on a garment, the stained part must be washed in a sacred place.

text.NRSV: It shall be made with oil on a griddle; you shall bring it well soaked, as a grain offering of baked pieces, and you shall present it as a pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: on a pan, with oil, shall it be made when well mingled, shalt thou bring it in,—in baked portions, as a meal-offering in pieces, shalt thou bring it near as a satisfying odour unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 6:21 In a panne it shalbe made with oyle, and when it is baken, thou shalt bring it in: and the baken pieces of the meat offering shalt thou offer for a sweet sauour vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 6:21 A clay vessel in which it has been boiled shall be broken; if it is boiled in a copper vessel, this shall be scoured afterward and rinsed with water.

text.NRSV: And so the priest, anointed from among Aaron’s descendants as a successor, shall prepare it; it is the Lord’s—a perpetual due—to be turned entirely into smoke.
text.Rotherham: And the priest that is anointed in his stead from among his sons, shall prepare it,—[it is] an age-abiding statute, that unto Yahweh, shall a perfume, of the entire gift, be made:
text.KJV: 6:22 And the Priest of his sonnes that is anoynted in his stead, shal offer it: It is a statute for euer vnto the Lord, it shalbe wholly burnt.
text.NABRE: 6:22 Every male of the priestly line may eat it. It is most holy.

text.NRSV: Every grain offering of a priest shall be wholly burned; it shall not be eaten.
text.Rotherham: yea, every meal-offering of a priest, shall be, entire—it shall not be eaten.
text.KJV: 6:23 For euery meat offering for the Priest shal be wholly burnt: it shall not be eaten.
text.NABRE: 6:23 But no purification offering of which some blood has been brought into the tent of meeting to make atonement in the sanctuary shall be eaten; it must be burned with fire.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying—
text.KJV: 6:24 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,

text.NRSV: Speak to Aaron and his sons, saying: This is the ritual of the sin offering. The sin offering shall be slaughtered before the Lord at the spot where the burnt offering is slaughtered; it is most holy.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron and unto his sons, saying, This, is the law of the sin-bearer. In the place where the ascending-sacrifice is slain, shall the sin-bearer be slain, before Yahweh, most holy, it is.
text.KJV: 6:25 Speake vnto Aaron and to his sonnes, saying, This is the law of the sinne offering: In the place where the burnt offering is killed, shall the sinne offering be killed before the Lord: it is most holy.

text.NRSV: The priest who offers it as a sin offering shall eat of it; it shall be eaten in a holy place, in the court of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: The priest who maketh it a sin-bearer, shall eat it,—in a holy place, shall it be eaten, in the court of the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: 6:26 The Priest that offereth it for sinne, shall eat it: In the holy place shal it be eaten, in the court of the Tabernacle of the Congregation.

text.NRSV: Whatever touches its flesh shall become holy; and when any of its blood is spattered on a garment, you shall wash the bespattered part in a holy place.
text.Rotherham: Every one who toucheth the flesh thereof, shall be hallowed; and when one sprinkleth some of the blood thereof upon a garment, that whereon it was sprinkled, shalt thou wash in a holy place.
text.KJV: 6:27 Whatsoeuer shall touch the flesh thereof, shalbe holy: and when there is sprinckled of the blood thereof vpon any garment, thou shalt wash that whereon it was sprinckled, in the holy place.

text.NRSV: An earthen vessel in which it is boiled shall be broken; but if it is boiled in a bronze vessel, that shall be scoured and rinsed in water.
text.Rotherham: But, the earthen vessel wherein it is boiled, shall be broken,—or, if, in a vessel of bronze, it hath been boiled, then shall the vessel be scoured and rinsed in water.
text.KJV: 6:28 But the earthen vessell wherein it is sodden, shall be broken: And if it be sodden in a brasen pot, it shall be both scowred, and rinsed in water.

text.NRSV: Every male among the priests shall eat of it; it is most holy.
text.Rotherham: Any male among the priests may eat thereof,—most holy, it is.
text.KJV: 6:29 All the males among the Priests shall eate thereof: it is most holy.

text.NRSV: But no sin offering shall be eaten from which any blood is brought into the tent of meeting for making atonement in the holy place; it shall be burned with fire.
text.Rotherham: But, no sin-bearer whereof any of the blood is taken into the tent of meeting to make a propitiatory-covering in the sanctuary, shall be eaten,—with fire, shall it be consumed.
text.KJV: 6:30 And no sinne offering whereof any of the blood is brought into the Tabernacle of the Congregation to reconcile withall in the holy place, shall be eaten: it shall be burnt in the fire.

text.NRSV: This is the ritual of the guilt offering. It is most holy;
text.Rotherham: And this, is the law of the guilt-bearer,—most holy, it is!
text.KJV: 7:1 Likewise this is the lawe of the trespasse offering: it is most holy.
text.NABRE: 7:1 Chapter 7 - Reparation Offerings. This is the ritual for the reparation offering. It is most holy.

text.NRSV: at the spot where the burnt offering is slaughtered they shall slaughter the guilt offering, and its blood shall be dashed against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: In the place where they slay the ascending-sacrifice, shall they slay the guilt-bearer; and, the blood thereof, shall one dash against the altar, round about;
text.KJV: 7:2 In the place where they kil the burnt offring, shall they kil the trespasse offering; and the blood thereof shall hee sprinckle round about vpon the Altar.
text.NABRE: 7:2 At the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered, the reparation offering shall also be slaughtered. Its blood shall be splashed on all the sides of the altar.

text.NRSV: All its fat shall be offered: the broad tail, the fat that covers the entrails,
text.Rotherham: and, as for all the fat thereof, one shall bring near therefrom,—the fat-tail, and the fat that covereth the inwards;
text.KJV: 7:3 And he shall offer of it, all the fat thereof; the rumpe, and the fat that couereth the inwards,
text.NABRE: 7:3 All of its fat shall be offered: the fatty tail, the fat that covers the inner organs, and all the fat that adheres to them,

text.NRSV: the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins, and the appendage of the liver, which shall be removed with the kidneys.
text.Rotherham: and the two kidneys, with the fat that is upon them, which is upon the loins,—and the caul upon the liver, up to the kidneys, shall he remove it.
text.KJV: 7:4 And the two kidneys, and the fat that is on them, which is by the flankes, and the caule that is aboue the liuer, with the kidneys, it shall he take away.
text.NABRE: 7:4 as well as the two kidneys with the fat on them near the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which is removed with the kidneys.

text.NRSV: The priest shall turn them into smoke on the altar as an offering by fire to the Lord; it is a guilt offering.
text.Rotherham: And the priest shall make a perfume with them at the altar, an altar-flame unto Yahweh—a guilt-bearer, it is,
text.KJV: 7:5 And the Priest shall burne them vpon the Altar, for an offering made by fire vnto the Lord: it is a trespasse offering.
text.NABRE: 7:5 The priest shall burn these on the altar as an oblation to the LORD. It is a reparation offering.

text.NRSV: Every male among the priests shall eat of it; it shall be eaten in a holy place; it is most holy.
text.Rotherham: Any male among the priests, may eat thereof,—in a holy place, shall it be eaten, most holy, it is.
text.KJV: 7:6 Euery male among the Priestes shall eate thereof: it shall be eaten in the holy place: it is most holy.
text.NABRE: 7:6 Every male of the priestly line may eat of it; but it must be eaten in a sacred place. It is most holy.

text.NRSV: The guilt offering is like the sin offering, there is the same ritual for them; the priest who makes atonement with it shall have it.
text.Rotherham: As the sin-bearer, so the guilt-bearer,—one law, for them,—the priest who maketh a propitiatory-covering, therewith,—to him, shall it belong,
text.KJV: 7:7 As the sinne offering is, so is the trespasse offering: there is one law for them: the Priest that maketh atonement therewith, shall haue it.
text.NABRE: 7:7 Because the purification offering and the reparation offering are alike, both have the same ritual. The reparation offering belongs to the priest who makes atonement with it.

text.NRSV: So, too, the priest who offers anyone’s burnt offering shall keep the skin of the burnt offering that he offered.
text.Rotherham: And, as for the priest who bringeth near the ascending-sacrifice of any man, the skin of the ascending-sacrifice which he hath brought near, to the priest himself, shall it belong.
text.KJV: 7:8 And the Priest that offereth any mans burnt offering, euen the Priest shall haue to himselfe the skinne of the burnt offering which he hath offered.
text.NABRE: 7:8 As for the priest who offers someone’s burnt offering, to him belongs the hide of the burnt offering that is offered.

text.NRSV: And every grain offering baked in the oven, and all that is prepared in a pan or on a griddle, shall belong to the priest who offers it.
text.Rotherham: And as for any meal-offering that may be baked in an oven, or anything that hath been prepared in a boiler or on a girdle, to the priest that bringeth it near—to him, shall it belong,
text.KJV: 7:9 And all the meate offering that is baken in the ouen, and all that is dressed in the frying panne, and in the panne, shall be the Priests that offereth it.
text.NABRE: 7:9 Also, every grain offering that is baked in an oven or made in a pan or on a griddle shall belong to the priest who offers it,

text.NRSV: But every other grain offering, mixed with oil or dry, shall belong to all the sons of Aaron equally.
text.Rotherham: And, as for any meal-offering overflowed with oil or dry,—to all the sons of Aaron, shall it belong, to one as much as to another.
text.KJV: 7:10 And euery meate offering mingled with oyle, and drie, shall all the sonnes of Aaron haue, one as much as another.
text.NABRE: 7:10 whereas all grain offerings that are mixed with oil or are dry shall belong to all of Aaron’s sons without distinction. Communion Sacrifices.

text.NRSV: This is the ritual of the sacrifice of well-being that one may offer to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the law of the peace-offering which one may bring near unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 7:11 And this is the law of the sacrifice of peace offerings, which he shall offer vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 7:11 This is the ritual for the communion sacrifice that is offered to the LORD.

text.NRSV: If you offer it for thanksgiving, you shall offer with the thank offering unleavened cakes mixed with oil, unleavened wafers spread with oil, and cakes of choice flour well soaked in oil.
text.Rotherham: If, for thanksgiving, he bring it near, then shall he bring near, with the thanksgiving sacrifice perforated cakes unleavened, overflowed with oil, and wafers unleavened anointed with oil,—and of fine flour moistened, perforated cakes overflowed with oil.
text.KJV: 7:12 If hee offer it for a thankesgiuing, then he shall offer with the sacrifice of thankesgiuing vnleauened cakes mingled with oyle, and vnleauened wafers anointed with oile, and cakes mingled with oyle of fine flowre fried.
text.NABRE: 7:12 If someone offers it for thanksgiving, that person shall offer it with unleavened cakes mixed with oil, unleavened wafers spread with oil, and cakes made of bran flour mixed with oil and well kneaded.

text.NRSV: With your thanksgiving sacrifice of well-being you shall bring your offering with cakes of leavened bread.
text.Rotherham: With perforated cakes of leavened bread, shall he bring near his oblation, with his peace-offering of thanksgiving,
text.KJV: 7:13 Besides the cakes, hee shall offer for his offring leauened bread, with the sacrifice of thankesgiuing of his peace offerings.
text.NABRE: 7:13 One shall present this offering together with loaves of leavened bread along with the thanksgiving communion sacrifice.

text.NRSV: From this you shall offer one cake from each offering, as a gift to the Lord; it shall belong to the priest who dashes the blood of the offering of well-being.
text.Rotherham: Then shall he bring near therefrom one of each oblation, a heave-offering unto Yahweh,—to the priest who dasheth [against the altar] the blood of the peace-offering—to him, shall it belong.
text.KJV: 7:14 And of it he shall offer one out of the whole oblation, for an heaue offering vnto the Lord, and it shall bee the Priests that sprinkleth the blood of the peace offerings.
text.NABRE: 7:14 From this the individual shall offer one bread of each type of offering as a contribution to the LORD; this shall belong to the priest who splashes the blood of the communion offering.

text.NRSV: And the flesh of your thanksgiving sacrifice of well-being shall be eaten on the day it is offered; you shall not leave any of it until morning.
text.Rotherham: But, as for the flesh of his peace-offering of thanksgiving, on the day of bringing it near, shall it be eaten,—he shall not leave thereof until morning,
text.KJV: 7:15 And the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offerings for thankesgiuing, shall be eaten the same day that it is offered: he shall not leaue any of it vntill the morning.
text.NABRE: 7:15 The meat of the thanksgiving communion sacrifice shall be eaten on the day it is offered; none of it may be kept till the next morning.

text.NRSV: But if the sacrifice you offer is a votive offering or a freewill offering, it shall be eaten on the day that you offer your sacrifice, and what is left of it shall be eaten the next day;
text.Rotherham: But, if, a vow or a freewill offering, be his sacrifice of oblation, in the day he bringeth near his sacrifice, shall it be eaten, and on the morrow, then may the remainder thereof, be eaten;
text.KJV: 7:16 But if the sacrifice of his offering be a vow, or a voluntary offering, it shall be eaten the same day that he offereth his sacrifice: and on the morrowe also the remainder of it shall be eaten.
text.NABRE: 7:16 However, if the sacrifice offered is a votive or a voluntary offering, it shall be eaten on the day the sacrifice is offered, and on the next day what is left over may be eaten.

text.NRSV: but what is left of the flesh of the sacrifice shall be burned up on the third day.
text.Rotherham: but as for the remainder of the flesh of the sacrifice, on the third day—in fire, shall it be consumed.
text.KJV: 7:17 But the remainder of the flesh of the sacrifice on the third day shall bee burnt with fire.
text.NABRE: 7:17 But what is left over of the meat of the sacrifice on the third day must be burned in the fire.

text.NRSV: If any of the flesh of your sacrifice of well-being is eaten on the third day, it shall not be acceptable, nor shall it be credited to the one who offers it; it shall be an abomination, and the one who eats of it shall incur guilt.
text.Rotherham: And, if any of the flesh of his peace-offering, be at all eaten, on the third day, he that brought it near [shall not be accepted] it shall not be reckoned to him, unclean, shall it be,—and the person who shall eat thereof, shall bear his iniquity.
text.KJV: 7:18 And if any of the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offerings be eaten at all on the third day, it shall not be accepted, neither shal it be imputed vnto him that offereth it: it shall be an abomination, and the soule that eateth of it, shall beare his iniquitie.
text.NABRE: 7:18 If indeed any of the flesh of the communion sacrifice is eaten on the third day, it shall not be accepted; it will not be reckoned to the credit of the one offering it. Rather it becomes a desecrated meat. Anyone who eats of it shall bear the penalty.

text.NRSV: Flesh that touches any unclean thing shall not be eaten; it shall be burned. As for other flesh, all who are clean may eat such flesh.
text.Rotherham: And, the flesh that toucheth anything unclean, shall not be eaten, in fire, shall it be consumed,—but, as for the [other] flesh, any one who is clean, may eat flesh.
text.KJV: 7:19 And the flesh that toucheth any vncleane thing, shal not be eaten: it shal be burnt with fire, and as for the flesh, all that be cleane shall eate thereof.
text.NABRE: 7:19 Should the meat touch anything unclean, it may not be eaten, but shall be burned in the fire. As for other meat, all who are clean may eat of it.

text.NRSV: But those who eat flesh from the Lord’s sacrifice of well-being while in a state of uncleanness shall be cut off from their kin.
text.Rotherham: But as for the person who shall eat flesh from the peace-offering, which pertaineth unto Yahweh, while his uncleanness is on him, then shall that person be cut off from among his kinsfolk.
text.KJV: 7:20 But the soule that eateth of the flesh of the sacrifice of peace offerings, that pertaine vnto the Lord, hauing his vncleanesse vpon him, euen that soule shall be cut off from his people.
text.NABRE: 7:20 If, however, someone in a state of uncleanness eats the meat of a communion sacrifice belonging to the LORD, that person shall be cut off from the people.

text.NRSV: When any one of you touches any unclean thing—human uncleanness or an unclean animal or any unclean creature—and then eats flesh from the Lord’s sacrifice of well-being, you shall be cut off from your kin.
text.Rotherham: Yea, as for, any person, whensoever he shall touch anything unclean—the uncleanness of man or an unclean beast, or any unclean disgusting thing,—and then eateth of the flesh of a peace-offering that pertaineth unto Yahweh, then shall that person be cut off from among his kinsfolk.
text.KJV: 7:21 Moreouer, the soule that shall touch any vncleane thing, as the vncleannesse of man, or any vncleane beast, or any abominable vncleane thing, and eate of the flesh of the sacrifice of peace offerings which pertaine vnto the Lord, euen that soule shall be cut off from his people.
text.NABRE: 7:21 Likewise, if someone touches anything unclean, whether it be human uncleanness or an unclean animal or an unclean loathsome creature, and then eats the meat of the communion sacrifice belonging to the LORD, that person, too, shall be cut off from the people.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying—
text.KJV: 7:22 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 7:22 Prohibition Against Blood and Fat. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: You shall eat no fat of ox or sheep or goat.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, saying,—No fat, of ox or sheep or goat, shall ye eat:
text.KJV: 7:23 Speake vnto the children of Israel, saying, Ye shall eat no maner fat of oxe, or of sheepe, or of goat.
text.NABRE: 7:23 Tell the Israelites: You shall not eat the fat of any ox or sheep or goat.

text.NRSV: The fat of an animal that died or was mangled by wild animals may be put to any use, but you must not eat it.
text.Rotherham: and though the fat of what dieth of itself and the fat of that which has been torn in pieces, may be used for any other service, yet shall ye, in nowise eat it.
text.KJV: 7:24 And the fat of the beast that dieth of it selfe, and the fat of that which is torne with beasts, may be vsed in any other vse: but yee shall in no wise eate of it.
text.NABRE: 7:24 Although the fat of an animal that has died a natural death or has been killed by wild beasts may be put to any other use, you may not eat it.

text.NRSV: If any one of you eats the fat from an animal of which an offering by fire may be made to the Lord, you who eat it shall be cut off from your kin.
text.Rotherham: Nay, as touching any one who eateth the fat of any beast, wherefrom he might bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh, then shall the person that eateth be cut off from among his kinsfolk.
text.KJV: 7:25 For whosoeuer eateth the fat of the beast, of which men offer an offring made by fire vnto the Lord, euen the soule that eateth it, shall be cut off from his people.
text.NABRE: 7:25 If anyone eats the fat of an animal from which an oblation is made to the LORD, that person shall be cut off from the people.

text.NRSV: You must not eat any blood whatever, either of bird or of animal, in any of your settlements.
text.Rotherham: And no manner of blood, shall ye eat, in any of your dwellings,—whether of bird or of beast:
text.KJV: 7:26 Moreouer ye shall eat no maner of blood, whether it bee of foule or of beast in any of your dwellings.
text.NABRE: 7:26 Wherever you dwell, you shall not eat any blood, whether of bird or of animal.

text.NRSV: Any one of you who eats any blood shall be cut off from your kin.
text.Rotherham: as touching any person who eateth any manner of blood, then shall that person be cut off from among his kinsfolk.
text.KJV: 7:27 Whatsoeuer soule it be that eateth any maner of blood, euen that soule shalbe cut off from his people.
text.NABRE: 7:27 Every person who eats any blood shall be cut off from the people.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying,
text.KJV: 7:28 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 7:28 Portions from the Communion Sacrifice for Priests. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: Any who offer a sacrifice of well-being to the Lord shall bring the offering to the Lord from the sacrifice of well-being.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel saying,—He that offereth his peace-offering unto Yahweh, shall bring in his own oblation unto Yahweh out of his peace-offering:
text.KJV: 7:29 Speake vnto the children of Israel, saying, Hee that offereth the sacrifice of his peace offerings vnto the Lord, shall bring his oblation vnto the Lord, of the sacrifice of his peaceofferings.
text.NABRE: 7:29 Tell the Israelites: The person who offers a communion sacrifice to the LORD shall be the one to bring from it the offering to the LORD.

text.NRSV: The worshiper’s own hands shall bring the Lord’s offering by fire. You shall bring the fat with the breast, so that the breast may be raised as an elevation offering before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: his own hands shall bring in the altar-flames of Yahweh,—the fat with the breast, he shall bring it in, the breast, to wave it as a wave-offering before Yahweh.
text.KJV: 7:30 His owne hands shall bring the offerings of the Lord made by fire, the fat with the brest, it shall hee bring, that the brest may be waued for a waue offering before the Lord.
text.NABRE: 7:30 The offerer’s own hands shall carry the oblations for the LORD: the person shall bring the fat together with the brisket, which is to be raised as an elevated offering before the LORD.

text.NRSV: The priest shall turn the fat into smoke on the altar, but the breast shall belong to Aaron and his sons.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest make a perfume with the fat at the altar,—and the breast shall be for Aaron and for his sons.
text.KJV: 7:31 And the Priest shall burne the fat vpon the Altar: but the brest shalbe Aarons and his sonnes.
text.NABRE: 7:31 The priest shall burn the fat on the altar, but the brisket belongs to Aaron and his sons.

text.NRSV: And the right thigh from your sacrifices of well-being you shall give to the priest as an offering;
text.Rotherham: But, the right shoulder, shall ye give as a heave-offering, unto the priest,—from among your peace-offerings
text.KJV: 7:32 And the right shoulder shall ye giue vnto the Priest for an heaue offering of the sacrifices of your peace offerings.
text.NABRE: 7:32 Moreover, from your communion sacrifices you shall give to the priest the right leg as a contribution.

text.NRSV: the one among the sons of Aaron who offers the blood and fat of the offering of well-being shall have the right thigh for a portion.
text.Rotherham: he that bringeth near the blood of the peace-offerings and the fat from among the sons of Aaron, to him, shall belong the right shoulder for a portion.
text.KJV: 7:33 Hee among the sonnes of Aaron that offereth the blood of the peace offerings, and the fat, shall haue the right shoulder for his part.
text.NABRE: 7:33 The one among Aaron’s sons who offers the blood and the fat of the communion offering shall have the right leg as his portion,

text.NRSV: For I have taken the breast of the elevation offering, and the thigh that is offered, from the people of Israel, from their sacrifices of well-being, and have given them to Aaron the priest and to his sons, as a perpetual due from the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: For, the wave-breast and the heave-shoulder, have I taken of the sons of Israel, out of their peace-offerings,—and have given them unto Aaron the priest and unto his sons as an age-abiding statute, from the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 7:34 For the waue brest and the heaue shoulder haue I taken of the children of Israel, from off the sacrifices of their peace offerings, and haue giuen them vnto Aaron the Priest, and vnto his sonnes, by a statute for euer, from among the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: 7:34 for from the communion sacrifices of the Israelites I have taken the brisket that is elevated and the leg that is a contribution, and I have given them to Aaron, the priest, and to his sons as their due from the Israelites forever.

text.NRSV: This is the portion allotted to Aaron and to his sons from the offerings made by fire to the Lord, once they have been brought forward to serve as priests to the Lord;
text.Rotherham: This, is what pertaineth to the anointing of Aaron and to the anointing of his sons out of the altar-flames of Yahweh,—[granted] in the day when he brought them near, to minister as priests unto Yahweh:
text.KJV: 7:35 ¶ This is the portion of the anointing of Aaron, and of the anointing of his sonnes, out of the offerings of the Lord made by fire, in the day when he presented them, to minister vnto the Lord in the Priests office:
text.NABRE: 7:35 This is the priestly share from the oblations for the LORD, allotted to Aaron and his sons on the day they were brought forth to be the priests of the LORD,

text.NRSV: these the Lord commanded to be given them, when he anointed them, as a perpetual due from the people of Israel throughout their generations.
text.Rotherham: which Yahweh commanded to give them in the day when he anointed them, from among the sons of Israel,—an age-abiding statute to their generations.
text.KJV: 7:36 Which the Lord commanded to be giuen them of the children of Israel, in the day that hee anointed them, by a statute for euer, throughout their generations.
text.NABRE: 7:36 which the LORD ordered to be given them from the Israelites on the day they were anointed, as their due throughout their generations forever.

text.NRSV: This is the ritual of the burnt offering, the grain offering, the sin offering, the guilt offering, the offering of ordination, and the sacrifice of well-being,
text.Rotherham: This is the law—for the ascending-sacrifice, for the meal-offering, and for the sin-bearer, and for the guilt-bearer,—and for the installation-offerings, and for the peace-offering:
text.KJV: 7:37 This is the law of the burnt offering, of the meate offering, and of the sinne offering, and of the trespasse offering, and of the consecrations, and of the sacrifice of the peace offerings:
text.NABRE: 7:37 Summary. This is the ritual for the burnt offering, the grain offering, the purification offering, the reparation offering, the ordination offering, and the communion sacrifice,

text.NRSV: which the Lord commanded Moses on Mount Sinai, when he commanded the people of Israel to bring their offerings to the Lord, in the wilderness of Sinai.
text.Rotherham: which Yahweh commanded Moses in Mount Sinai,—in the day when he commanded the sons of Israel to bring near their oblations unto Yahweh in the desert of Sinai.
text.KJV: 7:38 Which the Lord commanded Moses in mount Sinai, in the day that he commanded the children of Israel to offer their oblations vnto the Lord, in the wildernesse of Sinai.
text.NABRE: 7:38 which the LORD enjoined on Moses at Mount Sinai at the time when he commanded the Israelites in the wilderness of Sinai to bring their offerings to the LORD.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 8:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 8:1 II. Ceremony of Ordination Chapter 8 - Ordination of Aaron and His Sons. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Take Aaron and his sons with him, and the vestments, and the anointing oil, and the bull of sin offering, and the two rams, and the basket of unleavened bread;
text.Rotherham: Take thou Aaron and his sons with him, and the garments, and the anointing oil,—and the sin-bearing bullock, and the two rams, and the basket of unleavened cakes;
text.KJV: 8:2 Take Aaron and his sonnes with him, and the garments, and the anointing oyle, and a bullocke for the sinne offering, and two rammes, and a basket of vnleauened bread.
text.NABRE: 8:2 Take Aaron along with his sons, the vestments, the anointing oil, the bull for a purification offering, the two rams, and the basket of unleavened bread,

text.NRSV: and assemble the whole congregation at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: and all the assembly, call thou together,—unto the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: 8:3 And gather thou all the Congregation together vnto the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation.
text.NABRE: 8:3 then assemble the whole community at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

text.NRSV: And Moses did as the Lord commanded him. When the congregation was assembled at the entrance of the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: And Moses did, as Yahweh commanded him,—and the assembly was called together, unto the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: 8:4 And Moses did as the Lord commanded him, & the assembly was gathered together vnto the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation.
text.NABRE: 8:4 Moses did as the LORD had commanded. When the community had assembled at the entrance of the tent of meeting,

text.NRSV: Moses said to the congregation, “This is what the Lord has commanded to be done.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses unto the assembly,—This is the thing, which Yahweh hath commanded to be done.
text.KJV: 8:5 And Moses saide vnto the Congregation, This is the thing which the Lord commanded to be done.
text.NABRE: 8:5 Moses told them: “This is what the LORD has ordered to be done.”

text.NRSV: Then Moses brought Aaron and his sons forward, and washed them with water.
text.Rotherham: So Moses brought near Aaron and his sons,—and bathed them with water,
text.KJV: 8:6 And Moses brought Aaron and his sonnes, and washed them with water.
text.NABRE: 8:6 Bringing forward Aaron and his sons, Moses first washed them with water.

text.NRSV: He put the tunic on him, fastened the sash around him, clothed him with the robe, and put the ephod on him; he then put the decorated band of the ephod around him, tying the ephod to him with it.
text.Rotherham: and put upon him the tunic and girded him with the band, and clothed him with the robe, and put upon him the ephod, and girded him with the curiously-woven band of the ephod, and bound it to him therewith;
text.KJV: 8:7 And he put vpon him the coate, and girded him with the girdle, and clothed him with the robe, and put the Ephod vpon him, and he girded him with the curious girdle of the Ephod, and bound it vnto him therewith.
text.NABRE: 8:7 Then he put the tunic on Aaron, girded him with the sash, clothed him with the robe, placed the ephod on him, and girded him with the ephod’s embroidered belt, fastening the ephod on him with it.

text.NRSV: He placed the breastpiece on him, and in the breastpiece he put the Urim and the Thummim.
text.Rotherham: and he put upon him the breastpiece,—and placed, in the breastpiece, the Lights and the Perfections;
text.KJV: 8:8 And hee put the brest plate vpon him: also he put in the brest plate the Urim and the Thummim.
text.NABRE: 8:8 He then set the breastpiece on him, putting the Urim and Thummim in it.

text.NRSV: And he set the turban on his head, and on the turban, in front, he set the golden ornament, the holy diadem, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: and he put the turban upon his head,—and put upon the turban, on the forefront thereof the burnished plate of gold—the holy crown, As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: 8:9 And he put the miter vpon his head; also vpon the miter, euen vpon his forefront did hee put the golden plate, the holy crowne, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: 8:9 He put the turban on his head, attaching the gold medallion, the sacred headband, on the front of the turban, as the LORD had commanded Moses to do.

text.NRSV: Then Moses took the anointing oil and anointed the tabernacle and all that was in it, and consecrated them.
text.Rotherham: And Moses took the anointing oil, and anointed the habitation and all that was therein,—and hallowed them;
text.KJV: 8:10 And Moses tooke the anointing oile, and anointed the tabernacle and all that was therein, and sanctified them.
text.NABRE: 8:10 Taking the anointing oil, Moses anointed and consecrated the tabernacle and all that was in it.

text.NRSV: He sprinkled some of it on the altar seven times, and anointed the altar and all its utensils, and the basin and its base, to consecrate them.
text.Rotherham: and he sprinkled thereof upon the altar seven times,—and anointed the altar, and all the utensils thereof and the laver and its stand to hallow them
text.KJV: 8:11 And he sprinkled thereof vpon the altar seuen times, and anointed the altar and all his vessels, both the lauer and his foot, to sanctifie them.
text.NABRE: 8:11 Then he sprinkled some of the oil seven times on the altar, and anointed the altar, with all its utensils, and the laver, with its base, to consecrate them.

text.NRSV: He poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron’s head and anointed him, to consecrate him.
text.Rotherham: and he poured of the anointing oil, upon the head of Aaron,—and anointed him, to hallow him.
text.KJV: 8:12 And he powred of the anointing oile vpon Aarons head, and anointed him, to sanctifie him.
text.NABRE: 8:12 He also poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron’s head and anointed him, to consecrate him.

text.NRSV: And Moses brought forward Aaron’s sons, and clothed them with tunics, and fastened sashes around them, and tied headdresses on them, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And Moses brought near the sons of Aaron and clothed them with tunics and girded them with bands, and wrapped round for them turbans,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: 8:13 And Moses brought Aarons sonnes, and put coats vpon them, and girded them with girdles, and put bonnets vpon them, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: 8:13 Moses likewise brought forward Aaron’s sons, clothed them with tunics, girded them with sashes, and put skullcaps on them, as the LORD had commanded him to do.

text.NRSV: He led forward the bull of sin offering; and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the bull of sin offering,
text.Rotherham: Then was led near the sin-bearing bullock,—and Aaron and his sons leaned their hands upon the head of the sin-bearing bullock,
text.KJV: 8:14 And he brought the bullocke for the sinne offering, and Aaron and his sonnes laid their hands vpon the head of the bullocke for the sinne offering.
text.NABRE: 8:14 Ordination Sacrifices. He brought forward the bull for a purification offering, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on its head.

text.NRSV: and it was slaughtered. Moses took the blood and with his finger put some on each of the horns of the altar, purifying the altar; then he poured out the blood at the base of the altar. Thus he consecrated it, to make atonement for it.
text.Rotherham: Then was it slain, and Moses took the blood and put [thereof] upon the horns of the altar round about, with his finger, and cleansed the altar from sin,—and the [remainder of the] blood, poured he forth at the base of the altar, and hallowed it putting a propitiatory-covering thereupon.
text.KJV: 8:15 And he slew it, and Moses tooke the blood, and put it vpon the hornes of the altar round about with his finger, and purified the altar, and powred the blood at the bottome of the altar, and sanctified it, to make reconciliation vpon it.
text.NABRE: 8:15 When it was slaughtered, Moses took the blood and with his finger he put it on the horns around the altar, thus purifying the altar. He poured out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar. Thus he consecrated it so that atonement could be made on it.

text.NRSV: Moses took all the fat that was around the entrails, and the appendage of the liver, and the two kidneys with their fat, and turned them into smoke on the altar.
text.Rotherham: And he took all the fat that was on the inwards, and the caul of the liver, and the two kidneys and their fat,—and Moses made a perfume at the altar.
text.KJV: 8:16 And he tooke all the fat that was vpon the inwards, and the kall aboue the liuer, and the two kidneis, and their fat, and Moses burned it vpon the Altar.
text.NABRE: 8:16 Taking all the fat that was over the inner organs, as well as the lobe of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat, Moses burned them on the altar.

text.NRSV: But the bull itself, and its skin and flesh and its dung, he burned with fire outside the camp, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: But the bullock itself, and its skin and its flesh and its dung, consumed he with fire, outside the camp,—As Yahweh commanded Moses,
text.KJV: 8:17 But the bullocke, and his hide, his flesh and his doung, he burnt with fire without the campe, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: 8:17 The bull, however, with its hide and flesh and dung he burned in the fire outside the camp, as the LORD had commanded Moses to do.

text.NRSV: Then he presented the ram of burnt offering. Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram,
text.Rotherham: Then was brought near the ram for the ascending-sacrifice,—and Aaron and his sons leaned their hands upon the head of the ram;
text.KJV: 8:18 ¶ And he brought the ramme for the burnt offring: and Aaron and his sonnes laid their hands vpon the head of the ramme.
text.NABRE: 8:18 He next brought forward the ram of the burnt offering, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on its head.

text.NRSV: and it was slaughtered. Moses dashed the blood against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: and it was slain,—and Moses dashed the blood against the altar round about;
text.KJV: 8:19 And he killed it, and Moses sprinkled the blood vpon the Altar round about.
text.NABRE: 8:19 When it was slaughtered, Moses splashed the blood on all sides of the altar.

text.NRSV: The ram was cut into its parts, and Moses turned the head, the parts, and the suet into smoke on the altar;
text.Rotherham: and the ram itself, was cut up into its pieces,—and Moses made a perfume with the head and with the pieces and with the fat;
text.KJV: 8:20 And he cut the ramme into pieces, and Moses burnt the head, and the pieces, and the fat.
text.NABRE: 8:20 After the ram was cut up into pieces, Moses burned the head, the cut-up pieces and the suet.

text.NRSV: but the entrails and the legs he washed with water. Moses turned the whole ram into smoke on the altar; it was a burnt offering for a pleasing odor, an offering by fire to the Lord, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: but the inwards and the legs, were bathed in water,—and Moses made a perfume with the whole ram at the altar, an ascending-sacrifice, it was—for a satisfying odour an altar-flame, it was unto Yahweh, As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: 8:21 And he washed the inwards and the legges in water, and Moses burnt the whole ramme vpon the Altar: It was a burnt sacrifice for a sweet sauour, and an offering made by fire vnto the Lord, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: 8:21 After the inner organs and the shanks were washed with water, Moses burned these remaining parts of the ram on the altar. It was a burnt offering for a sweet aroma, an oblation to the LORD, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: Then he presented the second ram, the ram of ordination. Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram,
text.Rotherham: Then was brought near the second ram, the ram of installation, and Aaron and his sons leaned their hands upon the head of the ram;
text.KJV: 8:22 ¶ And hee brought the other ramme, the ramme of consecration: and Aaron and his sonnes layd their hands vpon the head of the ramme.
text.NABRE: 8:22 Then he brought forward the second ram, the ordination ram, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on its head.

text.NRSV: and it was slaughtered. Moses took some of its blood and put it on the lobe of Aaron’s right ear and on the thumb of his right hand and on the big toe of his right foot.
text.Rotherham: and it was slain, and Moses took of the blood thereof, and put upon the tip of Aaron’s right ear,—and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot.
text.KJV: 8:23 And he slew it, and Moses tooke of the blood of it, and put it vpon the tip of Aarons right eare, and vpon the thumbe of his right hand, and vpon the great toe of his right foot.
text.NABRE: 8:23 When it was slaughtered, Moses took some of its blood and put it on the lobe of Aaron’s right ear, on the thumb of his right hand, and on the big toe of his right foot.

text.NRSV: After Aaron’s sons were brought forward, Moses put some of the blood on the lobes of their right ears and on the thumbs of their right hands and on the big toes of their right feet; and Moses dashed the rest of the blood against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: Then were brought near the sons of Aaron, and Moses put of the blood upon the tip of their right ear, and upon the thumb of their right hand, and upon the great toe of their right foot,—and Moses dashed the blood against the altar round about.
text.KJV: 8:24 And he brought Aarons sonnes, and Moses put of the blood vpon the tippe of their right eare, and vpon the thumbs of their right hands, and vpon the great toes of their right feete: and Moses sprinkled the blood vpon the Altar round about.
text.NABRE: 8:24 Moses had the sons of Aaron also come forward, and he put some of the blood on the lobes of their right ears, on the thumbs of their right hands, and on the big toes of their right feet. The rest of the blood he splashed on all the sides of the altar.

text.NRSV: He took the fat—the broad tail, all the fat that was around the entrails, the appendage of the liver, and the two kidneys with their fat—and the right thigh.
text.Rotherham: And he took the fat and the fat-tail, and all the fat that was on the inwards, and the caul of the liver, and the two kidneys and their fat,—and the right shoulder;
text.KJV: 8:25 And hee tooke the fat, and the rumpe, and all the fat that was vpon the inwards, and the caule aboue the liuer, and the two kidneys and their fat, and the right shoulder.
text.NABRE: 8:25 He then took the fat: the fatty tail and all the fat over the inner organs, the lobe of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat, and likewise the right thigh;

text.NRSV: From the basket of unleavened bread that was before the Lord, he took one cake of unleavened bread, one cake of bread with oil, and one wafer, and placed them on the fat and on the right thigh.
text.Rotherham: and out of the basket of unleavened-cakes that was before Yahweh, took he one round unleavened-cake and one round oil-cake, and one wafer, and put them upon the fat portions, and upon the right shoulder;
text.KJV: 8:26 And out of the basket of vnleauened bread, that was before the Lord, he tooke one vnleauened cake, and a cake of oyled bread, and one wafer, and put them on the fat, and vpon the right shoulder.
text.NABRE: 8:26 from the basket of unleavened bread that was set before the LORD he took one unleavened cake, one loaf of bread made with oil, and one wafer; these he placed on top of the portions of fat and the right thigh.

text.NRSV: He placed all these on the palms of Aaron and on the palms of his sons, and raised them as an elevation offering before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and placed the whole upon the opened palms of Aaron, and upon the opened palms of his sons,—and waved them as a wave-offering, before Yahweh.
text.KJV: 8:27 And hee put all vpon Aarons hands, and vpon his sonnes hands, and waued them for a waue offering before the Lord.
text.NABRE: 8:27 He then put all these things upon the palms of Aaron and his sons, whom he had raise them as an elevated offering before the LORD.

text.NRSV: Then Moses took them from their hands and turned them into smoke on the altar with the burnt offering. This was an ordination offering for a pleasing odor, an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And Moses took them from off their opened palms, and made a perfume at the altar upon the ascending-sacrifice,—an installation, they were for a satisfying odour, an altar-flame, it was unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 8:28 And Moses tooke them from off their hands, and burnt them on the Altar, vpon the burnt offering: They were consecrations for a sweet sauour: It is an offering made by fire vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 8:28 When Moses had removed them from their palms, he burned them on the altar with the burnt offering. They were an ordination offering for a sweet aroma, an oblation to the LORD.

text.NRSV: Moses took the breast and elevated it as an elevation offering before the Lord; it was Moses’ portion of the ram of ordination, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And Moses took the breast, and waved it as a wave-offering, before Yahweh,—of the ram of installation, unto Moses it belonged, as a portion, As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: 8:29 And Moses tooke the brest, and waued it for a waue offering before the Lord: For of the ramme of consecration it was Moses part, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: 8:29 He then took the brisket and raised it as an elevated offering before the LORD; this was Moses’ own portion of the ordination ram, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: Then Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that was on the altar and sprinkled them on Aaron and his vestments, and also on his sons and their vestments. Thus he consecrated Aaron and his vestments, and also his sons and their vestments.
text.Rotherham: And Moses took of the anointing oil and of the blood that was upon the altar, and sprinkled upon Aaron, upon his garments, and upon his sons and upon the garments of his sons with him,—and hallowed Aaron, his garments, and his sons and the garments of his sons with him.
text.KJV: 8:30 And Moses tooke of the anoynting oyle, and of the blood which was vpon the Altar, and sprinckled it vpon Aaron, and vpon his garments, and vpon his sonnes, and vpon his sunnes garments with him: and sanctified Aaron, and his garments, and his sonnes, and his sonnes garments with him.
text.NABRE: 8:30 Taking some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that was on the altar, Moses sprinkled it upon Aaron and his vestments, as well as his sons and their vestments, thus consecrating both Aaron and his vestments and his sons and their vestments.

text.NRSV: And Moses said to Aaron and his sons, “Boil the flesh at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and eat it there with the bread that is in the basket of ordination offerings, as I was commanded, ‘Aaron and his sons shall eat it’;
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Aaron and unto his sons Boil ye the flesh, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and there, shall ye eat it, and the bread which is in the basket of installation,—as I have been commanded, saying, Aaron and his sons, shall eat it.
text.KJV: 8:31 ¶ And Moses said vnto Aaron and to his sonnes, Boile the flesh at the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation: and there eat it with the bread that is in the basket of consecrations, as I commanded, saying, Aaron and his sonnes shall eat it.
text.NABRE: 8:31 Moses said to Aaron and his sons, “Boil the meat at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and there eat it with the bread that is in the basket of the ordination offering, in keeping with the command I have received: ‘Aaron and his sons shall eat of it.’

text.NRSV: and what remains of the flesh and the bread you shall burn with fire.
text.Rotherham: But, that which is left of the flesh, and of the bread, with fire, shall ye consume.
text.KJV: 8:32 And that which remaineth of the flesh, and of the bread, shall yee burne with fire.
text.NABRE: 8:32 What is left over of the meat and the bread you shall burn in the fire.

text.NRSV: You shall not go outside the entrance of the tent of meeting for seven days, until the day when your period of ordination is completed. For it will take seven days to ordain you;
text.Rotherham: And from the entrance of the tent of meeting, shall ye not go forth for seven days, until the day that filleth up the days of your installation,—because for seven days, will he install you:
text.KJV: 8:33 And ye shall not goe out of the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation in seuen dayes, vntill the dayes of your consecration be at an end: for seuen dayes shall he consecrate you.
text.NABRE: 8:33 Moreover, you are not to depart from the entrance of the tent of meeting for seven days, until the days of your ordination are completed; for your ordination is to last for seven days.

text.NRSV: as has been done today, the Lord has commanded to be done to make atonement for you.
text.Rotherham: as hath been done this day, [so] hath Yahweh commanded to be done, to put a propitiatory-covering over you.
text.KJV: 8:34 As he hath done this day, so the Lord hath commanded to doe, to make an atonement for you.
text.NABRE: 8:34 What has been done today the LORD has commanded be done, to make atonement for you.

text.NRSV: You shall remain at the entrance of the tent of meeting day and night for seven days, keeping the Lord’s charge so that you do not die; for so I am commanded.”
text.Rotherham: Even at the entrance of the tent of meeting, shall ye abide day and night for seven days, and shall keep the watch of Yahweh and shall not die,—for, so, am I commanded.
text.KJV: 8:35 Therefore shall ye abide at the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation day and night, seuen dayes, and keepe the charge of the Lord, that ye die not: for so I am commanded.
text.NABRE: 8:35 You must remain at the entrance of the tent of meeting day and night for seven days, carrying out the prescriptions of the LORD, so that you do not die, for this is the command I have received.”

text.NRSV: Aaron and his sons did all the things that the Lord commanded through Moses.
text.Rotherham: And Aaron and his sons did all the things which Yahweh had commanded through the mediation of Moses.
text.KJV: 8:36 So Aaron and his sonnes did all things which the Lord commanded by the hand of Moses.
text.NABRE: 8:36 So Aaron and his sons did all that the LORD had commanded through Moses.

text.NRSV: On the eighth day Moses summoned Aaron and his sons and the elders of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass on the eighth day, that Moses called for Aaron, and for his sons,—and for the elders of Israel;
text.KJV: 9:1 And it came to passe on the eight day, that Moses called Aaron and his sonnes, and the elders of Israel.
text.NABRE: 9:1 Chapter 9 - Octave of the Ordination. On the eighth day Moses summoned Aaron and his sons, together with the elders of Israel,

text.NRSV: He said to Aaron, “Take a bull calf for a sin offering and a ram for a burnt offering, without blemish, and offer them before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and said unto Aaron—Take for thyself a choice calf, as a sin-bearer, and a ram for an ascending-sacrifice, each without defect,—and bring them near before Yahweh;
text.KJV: 9:2 And hee saide vnto Aaron, Take thee a yong calfe for a sinne offering, and a ramme for a burnt offering, without blemish, and offer them before the Lord.
text.NABRE: 9:2 and said to Aaron, “Take a calf of the herd for a purification offering and a ram for a burnt offering, both without blemish, and offer them before the LORD.

text.NRSV: And say to the people of Israel, ‘Take a male goat for a sin offering; a calf and a lamb, yearlings without blemish, for a burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: and unto the sons of Israel, shalt thou speak, saying,—Take ye a he-goat, as a sin-bearer, and a calf and a young sheep, each a year old, without defect for an ascending-sacrifice;
text.KJV: 9:3 And vnto the children of Israel thou shalt speake, saying, Take ye a kid of the goats, for a sinne offering, and a calfe, and a lambe, both of the first yeere without blemish, for a burnt offering.
text.NABRE: 9:3 Tell the Israelites, too: Take a he-goat for a purification offering, a calf and a lamb, both unblemished yearlings, for a burnt offering,

text.NRSV: and an ox and a ram for an offering of well-being to sacrifice before the Lord; and a grain offering mixed with oil. For today the Lord will appear to you.’”
text.Rotherham: and an ox and a ram for peace-offerings, to sacrifice before Yahweh, and a meal-offering overflowed with oil,—for, to-day, doth, Yahweh, appear unto you.
text.KJV: 9:4 Also a bullocke and a ramme, for peace offerings, to sacrifice before the Lord, and a meat offring mingled with oyle: for to day the Lord will appeare vnto you.
text.NABRE: 9:4 and an ox and a ram for a communion sacrifice, to sacrifice before the LORD, along with a grain offering mixed with oil; for today the LORD will appear to you.”

text.NRSV: They brought what Moses commanded to the front of the tent of meeting; and the whole congregation drew near and stood before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So they brought that which Moses commanded before the tent of meeting,—and all the assembly drew near, and stood before Yahweh.
text.KJV: 9:5 ¶ And they brought that which Moses commanded, before the Tabernacle of the Congregation: and all the Congregation drew neere and stood before the Lord.
text.NABRE: 9:5 So they brought what Moses had ordered before the tent of meeting. When the whole community had come forward and stood before the LORD,

text.NRSV: And Moses said, “This is the thing that the Lord commanded you to do, so that the glory of the Lord may appear to you.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said—This, is the thing which Yahweh hath commanded that ye shall do,—that the glory of Yahweh may appear unto you.
text.KJV: 9:6 And Moses said, This is the thing which the Lord commanded that ye should doe: and the glory of the Lord shall appeare vnto you.
text.NABRE: 9:6 Moses said, “This is what the LORD orders you to do, that the glory of the LORD may appear to you.

text.NRSV: Then Moses said to Aaron, “Draw near to the altar and sacrifice your sin offering and your burnt offering, and make atonement for yourself and for the people; and sacrifice the offering of the people, and make atonement for them; as the Lord has commanded.”
text.Rotherham: and Moses said unto Aaron—Draw near unto the altar, and offer thy sin-bearer and thine ascending-sacrifice, and put a propitiatory-covering about thyself and about the people,—and offer the oblation of the people and put a propitiatory-covering about them, As Yahweh hath commanded.
text.KJV: 9:7 And Moses said vnto Aaron, Goe vnto the Altar, and offer thy sinne offering, and thy burnt offering, and make an atonement for thy selfe, and for the people: and offer the offering of the people, and make an atonement for them, as the Lord commanded.
text.NABRE: 9:7 Approach the altar,” Moses then told Aaron, “and make your purification offering and your burnt offering in atonement for yourself and for your household; then make the offering of the people in atonement for them, as the LORD has commanded.”

text.NRSV: Aaron drew near to the altar, and slaughtered the calf of the sin offering, which was for himself.
text.Rotherham: So Aaron drew near unto the altar,—and slew the sin-bearing calf which was for himself;
text.KJV: 9:8 ¶ Aaron therefore went vnto the Altar, and slew the calfe of the sinne offering, which was for himselfe.
text.NABRE: 9:8 Approaching the altar, Aaron first slaughtered the calf of the purification offering that was his own offering.

text.NRSV: The sons of Aaron presented the blood to him, and he dipped his finger in the blood and put it on the horns of the altar; and the rest of the blood he poured out at the base of the altar.
text.Rotherham: and the sons of Aaron brought the blood unto him, and he dipped his finger in the blood, and put [thereof] upon the horns of the altar,—and the [remainder of the] blood, poured he out at the base of the altar;
text.KJV: 9:9 And the sonnes of Aaron brought the blood vnto him, and he dipt his finger in the blood, and put it vpon the hornes of the Altar, and powred out the blood at the bottome of the Altar.
text.NABRE: 9:9 When his sons presented the blood to him, he dipped his finger in the blood and put it on the horns of the altar. The rest of the blood he poured out at the base of the altar.

text.NRSV: But the fat, the kidneys, and the appendage of the liver from the sin offering he turned into smoke on the altar, as the Lord commanded Moses;
text.Rotherham: and with the fat and the kidneys and the caul a from the liver, of the sin-bearer, made he a perfume at the altar,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: 9:10 But the fat and the kidneys, and the caule aboue the liuer of the sinne offering he burnt vpon the Altar, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: 9:10 He then burned on the altar the fat, the kidneys and the lobe of the liver from the purification offering, as the LORD had commanded Moses;

text.NRSV: and the flesh and the skin he burned with fire outside the camp.
text.Rotherham: But, the flesh and the skin, consumed he with fire, outside the camp.
text.KJV: 9:11 And the flesh and the hide he burnt with fire, without the campe.
text.NABRE: 9:11 but the flesh and the hide he burned in the fire outside the camp.

text.NRSV: Then he slaughtered the burnt offering. Aaron’s sons brought him the blood, and he dashed it against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: Then slew he the ascending-sacrifice,—and the sons of Aaron presented unto him the blood, and he dashed it against the altar round about.
text.KJV: 9:12 And hee slew the burnt offering, and Aarons sonnes presented vnto him the blood, which he sprinckled round about vpon the Altar.
text.NABRE: 9:12 Then Aaron slaughtered the burnt offering. When his sons brought him the blood, he splashed it on all sides of the altar.

text.NRSV: And they brought him the burnt offering piece by piece, and the head, which he turned into smoke on the altar.
text.Rotherham: And the ascending-sacrifice, presented they unto him piece by piece thereof also the head,—and he made a perfume upon the altar:
text.KJV: 9:13 And they presented the burnt offering vnto him, with the pieces thereof, and the head: and he burnt them vpon the Altar.
text.NABRE: 9:13 They then brought him the pieces and the head of the burnt offering, and he burned them on the altar.

text.NRSV: He washed the entrails and the legs and, with the burnt offering, turned them into smoke on the altar.
text.Rotherham: and he bathed the inwards and the legs,—and made a perfume upon the ascending-sacrifice at the altar.
text.KJV: 9:14 And he did wash the inwards, and the legs, and burnt them vpon the burnt offering on the Altar.
text.NABRE: 9:14 Having washed the inner organs and the shanks, he burned these also with the burnt offering on the altar.

text.NRSV: Then he presented the people’s offering. He took the goat of the sin offering that was for the people, and slaughtered it, and presented it as a sin offering like the first one.
text.Rotherham: Then brought he near the oblation of the people,—and took the sin-bearing goat which pertained to the people, and slew it and made therewith a sin-bearer, like the first.
text.KJV: 9:15 ¶ And he brought the peoples offering, and tooke the goat, which was the sinne offering for the people, and slew it, and offered it for sinne, as the first.
text.NABRE: 9:15 Then he had the people’s offering brought. Taking the goat that was for the people’s purification offering, he slaughtered it and offered it as a purification offering as before.

text.NRSV: He presented the burnt offering, and sacrificed it according to the regulation.
text.Rotherham: Then brought he near the ascending-sacrifice,—and offered it, according to the regulation,
text.KJV: 9:16 And he brought the burnt offring, and offered it according to the maner.
text.NABRE: 9:16 Then he brought forward the burnt offering and offered it according to procedure.

text.NRSV: Next he presented the grain offering, took a handful of it, and turned it into smoke on the altar, in addition to the burnt offering of the morning.
text.Rotherham: Then brought he near the meal-offering, and filled his hand therefrom, and made a perfume upon the altar,—in addition to the ascending-sacrifice of the morning.
text.KJV: 9:17 And he brought the meat offring, and tooke an handfull thereof, and burnt it vpon the Altar, beside the burnt sacrifice of the morning.
text.NABRE: 9:17 He then presented the grain offering; taking a handful of it, he burned it on the altar, in addition to the morning burnt offering.

text.NRSV: He slaughtered the ox and the ram as a sacrifice of well-being for the people. Aaron’s sons brought him the blood, which he dashed against all sides of the altar,
text.Rotherham: Then slew he the ox, and the ram, as a peace-offering, which pertained unto the people,—and the sons of Aaron presented the blood unto him, and he dashed it against the altar, round about;
text.KJV: 9:18 He slew also the bullocke and the ramme, for a sacrifice of peace offerings, which was for the people: And Aarons sonnes presented vnto him the blood, (which hee sprinckled vpon the Altar round about)
text.NABRE: 9:18 Finally he slaughtered the ox and the ram, the communion sacrifice of the people. When his sons brought him the blood, Aaron splashed it on all sides of the altar.

text.NRSV: and the fat from the ox and from the ram—the broad tail, the fat that covers the entrails, the two kidneys and the fat on them, and the appendage of the liver.
text.Rotherham: and the portions of fat from the ox,—and from the ram, the fat-tail, and the covering, and the kidneys, and the caul of the liver;
text.KJV: 9:19 And the fat of the bullocke and of the ramme, the rumpe, and that which couereth the inwards, and the kidneys, and the caule aboue the liuer,
text.NABRE: 9:19 The portions of fat from the ox and from the ram, the fatty tail, the covering fat, the kidneys, and the lobe of the liver

text.NRSV: They first laid the fat on the breasts, and the fat was turned into smoke on the altar;
text.Rotherham: and they put the portions of fat upon the breasts,—and he made a perfume with the fat at the altar;
text.KJV: 9:20 And they put the fat vpon the brests, & he burnt the fat vpon the altar:
text.NABRE: 9:20 they placed on top of the briskets. Aaron burned the fat pieces on the altar,

text.NRSV: and Aaron raised the breasts and the right thigh as an elevation offering before the Lord, as Moses had commanded.
text.Rotherham: but the breasts and the right shoulder, did Aaron wave as a wave-offering, before Yahweh,—as Moses commanded,
text.KJV: 9:21 And the breasts and the right shoulder, Aaron waued for a waue offering before the Lord, as Moses commanded.
text.NABRE: 9:21 but the briskets and the right thigh he raised as an elevated offering before the LORD, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: Aaron lifted his hands toward the people and blessed them; and he came down after sacrificing the sin offering, the burnt offering, and the offering of well-being.
text.Rotherham: Then Aaron lifted up his hands towards the people, and blessed them,—and he came down from offering the sin-bearer, and the ascending-sacrifice and the peace-offering.
text.KJV: 9:22 And Aaron lift up his hand towards the people, and blessed them, and came downe from offering of the sinne offering, and the burnt offering, and peace offerings.
text.NABRE: 9:22 Revelation of the Lord’s Glory. Aaron then raised his hands over the people and blessed them. When he came down from offering the purification offering, the burnt offering, and the communion offering,

text.NRSV: Moses and Aaron entered the tent of meeting, and then came out and blessed the people; and the glory of the Lord appeared to all the people.
text.Rotherham: And when Moses and Aaron had entered into the tent of meeting,—and had come forth, and blessed the people, then appeared the glory of Yahweh, unto all the people;
text.KJV: 9:23 And Moses and Aaron went into the Tabernacle of the Congregation, and came out, and blessed the people: and the glory of the Lord appeared vnto all the people.
text.NABRE: 9:23 Moses and Aaron went into the tent of meeting. On coming out they blessed the people. Then the glory of the LORD appeared to all the people.

text.NRSV: Fire came out from the Lord and consumed the burnt offering and the fat pieces on the altar; and when all the people saw it, they shouted and fell on their faces.
text.Rotherham: yea, there came forth fire from before Yahweh, and consumed upon the altar, the ascending-sacrifice and the portions of fat,—and all the people beheld and shouted, and fell down upon their faces.
text.KJV: 9:24 And there came a fire out from before the Lord, and consumed vpon the Altar the burnt offering, and the fat: which when all the people saw, they shouted, and fell on their faces.
text.NABRE: 9:24 Fire came forth from the LORD’s presence and consumed the burnt offering and the fat on the altar. Seeing this, all the people shouted with joy and fell prostrate.

text.NRSV: Now Aaron’s sons Nadab and Abihu each took his censer, put fire in it, and laid incense on it; they offered unholy fire before the Lord, such as he had not commanded them.
text.Rotherham: Then Aaron’s sons, Nadab and Abihu, took each man his censer and placed therein fire, and put thereon incense,—and brought near before Yahweh strange fire, which he had not commanded them.
text.KJV: 10:1 And Nadab and Abihu, the sonnes of Aaron, tooke either of them his censer, and put fire therein, and put incense thereon, and offered strange fire before the Lord, which hee commaunded them not.
text.NABRE: 10:1 Chapter 10 - Nadab and Abihu. Aaron’s sons Nadab and Abihu took their censers and, putting incense on the fire they had set in them, they offered before the LORD unauthorized fire, such as he had not commanded.

text.NRSV: And fire came out from the presence of the Lord and consumed them, and they died before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then came there forth fire from before Yahweh, and consumed them,—and they died before Yahweh.
text.KJV: 10:2 And there went out fire from the Lord and deuoured them, and they died before the Lord.
text.NABRE: 10:2 Fire therefore came forth from the LORD’s presence and consumed them, so that they died in the LORD’s presence.

text.NRSV: Then Moses said to Aaron, “This is what the Lord meant when he said, ‘Through those who are near me I will show myself holy, and before all the people I will be glorified.’” And Aaron fell silent.
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses unto Aaron—The very thing, that Yahweh spake, saying—In them that draw near to me, must I be hallowed, And before the faces of all the people, must I get myself honour,—And Aaron, was dumb.
text.KJV: 10:3 Then Moses said vnto Aaron, This is it that the Lord spake, saying, I will bee sanctified in them that come nigh me, and before all the people I will be glorified: And Aaron held his peace.
text.NABRE: 10:3 Moses then said to Aaron, “This is as the LORD said: Through those near to me I will be sanctified; in the sight of all the people I will obtain glory.” But Aaron said nothing.

text.NRSV: Moses summoned Mishael and Elzaphan, sons of Uzziel the uncle of Aaron, and said to them, “Come forward, and carry your kinsmen away from the front of the sanctuary to a place outside the camp.”
text.Rotherham: Then called Moses unto Mishael, and unto Elzaphan, sons of Uzziel, uncle of Aaron,—and said unto them—Draw near bear away your brethren from before the sanctuary, unto the outside of the camp.
text.KJV: 10:4 And Moses called Mishael and Elzaphan the sonnes of Uzziel, the vncle of Aaron, and said vnto them, Come neere, cary your brethren from before the Sanctuary, out of the campe.
text.NABRE: 10:4 Then Moses summoned Mishael and Elzaphan, the sons of Aaron’s uncle Uzziel, with the order, “Come, carry your kinsmen from before the sanctuary to a place outside the camp.”

text.NRSV: They came forward and carried them by their tunics out of the camp, as Moses had ordered.
text.Rotherham: So they drew near and bare them away, in their tunics, unto the outside of the camp,—as spake Moses.
text.KJV: 10:5 So they went neere, and caried them in their coats out of the campe, as Moses had said.
text.NABRE: 10:5 So they drew near and carried them by means of their tunics outside the camp, as Moses had commanded.

text.NRSV: And Moses said to Aaron and to his sons Eleazar and Ithamar, “Do not bare your heads or tear your clothes, or you will die and wrath will strike all the congregation; but your kindred, the whole house of Israel, shall bewail the burning that the Lord has kindled.
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses unto Aaron and to Eleazar and to Ithamar, his sons—Your heads, ye may not bare and your garments, ye shall not rend so shall ye not die, neither against all the assembly, will he be wroth,—but let, your brethren the whole house of Israel, bewail the consuming fire wherewith Yahweh hath consumed.
text.KJV: 10:6 And Moses said vnto Aaron, and vnto Eleazar and vnto Ithamar his sonnes, Uncouer not your heads, neither rend your clothes, lest you die, and lest wrath come vpon all the people: But let your brethren, the whole house of Israel, bewaile the burning which the Lord hath kindled.
text.NABRE: 10:6 Conduct of the Priests. Moses said to Aaron and his sons Eleazar and Ithamar, “Do not dishevel your hair or tear your garments, lest you die and bring God’s wrath also on the whole community.
