While your kindred, the rest of the house of Israel, may mourn for those whom the LORD’s fire has burned up,

text.NRSV: You shall not go outside the entrance of the tent of meeting, or you will die; for the anointing oil of the Lord is on you.” And they did as Moses had ordered.
text.Rotherham: But, from the entrance of the tent of meeting, shall ye not go forth, lest ye die, for, the anointing oil of Yahweh is upon you. And they did according to the word of Moses.
text.KJV: 10:7 And ye shal not goe out from the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation, lest you die: for the anointing oyle of the Lord is vpon you: and they did according to the word of Moses.
text.NABRE: 10:7 you shall not go beyond the entrance of the tent of meeting, else you shall die; for the anointing oil of the LORD is upon you.” So they did as Moses told them.

text.NRSV: And the Lord spoke to Aaron:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Aaron, saying:
text.KJV: 10:8 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Aaron, saying,
text.NABRE: 10:8 The LORD said to Aaron:

text.NRSV: Drink no wine or strong drink, neither you nor your sons, when you enter the tent of meeting, that you may not die; it is a statute forever throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: Wine and strong drink, thou mayest not drink,—thou nor thy sons with thee when ye enter into the tent of meeting, so shall ye not die,—an age-abiding statute to your generations;
text.KJV: 10:9 Doe not drinke wine nor strong drinke, thou, nor thy sonnes with thee, when ye goe into the Tabernacle of the Congregation, lest yee die: It shall bee a statute for euer, throughout your generations:
text.NABRE: 10:9 When you are to go to the tent of meeting, you and your sons are forbidden, by a perpetual statute throughout your generations, to drink any wine or strong drink, lest you die.

text.NRSV: You are to distinguish between the holy and the common, and between the unclean and the clean;
text.Rotherham: That ye may make a difference, between the sacred and the common,—and between the unclean and the clean;
text.KJV: 10:10 And that ye may put difference betweene holy and vnholy, and betweene vncleane and cleane:
text.NABRE: 10:10 You must be able to distinguish between what is sacred and what is profane, and between what is clean and what is unclean;

text.NRSV: and you are to teach the people of Israel all the statutes that the Lord has spoken to them through Moses.
text.Rotherham: And may teach the sons of Israel,—all the statutes which Yahweh hath spoken unto them, by the mediation of Moses.
text.KJV: 10:11 And that ye may teach the children of Israel all the statutes which the Lord hath spoken vnto them by the hand of Moses.
text.NABRE: 10:11 and you must be able to teach the Israelites all the statutes that the LORD has given them through Moses.

text.NRSV: Moses spoke to Aaron and to his remaining sons, Eleazar and Ithamar: “Take the grain offering that is left from the Lord’s offerings by fire, and eat it unleavened beside the altar, for it is most holy;
text.Rotherham: Then spake Moses unto Aaron and unto Eleazar and unto Ithamar his sons that were left, Take ye the meal-offering that is left of the altar-flames of Yahweh, and eat it unleavened beside the altar,—for, most holy, it is;
text.KJV: 10:12 ¶ And Moses spake vnto Aaron, and vnto Eleazar and vnto Ithamar his sonnes that were left, Take the meate offering that remaineth of the offerings of the Lord made by fire, and eate it without leauen, beside the altar: for it is most holy.
text.NABRE: 10:12 The Eating of the Priestly Portions. Moses said to Aaron and his surviving sons, Eleazar and Ithamar, “Take the grain offering left over from the oblations to the LORD, and eat it beside the altar in the form of unleavened cakes, since it is most holy.

text.NRSV: you shall eat it in a holy place, because it is your due and your sons’ due, from the offerings by fire to the Lord; for so I am commanded.
text.Rotherham: therefore shall ye eat it in a holy place, for, thine by statute, and thy sons’ by statute, it is, from among the altar-flames of Yahweh,—for, so, am I commanded.
text.KJV: 10:13 And ye shal eat it in the holy place, because it is thy due, and thy sonnes due of the sacrifices of the Lord, made by fire: for so I am commanded.
text.NABRE: 10:13 You must eat it in a sacred place because it is your and your sons’ due from the oblations to the LORD; such is the command I have received.

text.NRSV: But the breast that is elevated and the thigh that is raised, you and your sons and daughters as well may eat in any clean place; for they have been assigned to you and your children from the sacrifices of the offerings of well-being of the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And the wave-breast, and the heave-shoulder, shall ye eat in clean place, thou, and thy sons and thy daughters with thee,—for as thine by statute and thy sons’ by statute, have they been given, from among the peace-offerings of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 10:14 And the waue breast and heaue shoulder shall ye eate in a cleane place, thou, and thy sonnes, and thy daughters with thee: For they be thy due and thy sonnes due, which are giuen out of the sacrifice of peace offerings, of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: 10:14 The brisket of the elevated offering and the leg of the contribution, however, you and your sons and daughters may eat, in a clean place; for these have been assigned to you and your children as your due from the communion sacrifices of the Israelites.

text.NRSV: The thigh that is raised and the breast that is elevated they shall bring, together with the offerings by fire of the fat, to raise for an elevation offering before the Lord; they are to be your due and that of your children forever, as the Lord has commanded.
text.Rotherham: The heave-shoulder and the wave-breast upon the altar-flames of the fat portions, shall they bring in, to wave as a wave-offering, before Yahweh,—so shall they be thine, and thy sons’ with thee, by an age-abiding statute, As Yahweh hath commanded.
text.KJV: 10:15 The heaue shoulder, and the waue breast shal they bring, with the offrings made by fire of the fat, to waue it for a waue offering before the Lord: and it shall bee thine, and thy sonnes with thee, by a statute for euer, as the Lord hath commanded.
text.NABRE: 10:15 The leg of the contribution and the brisket of the elevated offering shall be brought in with the oblations of fat to be raised as an elevated offering before the LORD. They shall belong to you and your children as your due forever, as the LORD has commanded.”

text.NRSV: Then Moses made inquiry about the goat of the sin offering, and—it had already been burned! He was angry with Eleazar and Ithamar, Aaron’s remaining sons, and said,
text.Rotherham: Now, as for the sin-bearing goat, Moses diligently sought it but lo! it had been burnt up,—then was he wroth against Eleazar and against Ithamar, the sons of Aaron that were left, saying:
text.KJV: 10:16 ¶ And Moses diligently sought the goate of the sinne offering, and behold, it was burnt: and he was angry with Eleazar and Ithamar, the sonnes of Aaron, which were left aliue, saying,
text.NABRE: 10:16 Moses inquired closely about the goat of the purification offering and discovered that it had all been burned. So he was angry with the surviving sons of Aaron, Eleazar and Ithamar, and said,

text.NRSV: “Why did you not eat the sin offering in the sacred area? For it is most holy, and God has given it to you that you may remove the guilt of the congregation, to make atonement on their behalf before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore did ye not eat the sin-bearer in the holy place? for most, holy, it is,—and thee same, hath he given you that ye may bear the iniquity of the assembly, to put a propitiatory-covering over them before Yahweh.
text.KJV: 10:17 Wherefore haue ye not eaten the sinne offering in the holy place, seeing it is most holy, and God hath giuen it you to beare the iniquitie of the Congregation, to make atonement for them, before the Lord?
text.NABRE: 10:17 “Why did you not eat the purification offering in the sacred place, since it is most holy? It has been given to you that you might remove the guilt of the community and make atonement for them before the LORD.

text.NRSV: Its blood was not brought into the inner part of the sanctuary. You should certainly have eaten it in the sanctuary, as I commanded.”
text.Rotherham: Lo! the blood thereof had not been taken into the holy place, within,—ye should have, indeed eaten, it, in a holy place, as I commanded.
text.KJV: 10:18 Behold, the blood of it was not brought in, within the holy place: yee should indeed haue eaten it in the holy place, as I commanded.
text.NABRE: 10:18 Since its blood was not brought inside the sanctuary, you should certainly have eaten the offering in the sanctuary, as I was commanded.”

text.NRSV: And Aaron spoke to Moses, “See, today they offered their sin offering and their burnt offering before the Lord; and yet such things as these have befallen me! If I had eaten the sin offering today, would it have been acceptable to the Lord?”
text.Rotherham: Then spake Aaron unto Moses—Lo! this very day, when they had brought near their own sin-bearer, and their own ascending-sacrifice, there befell me such things as these,—if, then, I had eaten of the sin-bearer this day, would it have been well-pleasing in the eyes of Yahweh?
text.KJV: 10:19 And Aaron said vnto Moses, Behold, this day haue they offered their sinne offering, and their burnt offering before the Lord: and such things haue befallen me: and if I had eaten the sinne offering to day, should it haue bin accepted in the sight of the Lord?
text.NABRE: 10:19 Aaron answered Moses, “Even though they presented their purification offering and burnt offering before the LORD today, still this misfortune has befallen me. Had I then eaten of the purification offering today, would it have been pleasing to the LORD?”

text.NRSV: And when Moses heard that, he agreed.
text.Rotherham: And, when Moses heard that, then was it well-pleasing in his eyes.
text.KJV: 10:20 And when Moses heard that, he was content.
text.NABRE: 10:20 On hearing this, Moses was satisfied.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying unto them:
text.KJV: 11:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, and to Aaron, saying vnto them,
text.NABRE: 11:1 III. Laws Regarding Ritual Purity Chapter 11 - Clean and Unclean Meats. The LORD said to Moses and Aaron:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: From among all the land animals these are the creatures that you may eat.
text.Rotherham: Speak ye unto the sons of Israel saying,—These are the living things which ye may eat, of all the beasts which are upon the earth:
text.KJV: 11:2 Speake vnto the children of Israel, saying, These are the beasts which ye shal eat among all the beasts that are on the earth:
text.NABRE: 11:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: Of all land animals these are the ones you may eat:

text.NRSV: Any animal that has divided hoofs and is cleft-footed and chews the cud—such you may eat.
text.Rotherham: Whatsoever parteth the hoof and is cloven-footed, and cheweth the cud among beasts, that, may ye eat.
text.KJV: 11:3 Whatsoever parteth the hoofe, and is clouen footed, & cheweth cud among the beasts, that shall ye eate.
text.NABRE: 11:3 Any animal that has hoofs you may eat, provided it is cloven-footed and chews the cud.

text.NRSV: But among those that chew the cud or have divided hoofs, you shall not eat the following: the camel, for even though it chews the cud, it does not have divided hoofs; it is unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, these, shall ye not eat, of them that chew the cud, and of them that part the hoof,—the camel, because though he, cheweth the cud, yet, the hoof, he parteth not, unclean, he is to you;
text.KJV: 11:4 Neuerthelesse, these shall ye not eate, of them that chewe the cud, or of them that diuide the hoofe: as the camel, because hee cheweth the cud, but diuideth not the hoofe, he is vncleane vnto you.
text.NABRE: 11:4 But you shall not eat any of the following from among those that only chew the cud or only have hoofs: the camel, which indeed chews the cud, but does not have hoofs and is therefore unclean for you;

text.NRSV: The rock badger, for even though it chews the cud, it does not have divided hoofs; it is unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: And, the coney, because, though he cheweth the cud, yet, the hoof, he parteth not,—unclean, he is to you;
text.KJV: 11:5 And the conie, because he cheweth the cud, but diuideth not the hoofe, he is vncleane vnto you.
text.NABRE: 11:5 the rock hyrax, which indeed chews the cud, but does not have hoofs and is therefore unclean for you;

text.NRSV: The hare, for even though it chews the cud, it does not have divided hoofs; it is unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: And the hare, because though she cheweth the cud, yet the hoof, she parteth not,—unclean, she is to you;
text.KJV: 11:6 And the hare, because he cheweth the cud, but diuideth not the hoofe, he is vncleane vnto you.
text.NABRE: 11:6 the hare, which indeed chews the cud, but does not have hoofs and is therefore unclean for you;

text.NRSV: The pig, for even though it has divided hoofs and is cleft-footed, it does not chew the cud; it is unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: And, the swine, because though he parteth the hoof, and is cloven-footed, yet, the cud, he cheweth not,—unclean, he is to you;
text.KJV: 11:7 And the swine, though he diuide the hoofe, and be clouen footed, yet hee cheweth not the cud: he is vncleane to you.
text.NABRE: 11:7 and the pig, which does indeed have hoofs and is cloven-footed, but does not chew the cud and is therefore unclean for you.

text.NRSV: Of their flesh you shall not eat, and their carcasses you shall not touch; they are unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: Of their flesh, shall ye not eat, and, their carcase, shall ye not touch,—unclean, they are to you.
text.KJV: 11:8 Of their flesh shall ye not eat, and their carcase shall ye not touch: they are vncleane to you.
text.NABRE: 11:8 You shall not eat their meat, and you shall not touch their carcasses; they are unclean for you.

text.NRSV: These you may eat, of all that are in the waters. Everything in the waters that has fins and scales, whether in the seas or in the streams—such you may eat.
text.Rotherham: these, may ye eat, of all that are in the waters,—all that have fins and scales in the waters, in the seas and in the rivers, them, may ye eat.
text.KJV: 11:9 ¶ These shal ye eat, of all that are in the waters: whatsoeuer hath finnes and scales in the waters, in the seas, and in the riuers, them shall ye eate.
text.NABRE: 11:9 Of the various creatures that live in the water, you may eat the following: whatever in the seas or in river waters that has both fins and scales you may eat.

text.NRSV: But anything in the seas or the streams that does not have fins and scales, of the swarming creatures in the waters and among all the other living creatures that are in the waters—they are detestable to you
text.Rotherham: But, all that have not fins and scales, in the seas and in the rivers, of all that swarm in the waters, and of all the living souls that are in the waters, an abomination, they are unto you;
text.KJV: 11:10 And all that haue not finnes nor scales in the seas, and in the riuers, of all that moue in the waters, and of any liuing thing which is in the waters, they shalbe an abomination vnto you:
text.NABRE: 11:10 But of the creatures that swarm in the water or of animals that otherwise live in the water, whether in the sea or in the rivers, all those that lack either fins or scales are loathsome for you,

text.NRSV: and detestable they shall remain. Of their flesh you shall not eat, and their carcasses you shall regard as detestable.
text.Rotherham: and an abomination, shall they remain to you,—of their flesh, ye shall not eat, and their carcases, shall ye abhor.
text.KJV: 11:11 They shalbe euen an abomination vnto you: ye shall not eat of their flesh, but you shall haue their carcases in abomination.
text.NABRE: 11:11 and shall always be loathsome to you. Their meat you shall not eat, and their carcasses you shall loathe.

text.NRSV: Everything in the waters that does not have fins and scales is detestable to you.
text.Rotherham: Whatsoever hath not fins and scales in the waters, an abomination, it is unto you.
text.KJV: 11:12 Whatsoeuer hath no finnes nor scales in the waters, that shalbe an abomination vnto you.
text.NABRE: 11:12 Every water creature that lacks fins or scales is loathsome for you.

text.NRSV: These you shall regard as detestable among the birds. They shall not be eaten; they are an abomination: the eagle, the vulture, the osprey,
text.Rotherham: And, these, shall ye abhor of birds, they shall not be eaten, an abomination, they are,—the eagle, and the ossifrage, and the osprey;
text.KJV: 11:13 ¶ And these are they which ye shall haue in abomination among the foules, they shall not be eaten, they are an abomination: The Eagle, and the Ossifrage, and the Ospray,
text.NABRE: 11:13 Of the birds, these you shall loathe; they shall not be eaten, they are loathsome: the griffon vulture, the bearded vulture, the black vulture,

text.NRSV: the buzzard, the kite of any kind;
text.Rotherham: and the vulture, and the falcon, after its kind;
text.KJV: 11:14 And the Uulture, and the Kite, after his kinde:
text.NABRE: 11:14 the kite, the various species of falcons,

text.NRSV: every raven of any kind;
text.Rotherham: every raven, after its kind;
text.KJV: 11:15 Euery Rauen after his kinde:
text.NABRE: 11:15 the various species of crows,

text.NRSV: the ostrich, the nighthawk, the sea gull, the hawk of any kind;
text.Rotherham: and the female ostrich, and the male ostrich, and the sea-gull,—and the hawk after its kind;
text.KJV: 11:16 And the Owle, and the nighthauke, & the Cuckow, and the Hawke after his kinde,
text.NABRE: 11:16 the eagle owl, the kestrel, the long-eared owl, the various species of hawks,

text.NRSV: the little owl, the cormorant, the great owl,
text.Rotherham: and the pelican and the gannet, and the bittern;
text.KJV: 11:17 And the little Owle, and the Cormorant, and the great Owle,
text.NABRE: 11:17 the little owl, the cormorant, the screech owl,

text.NRSV: the water hen, the desert owl, the carrion vulture,
text.Rotherham: and the swan and the vomiting pelican and the little vulture;
text.KJV: 11:18 And the Swanne, and the Pellicane, and the Gier-eagle,
text.NABRE: 11:18 the barn owl, the horned owl, the osprey,

text.NRSV: the stork, the heron of any kind, the hoopoe, and the bat.
text.Rotherham: and the stork, and the parrot, after its kind,—and the mountain-cock and the bat.
text.KJV: 11:19 And the Storke, the Heron after her kinde, and the Lapwing, and the Batte.
text.NABRE: 11:19 the stork, the various species of herons, the hoopoe, and the bat.

text.NRSV: All winged insects that walk upon all fours are detestable to you.
text.Rotherham: Every creeping thing that flieth, that goeth on all-fours, an abomination, it is unto you.
text.KJV: 11:20 All foules that creepe, going vpon all foure, shalbe an abomination vnto you.
text.NABRE: 11:20 The various winged insects that walk on all fours are loathsome for you.

text.NRSV: But among the winged insects that walk on all fours you may eat those that have jointed legs above their feet, with which to leap on the ground.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, these, may ye eat, of all creeping things that fly, that go on all-fours,—such as have legs above their feet, to leap therewith, upon the earth—
text.KJV: 11:21 Yet these may ye eat, of euery flying creeping thing that goeth vpon all foure, which haue legges aboue their feet, to leape withall vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: 11:21 But of the various winged insects that walk on all fours you may eat those that have legs jointed above their feet for leaping on the ground;

text.NRSV: Of them you may eat: the locust according to its kind, the bald locust according to its kind, the cricket according to its kind, and the grasshopper according to its kind.
text.Rotherham: these of them, may ye eat: the swarming-locust after its kind, and the devouring locust after its kind,—and the chargol-locust after its kind, and the chagab-locust after its kind.
text.KJV: 11:22 Euen these of them ye may eate: the Locust, after his kinde, and the Bald-locust after his kinde, and the Beetle after his kinde, and the Grassehopper after his kinde.
text.NABRE: 11:22 hence of these you may eat the following: the various kinds of locusts, the various kinds of bald locusts, the various kinds of crickets, and the various kinds of grasshoppers.

text.NRSV: But all other winged insects that have four feet are detestable to you.
text.Rotherham: But every [other] creeping thing that flieth, which hath four feet, an abomination, it is unto you;
text.KJV: 11:23 But al other flying creeping things which haue foure feet, shall be an abomination vnto you.
text.NABRE: 11:23 All other winged insects that have four legs are loathsome for you.

text.NRSV: By these you shall become unclean; whoever touches their carcass shall be unclean until the evening,
text.Rotherham: and, for these, shall ye count yourselves unclean—whosoever toucheth the carcase of them shall be unclean until the evening;
text.KJV: 11:24 And for these ye shalbe vncleane: whosoeuer toucheth the carkasse of them, shall be vncleane vntill the euen.
text.NABRE: 11:24 You become unclean by the following—anyone who touches their carcasses shall be unclean until evening,

text.NRSV: and whoever carries any part of their carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
text.Rotherham: and whosoever beareth away aught of the carcase of them, shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
text.KJV: 11:25 And whosoeuer beareth ought of the carkasse of them, shall wash his clothes, & be vncleane vntill the euen.
text.NABRE: 11:25 and anyone who carries any part of their carcasses shall wash his garments and be unclean until evening—

text.NRSV: Every animal that has divided hoofs but is not cleft-footed or does not chew the cud is unclean for you; everyone who touches them shall be unclean.
text.Rotherham: As for every kind of beast which, though it parteth the hoof, yet is not cloven-footed nor cheweth the cud, unclean, they are unto you,—every one who toucheth them shall be unclean.
text.KJV: 11:26 The carkasses of euery beast which diuideth the hoofe, and is not clouen footed, nor cheweth the cud, are vncleane vnto you: euery one that toucheth them, shalbe vncleane.
text.NABRE: 11:26 by all hoofed animals that are not cloven-footed or do not chew the cud; they are unclean for you; anyone who touches them becomes unclean.

text.NRSV: All that walk on their paws, among the animals that walk on all fours, are unclean for you; whoever touches their carcass shall be unclean until the evening,
text.Rotherham: And, all that go upon their paws, among all the living things that go on all-fours, unclean, they are unto you,—whoso toucheth the carcase of them, shall be unclean until the evening.
text.KJV: 11:27 And whatsoeuer goeth vpon his pawes, among all maner of beasts, that goe on all foure, those are vncleane vnto you: who so toucheth their carkasse, shall be vncleane vntill the Euen.
text.NABRE: 11:27 Also by the various quadrupeds that walk on paws; they are unclean for you; anyone who touches their carcasses shall be unclean until evening,

text.NRSV: and the one who carries their carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening; they are unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: And, he that beareth away the carcase of them shall wash his clothes, and shall be unclean until the evening,—unclean, they are unto you.
text.KJV: 11:28 And he that beareth the carkasse of them, shall wash his clothes, and be vncleane vntill the Euen: they are vncleane vnto you.
text.NABRE: 11:28 and anyone who carries their carcasses shall wash his garments and be unclean until evening. They are unclean for you.

text.NRSV: These are unclean for you among the creatures that swarm upon the earth: the weasel, the mouse, the great lizard according to its kind,
text.Rotherham: And, these, unto you, shall be unclean, among the creeping things that creep upon the earth,—the weasel and the mouse, and the lizard after its kind;
text.KJV: 11:29 ¶ These also shalbe vncleane vnto you, among the creeping things that creepe vpon the earth: the Weasell, and the Mouse, and the Tortois, after his kinde,
text.NABRE: 11:29 Of the creatures that swarm on the ground, the following are unclean for you: the rat, the mouse, the various kinds of lizards,

text.NRSV: the gecko, the land crocodile, the lizard, the sand lizard, and the chameleon.
text.Rotherham: and the ferret and the chameleon and the wall-lizard,—and the winding lizard, and the mole.
text.KJV: 11:30 And the Ferret, and the Cameleon, and the Lyzard, and the Snaile, and the Molle.
text.NABRE: 11:30 the gecko, the spotted lizard, the agama, the skink, and the chameleon.

text.NRSV: These are unclean for you among all that swarm; whoever touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until the evening.
text.Rotherham: These, are they which are unclean to you among all that creep,—whosoever toucheth them when they are dead shall be unclean until the evening;
text.KJV: 11:31 These are vncleane to you among all that creepe: whosoeuer doth touch them when they bee dead, shall be vncleane vntill the Euen.
text.NABRE: 11:31 Among the various swarming creatures, these are unclean for you. Everyone who touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until evening.

text.NRSV: And anything upon which any of them falls when they are dead shall be unclean, whether an article of wood or cloth or skin or sacking, any article that is used for any purpose; it shall be dipped into water, and it shall be unclean until the evening, and then it shall be clean.
text.Rotherham: and, everything whereon any of them shall fall when they are dead shall be unclean—of any articles of wood, or cloth, or skin, or sackcloth, any article wherewith any work is done,—shall be put in water and shall be unclean until the evening, and then be clean,
text.KJV: 11:32 And vpon whatsoeuer any of them, when they are dead, doeth fall, it shalbe vncleane, whether it be any vessel of wood, or raiment, or skinne, or sacke, whatsoeuer vessell it be, wherein any worke is done, it must be put into water, and it shall be vncleane vntill the Euen; so it shalbe cleansed.
text.NABRE: 11:32 Everything on which one of them falls when dead becomes unclean, including any article of wood, cloth, leather or goat hair—any article of which use can be made. It must be immersed in water and remain unclean until evening, when it again becomes clean.

text.NRSV: And if any of them falls into any earthen vessel, all that is in it shall be unclean, and you shall break the vessel.
text.Rotherham: And, as for any earthen vessel whereinto any of them may fall, everything therein shall be unclean, and, the vessel itself, shall ye break in pieces.
text.KJV: 11:33 And euery earthen vessel, whereinto any of them falleth, whatsoeuer is in it shall bee vncleane; and yee shall breake it.
text.NABRE: 11:33 Should any of these creatures fall into a clay vessel, everything in it becomes unclean, and the vessel itself you must break.

text.NRSV: Any food in it which may be eaten, upon which water falls, shall be unclean; all drink that may be drunk from any such vessel shall be unclean.
text.Rotherham: Of all the food that may be eaten, that whereon shall come water, shall be unclean,—and, all drink which might be drunk in any [such] vessel, shall be unclean.
text.KJV: 11:34 Of all meat which may be eaten, that on which such water commeth, shall be vncleane: And all drinke that may be drunkein euery such vessell, shalbe vncleane.
text.NABRE: 11:34 Any food that can be eaten which makes contact with water, and any liquid that may be drunk, in any such vessel become unclean.

text.NRSV: Anything on which any part of the carcass falls shall be unclean; whether an oven or stove, it shall be broken in pieces; they are unclean, and shall remain unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: And, everything whereon shall fall any part of the carcase of them shall be unclean, oven or fire-range, it shall be destroyed, unclean, they are,—and, unclean, Shall they remain to you.
text.KJV: 11:35 And euery thing, whereupon any part of their carkasse falleth, shall be vncleane, whether it be ouen, or ranges for pots, they shalbe broken downe: for they are vncleane, and shall be vncleane vnto you.
text.NABRE: 11:35 Any object on which any part of their carcasses falls becomes unclean; if it is an oven or stove, this must be broken to pieces; they are unclean and shall always be unclean to you.

text.NRSV: But a spring or a cistern holding water shall be clean, while whatever touches the carcass in it shall be unclean.
text.Rotherham: Notwithstanding, a fountain or cistern wherein is a gathering of waters shall be clean,—but, he that toucheth the carcase of them shall be unclean.
text.KJV: 11:36 Neuerthelesse, a fountaine or pit, wherein there is plenty of water, shalbe cleane: but that which toucheth their carkasse shalbe vncleane.
text.NABRE: 11:36 However, a spring or a cistern for collecting water remains clean; but whoever touches such an animal’s carcass becomes unclean.

text.NRSV: If any part of their carcass falls upon any seed set aside for sowing, it is clean;
text.Rotherham: And when any part of the carcase of them shall fall upon seed for sowing, which is to be sown, the same is, clean.
text.KJV: 11:37 And if any part of their carkasse fall vpon any sowing seed which is to be sowen, it shalbe cleane:
text.NABRE: 11:37 If any part of their carcasses falls on any sort of grain that is to be sown, it remains clean;

text.NRSV: but if water is put on the seed and any part of their carcass falls on it, it is unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: But, when water shall be put upon seed, and there shall fall thereon any part of the carcase of them, unclean, it is to you.
text.KJV: 11:38 But if any water be put vpon the seed, and any part of their carkasse fall thereon, it shalbe vncleane vnto you.
text.NABRE: 11:38 but if the grain has become moistened, it becomes unclean to you when any part of their carcasses falls on it.

text.NRSV: If any animal of which you may eat dies, anyone who touches its carcass shall be unclean until the evening.
text.Rotherham: And, when any of the beasts which are yours for food shall die, he that toucheth the carcase of it shall be unclean until the evening.
text.KJV: 11:39 And if any beast of which ye may eat, die, he that toucheth the carkasse thereof, shall be vncleane vntill the Euen.
text.NABRE: 11:39 When one of the animals that you could otherwise eat dies of itself, anyone who touches its carcass shall be unclean until evening;

text.NRSV: Those who eat of its carcass shall wash their clothes and be unclean until the evening; those who carry the carcass shall wash their clothes and be unclean until the evening.
text.Rotherham: And, he that eateth of the carcase of it, shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the evening,—he also that carrieth away the carcase thereof shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the evening.
text.KJV: 11:40 And hee that eateth of the carkasse of it, shall wash his clothes, and be vncleane vntil the Euen: he also that beareth the carkasse of it, shal wash his clothes, and bee vncleane vntill the Euen.
text.NABRE: 11:40 and anyone who eats any part of its carcass shall wash his garments and be unclean until evening; so also, anyone who carries its carcass shall wash his garments and be unclean until evening.

text.NRSV: All creatures that swarm upon the earth are detestable; they shall not be eaten.
text.Rotherham: And, any creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth, an abomination, it is—it shall not be eaten.
text.KJV: 11:41 And euery creeping thing that creepeth vpon the earth, shalbe an abomination: it shall not be eaten.
text.NABRE: 11:41 All the creatures that swarm on the ground are loathsome and shall not be eaten.

text.NRSV: Whatever moves on its belly, and whatever moves on all fours, or whatever has many feet, all the creatures that swarm upon the earth, you shall not eat; for they are detestable.
text.Rotherham: Everything that goeth upon the belly, and everything that goeth upon all-fours, even to everything having many feet, as regardeth any creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth, ye shall not eat them for, an abomination, they are.
text.KJV: 11:42 Whatsoeuer goeth vpon the bellie, and whatsoeuer goeth vpon all foure, or whatsoeuer hath more feet among all creeping things that creepe vpon the earth, them ye shall not eate, for they are an abomination.
text.NABRE: 11:42 Whether it crawls on its belly, goes on all fours, or has many legs—any creature that swarms on the earth—you shall not eat them; they are loathsome.

text.NRSV: You shall not make yourselves detestable with any creature that swarms; you shall not defile yourselves with them, and so become unclean.
text.Rotherham: Do not make your persons abominable, with any creeping thing that creepeth,—neither shall ye make yourselves unclean with them, that ye should become unclean thereby.
text.KJV: 11:43 Yee shall not make your selues abominable with any creeping thing that creepeth, neither shall ye make your selues vncleane with them, that ye should be defiled thereby.
text.NABRE: 11:43 Do not make yourselves loathsome by any swarming creature nor defile yourselves with them and so become unclean by them.

text.NRSV: For I am the Lord your God; sanctify yourselves therefore, and be holy, for I am holy. You shall not defile yourselves with any swarming creature that moves on the earth.
text.Rotherham: For, I—Yahweh, am your God, therefore shall ye hallow yourselves and remain holy; for, holy, am, I,—therefore shall ye not make your persons unclean, with any creeping thing that crawleth upon the earth;
text.KJV: 11:44 For I am the Lord your God: yee shall therefore sanctifie your selues, and ye shall be holy, for I am holy: neither shall ye defile your selues with any maner of creeping thing that creepeth vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: 11:44 For I, the LORD, am your God. You shall make and keep yourselves holy, because I am holy. You shall not make yourselves unclean, then, by any swarming creature that crawls on the ground.

text.NRSV: For I am the Lord who brought you up from the land of Egypt, to be your God; you shall be holy, for I am holy.
text.Rotherham: for, I—Yahweh, am he that brought you up out of the land of Egypt, that I might become your God,—therefore shall ye be holy, for, holy, am, I.
text.KJV: 11:45 For I am the Lord that bringeth you vp out of the land of Egypt to be your God: ye shal therefore be holy, for I am holy.
text.NABRE: 11:45 Since I, the LORD, am the one who brought you up from the land of Egypt that I might be your God, you shall be holy, because I am holy.

text.NRSV: This is the law of animals and birds and all living creatures that move in the waters and all creatures that swarm on the ground,
text.Rotherham: This, is the law of beast and of bird, and of every living soul that moveth in the waters,—and as to every soul that creepeth upon the earth:
text.KJV: 11:46 This is the law of the beasts, and of the foule, and of euery liuing creature that moueth in the waters, and of euerie creature that creepeth vpon the earth:
text.NABRE: 11:46 This is the instruction for land animals, birds, and all the creatures that move about in the water, as well as any animal that swarms on the ground,

text.NRSV: to make a distinction between the unclean and the clean, and between the living creature that may be eaten and the living creature that may not be eaten.
text.Rotherham: That ye may make a difference—between the unclean and the clean,—and between the living thing that may be eaten, and the living thing which may not be eaten.
text.KJV: 11:47 To make a difference betweene the vncleane and the cleane, & betweene the beast that may be eaten, and the beast that may not be eaten.
text.NABRE: 11:47 that you may distinguish between the clean and the unclean, and between creatures that may be eaten and those that may not be eaten.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 12:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 12:1 Chapter 12 - Uncleanness of Childbirth. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: If a woman conceives and bears a male child, she shall be ceremonially unclean seven days; as at the time of her menstruation, she shall be unclean.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, saying, A woman when she conceiveth seed and giveth birth to a male child, then shall she be unclean seven days, according to the days of her removal in her sickness shall she be unclean.
text.KJV: 12:2 Speake vnto the children of Israel, saying, If a woman haue conceiued seed, and borne a man child, then she shal be vncleane seuen dayes: according to the dayes of the separation for her infirmitie shall she be vncleane.
text.NABRE: 12:2 Tell the Israelites: When a woman has a child, giving birth to a boy, she shall be unclean for seven days, with the same uncleanness as during her menstrual period.

text.NRSV: On the eighth day the flesh of his foreskin shall be circumcised.
text.Rotherham: And, on the eighth day, shall the flesh of his foreskin be circumcised.
text.KJV: 12:3 And in the eight day, the flesh of his foreskinne shall be circumcised.
text.NABRE: 12:3 On the eighth day, the flesh of the boy’s foreskin shall be circumcised,

text.NRSV: Her time of blood purification shall be thirty-three days; she shall not touch any holy thing, or come into the sanctuary, until the days of her purification are completed.
text.Rotherham: And, for thirty-three days, shall she continue in the blood of purification,—no hallowed thing, shall she touch, and, into the sanctuary, shall she not enter, until her days of purification are fulfilled.
text.KJV: 12:4 And she shal then continue in the blood of her purifying three and thirtie dayes: Shee shall touch no hallowed thing, nor come into the Sanctuary, vntill the dayes of her purifying be fulfilled.
text.NABRE: 12:4 and then she shall spend thirty-three days more in a state of blood purity; she shall not touch anything sacred nor enter the sanctuary till the days of her purification are fulfilled.

text.NRSV: If she bears a female child, she shall be unclean two weeks, as in her menstruation; her time of blood purification shall be sixty-six days.
text.Rotherham: But if, a female child, she bear, then shall she be unclean two weeks as in her removal,—and, for sixty-six days, shall she continue in the blood of purification.
text.KJV: 12:5 But if she beare a maid child, then she shalbe vncleane two weekes, as in her separation: and she shall continue in the blood of her purifying threescore and sixe dayes.
text.NABRE: 12:5 If she gives birth to a girl, for fourteen days she shall be as unclean as during her menstrual period, after which she shall spend sixty-six days in a state of blood purity.

text.NRSV: When the days of her purification are completed, whether for a son or for a daughter, she shall bring to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting a lamb in its first year for a burnt offering, and a pigeon or a turtledove for a sin offering.
text.Rotherham: And, when the days of her purification are fulfilled, whether for a son or for a daughter, she shall bring in a lamb, the choice of its year, as an ascending-sacrifice, and a young pigeon or a turtle-dove, as a sin-bearer, unto the entrance of the tent of meeting, unto the priest;
text.KJV: 12:6 And when the dayes of her purifying are fulfilled, for a sonne, or for a daughter, she shall bring a lambe of the first yeere for a burnt offring, & a yong pigeon, or a turtle doue for a sinne offering, vnto the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation, vnto the Priest:
text.NABRE: 12:6 When the days of her purification for a son or for a daughter are fulfilled, she shall bring to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting a yearling lamb for a burnt offering and a pigeon or a turtledove for a purification offering.

text.NRSV: He shall offer it before the Lord, and make atonement on her behalf; then she shall be clean from her flow of blood. This is the law for her who bears a child, male or female.
text.Rotherham: and he shall bring it near before Yahweh so shall the priest put a propitiatory-covering over her, and she shall be clean from her fountain of blood. This, is the law for her that hath given birth, to a male child or to a female.
text.KJV: 12:7 Who shall offer it before the Lord, and make an atonement for her, and she shall be cleansed from the issue of her blood. This is the law for her that hath borne a male or a female.
text.NABRE: 12:7 The priest shall offer them before the LORD to make atonement for her, and thus she will be clean again after her flow of blood. Such is the ritual for the woman who gives birth to a child, male or female.

text.NRSV: If she cannot afford a sheep, she shall take two turtledoves or two pigeons, one for a burnt offering and the other for a sin offering; and the priest shall make atonement on her behalf, and she shall be clean.
text.Rotherham: But, if her hand findeth not sufficiency for a lamb, then shall she take two turtle doves or two young pigeons, one for an ascending-sacrifice and one for a sin-bearer,—so shall the priest put a propitiatory-covering over her, and she shall be clean.
text.KJV: 12:8 And if she be not able to bring a lambe, then she shall bring two turtles, or two yong pigeons, the one for the burnt offering, and the other for a sinne offering: and the Priest shall make an atonement for her, and shee shall bee cleane.
text.NABRE: 12:8 If, however, she cannot afford a lamb, she may take two turtledoves or two pigeons, the one for a burnt offering and the other for a purification offering. The priest shall make atonement for her, and thus she will again be clean.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying—
text.KJV: 13:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses and Aaron, saying,
text.NABRE: 13:1 Chapter 13 - Scaly Infection. The LORD said to Moses and Aaron:

text.NRSV: When a person has on the skin of his body a swelling or an eruption or a spot, and it turns into a leprous disease on the skin of his body, he shall be brought to Aaron the priest or to one of his sons the priests.
text.Rotherham: When, any man, shall have—in the skin of his flesh—a rising, or a scab, or a bright spot, and it shall become in the skin of his flesh the plague-spot of leprosy, then shall he be brought in unto Aaron the priest, or unto one of his sons the priests;
text.KJV: 13:2 When a man shall haue in the skinne of his flesh, a rising, a scabbe, or bright spot, and it bee in the skinne of his flesh like the plague of leprosie, then he shall bee brought vnto Aaron the Priest, or vnto one of his sonnes the Priests.
text.NABRE: 13:2 When someone has on the skin a mark, lesion, or blotch which appears to develop into a scaly infection, the person shall be brought to Aaron, the priest, or to one of the priests among his sons.

text.NRSV: The priest shall examine the disease on the skin of his body, and if the hair in the diseased area has turned white and the disease appears to be deeper than the skin of his body, it is a leprous disease; after the priest has examined him he shall pronounce him ceremonially unclean.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall view the spot in the skin of his flesh—if, the hair in the plague, have turned white and the appearance of the spot be deeper than the skin of his flesh, the plague-spot of leprosy, it is,—so the priest shall view him and pronounce him unclean.
text.KJV: 13:3 And the Priest shall looke on the plague in the skinne of the flesh: and when the haire in the plague is turned white, and the plague in sight be deeper then the skin of his flesh, it is a plague of leprosie: and the Priest shall looke on him, and pronounce him vncleane.
text.NABRE: 13:3 If the priest, upon examination of the skin’s infection, finds that the hair on the infection has turned white and the infection itself appears to be deeper than the skin, it is indeed a scaly infection; the priest, on seeing this, shall declare the person unclean.

text.NRSV: But if the spot is white in the skin of his body, and appears no deeper than the skin, and the hair in it has not turned white, the priest shall confine the diseased person for seven days.
text.Rotherham: But, if the bright spot, though white in the skin of his flesh, is not deeper in appearance than the skin, and, the hair, hath not turned white, then shall the priest shut up the plagued one, seven days.
text.KJV: 13:4 If the bright spot be white in the skinne of his flesh, and in sight bee not deeper then the skinne, and the haire thereof be not turned white, then the Priest shall shut vp him that hath the plague, seuen dayes.
text.NABRE: 13:4 If, however, the blotch on the skin is white, but does not seem to be deeper than the skin, nor has the hair turned white, the priest shall quarantine the afflicted person for seven days.

text.NRSV: The priest shall examine him on the seventh day, and if in his eyes the disease is checked and the disease has not spread in the skin, the priest shall confine him seven days more.
text.Rotherham: And the priest shall view him on the seventh day, and lo! if the spot hath stayed to his sight, and the spot hath not spread in the skin, then shall the priest shut him up seven days more,
text.KJV: 13:5 And the Priest shall looke on him the seuenth day: and beholde, if the plague in his sight be at a stay, and the plague spread not in the skinne, then the Priest shall shut him vp seuen dayes more.
text.NABRE: 13:5 Should the priest, upon examination on the seventh day, find that the infection has remained unchanged in color and has not spread on the skin, the priest shall quarantine the person for another seven days.

text.NRSV: The priest shall examine him again on the seventh day, and if the disease has abated and the disease has not spread in the skin, the priest shall pronounce him clean; it is only an eruption; and he shall wash his clothes, and be clean.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest view him on the seventh day, a second time, and lo! if the spot is, faint, and the spot hath not spread in the skin, then shall the priest pronounce him clean—it is, a scab, and he shall wash his clothes, and be clean.
text.KJV: 13:6 And the Priest shall looke on him againe the seuenth day: and beholde, if the plague be somewhat darke, and the plague spread not in the skin; the Priest shall pronounce him cleane: it is but a scab: and he shall wash his clothes, and be cleane.
text.NABRE: 13:6 Should the priest, upon examination again on the seventh day, find that the infection is now faded and has not spread on the skin, the priest shall declare the person clean; it was merely a scab. The person shall wash his garments and so become clean.

text.NRSV: But if the eruption spreads in the skin after he has shown himself to the priest for his cleansing, he shall appear again before the priest.
text.Rotherham: But if the scab shall have, verily spread, in the skin, since he was shown to the priest that he might be cleansed, then shall he shew himself again unto the priest;
text.KJV: 13:7 But if the scab spread much abroad in the skinne after that hee hath beene seene of the Priest, for his cleansing hee shall be seene of the Priest againe.
text.NABRE: 13:7 But if, after the person was examined by the priest and declared clean, the scab spreads at all on the skin, the person shall once more be examined by the priest.

text.NRSV: The priest shall make an examination, and if the eruption has spread in the skin, the priest shall pronounce him unclean; it is a leprous disease.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall take a view, and lo! if the scab hath spread in the skin, then shall the priest pronounce him unclean—leprosy, it is.
text.KJV: 13:8 And if the Priest see, that behold, the scab spreadeth in the skin, then the Priest shall pronounce him vncleane: it is a leprosie.
text.NABRE: 13:8 Should the priest, upon examination, find that the scab has indeed spread on the skin, he shall declare the person unclean; it is a scaly infection.

text.NRSV: When a person has a leprous disease, he shall be brought to the priest.
text.Rotherham: When, the plague of leprosy, cometh to be in any human being, then shall he be brought in unto the priest;
text.KJV: 13:9 ¶ When the plague of leprosie is in a man, then he shall be brought vnto the Priest;
text.NABRE: 13:9 When someone is afflicted with a scaly infection, that person shall be brought to the priest.

text.NRSV: The priest shall make an examination, and if there is a white swelling in the skin that has turned the hair white, and there is quick raw flesh in the swelling,
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall take a view, and lo! if there is a white rising in the skin and, the same, hath turned the hair white,—and there be a wound of raw flesh in the rising,
text.KJV: 13:10 And the Priest shall see him: and behold, if the rising be white in the skin, and it haue turned the haire white, and there be quicke raw flesh in the rising:
text.NABRE: 13:10 Should the priest, upon examination, find that there is a white mark on the skin which has turned the hair white and that there is raw flesh in it,

text.NRSV: it is a chronic leprous disease in the skin of his body. The priest shall pronounce him unclean; he shall not confine him, for he is unclean.
text.Rotherham: an old leprosy, it is in the skin of his flesh, and the priest shall pronounce him unclean,—he shall not shut him up, for unclean, he is.
text.KJV: 13:11 It is an old leprosie in the skinne of his flesh, and the Priest shall pronounce him vncleane, and shal not shut him vp: for he is vncleane.
text.NABRE: 13:11 it is a chronic scaly infection on the skin. The priest shall declare the person unclean without quarantine, since the individual is certainly unclean.

text.NRSV: But if the disease breaks out in the skin, so that it covers all the skin of the diseased person from head to foot, so far as the priest can see,
text.Rotherham: But, if the leprosy, cometh quite out, in the skin, and the leprosy covereth all the skin of him that, is plagued, from his head even unto his feet,—so far as appeareth to the eyes of the priest,
text.KJV: 13:12 And if a leprosie breake out abroad in the skin, and the leprosie couer all the skin of him that hath the plague, from his head euen to his foot, wheresoeuer the Priest looketh:
text.NABRE: 13:12 If the scaly infection breaks out on the skin and, as far as the priest can see, covers all the skin of the afflicted person from head to foot,

text.NRSV: the priest shall make an examination, and if the disease has covered all his body, he shall pronounce him clean of the disease; since it has all turned white, he is clean.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest takes view and lo! if the leprosy hath covered all his flesh, then shall he pronounce clean him that was plagued,—all of it, hath turned white, clean, he is.
text.KJV: 13:13 Then the Priest shall consider: and behold, if the leprosie haue couered al his flesh, he shal pronounce him cleane that hath the plague, it is all turned white; he is cleane.
text.NABRE: 13:13 should the priest then, upon examination, find that the scaly infection does cover the whole body, he shall declare the afflicted person clean; since the person has turned completely white; that individual is clean.

text.NRSV: But if raw flesh ever appears on him, he shall be unclean;
text.Rotherham: But, the very day there appeareth in him raw flesh, he shall be unclean;
text.KJV: 13:14 But when raw flesh appeareth in him, he shall be vncleane.
text.NABRE: 13:14 But as soon as raw flesh appears, the individual is unclean;

text.NRSV: the priest shall examine the raw flesh and pronounce him unclean. Raw flesh is unclean, for it is a leprous disease.
text.Rotherham: so then the priest shall view the raw flesh, and pronounce him unclean,—as for the raw flesh, unclean, it is, leprosy, it is.
text.KJV: 13:15 And the Priest shall see the raw flesh, and pronounce him to bee vncleane: for the raw flesh is vncleane: it is a leprosie.
text.NABRE: 13:15 on observing the raw flesh, the priest shall declare the person unclean, because raw flesh is unclean; it is a scaly infection.

text.NRSV: But if the raw flesh again turns white, he shall come to the priest;
text.Rotherham: Or, if the raw flesh turn again and be changed to white, then shall he come in unto the priest;
text.KJV: 13:16 Or if the raw flesh turne againe, and bee changed vnto white, hee shall come vnto the Priest:
text.NABRE: 13:16 If, however, the raw flesh again turns white, the person shall return to the priest;

text.NRSV: the priest shall examine him, and if the disease has turned white, the priest shall pronounce the diseased person clean. He is clean.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall view him, and lo! if the spot hath changed to white, then shall the priest pronounce clean him that was plagued—clean, he is.
text.KJV: 13:17 And the Priest shall see him: and beholde, if the plague bee turned into white, then the Priest shall pronounce him cleane that hath the plague; hee is cleane.
text.NABRE: 13:17 should the latter, upon examination, find that the infection has indeed turned white, he shall declare the afflicted person clean; the individual is clean.

text.NRSV: When a boil appears on the skin of one’s body and it heals,
text.Rotherham: And, when, any one’s flesh, hath, in the skin thereof, a boil,—and then it is healed;
text.KJV: 13:18 ¶ The flesh also, in which, euen in the skinne thereof was a bile, and is healed,
text.NABRE: 13:18 If a boil appeared on a person’s skin which later healed,

text.NRSV: and in the place of the boil there appears a white swelling or a reddish-white spot, it shall be shown to the priest.
text.Rotherham: but in the place of the boil, is a white rising, or a bright spot, reddish white, then shall it be shown unto the priest.
text.KJV: 13:19 And in the place of the bile there be a white rising, or a bright spot white, and somewhat reddish, and it be shewed to the Priest:
text.NABRE: 13:19 should now in the place of the boil a white mark or a reddish white blotch develop, the person shall be examined by the priest.

text.NRSV: The priest shall make an examination, and if it appears deeper than the skin and its hair has turned white, the priest shall pronounce him unclean; this is a leprous disease, broken out in the boil.
text.Rotherham: And the priest shall take a view and lo! if the appearance thereof, is lower than the skin, and, the hair thereof, hath turned white, then shall the priest pronounce him unclean—the plague-spot of leprosy, it, is, in the boil, broken out.
text.KJV: 13:20 And if when the Priest seeth it, behold, it be in sight lower then the skinne, and the haire thereof be turned white, the Priest shall pronounce him vncleane: it is a plague of leprosie broken out of the bile.
text.NABRE: 13:20 If the latter, upon examination, finds that it is deeper than the skin and that the hair has turned white, he shall declare the person unclean; it is a scaly infection that has broken out in the boil.

text.NRSV: But if the priest examines it and the hair on it is not white, nor is it deeper than the skin but has abated, the priest shall confine him seven days.
text.Rotherham: But, if the priest shall view it and lo! there is no white hair therein, and it is not deeper than the skin, and, in itself, is faint, then shall the priest shut him up seven days;
text.KJV: 13:21 But if the Priest looke on it, and behold, there be no white haires therein, and if it be not lower then the skin, but be somewhat darke; then the Priest shall shut him vp seuen dayes.
text.NABRE: 13:21 But if the priest, upon examination, finds that there is no white hair in it and that it is not deeper than the skin and is faded, the priest shall quarantine the person for seven days.

text.NRSV: If it spreads in the skin, the priest shall pronounce him unclean; it is diseased.
text.Rotherham: and, if it, clearly spreadeth, in the skin, then shall the priest pronounce him unclean—a plague-spot, it is.
text.KJV: 13:22 And if it spread much abroad in the skinne, then the Priest shall pronounce him vncleane; it is a plague.
text.NABRE: 13:22 If it has then spread on the skin, the priest shall declare the person unclean; it is an infection.

text.NRSV: But if the spot remains in one place and does not spread, it is the scar of the boil; the priest shall pronounce him clean.
text.Rotherham: But if in its place the bright spot stayeth hath not spread, a boil, it is,—and the priest shall pronounce him clean.
text.KJV: 13:23 But if the bright spot stay in his place, and spread not, it is a burning bile; and the Priest shall pronounce him cleane.
text.NABRE: 13:23 But if the blotch remains the same without spreading, it is merely the scar of the boil; the priest shall therefore declare the person clean.

text.NRSV: Or, when the body has a burn on the skin and the raw flesh of the burn becomes a spot, reddish-white or white,
text.Rotherham: Or, when, any one’s flesh, hath in the skin thereof a fiery burning,—and the burning wound becometh a bright spot reddish white, or white,
text.KJV: 13:24 ¶ Or if there be any flesh in the skin whereof there is a hot burning, and the quicke flesh that burneth haue a white bright spot, somewhat reddish, or white;
text.NABRE: 13:24 If there was a burn on a person’s skin, and the burned area now becomes a reddish white or a white blotch,

text.NRSV: the priest shall examine it. If the hair in the spot has turned white and it appears deeper than the skin, it is a leprous disease; it has broken out in the burn, and the priest shall pronounce him unclean. This is a leprous disease.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest view it—and lo! if the hair is turned white in the bright spot and the appearance thereof is deeper than the skin, leprosy, it is, broken out, in the burning,—so the priest shall pronounce him unclean, the plague-spot of leprosy, it is.
text.KJV: 13:25 Then the Priest shall looke vpon it: and behold, if the haire in the bright spot be turned white, and it bee in sight deeper then the skinne, it is a leprosie broken out of the burning: wherefore the Priest shal pronounce him vncleane: it is the plague of leprosie.
text.NABRE: 13:25 when the priest, upon examination, finds that the hair has turned white in the blotch and this seems to be deeper than the skin, it is a scaly infection that has broken out in the burn; the priest shall therefore declare the person unclean; it is a scaly infection.

text.NRSV: But if the priest examines it and the hair on the spot is not white, and it is no deeper than the skin but has abated, the priest shall confine him seven days.
text.Rotherham: But, if the priest shall view it and lo! there is not, in the bright spot white hair, and it is not deeper than the skin but, itself, is faint, then shall the priest shut him up seven days;
text.KJV: 13:26 But if the Priest looke on it, and behold, there be no white haire in the bright spot, and it be no lower then the other skin, but be somewhat darke, then the Priest shal shut him vp seuen dayes.
text.NABRE: 13:26 But if the priest, upon examination, finds that there is no white hair in the blotch and that this is not deeper than the skin and is faded, the priest shall quarantine the person for seven days.

text.NRSV: The priest shall examine him the seventh day; if it is spreading in the skin, the priest shall pronounce him unclean. This is a leprous disease.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall view him on the seventh day,—if it, hath plainly spread in the skin, then shall the priest pronounce him unclean, the plague-spot of leprosy, it is.
text.KJV: 13:27 And the Priest shall looke vpon him the seuenth day: and if it be spread much abroad in the skin, then the Priest shall pronounce him vncleane; it is the plague of leprosie.
text.NABRE: 13:27 Should the priest, upon examination on the seventh day, find that it has spread at all on the skin, he shall declare the person unclean; it is a scaly infection.

text.NRSV: But if the spot remains in one place and does not spread in the skin but has abated, it is a swelling from the burn, and the priest shall pronounce him clean; for it is the scar of the burn.
text.Rotherham: But, if, in its place the bright spot hath stayed, and hath not spread in the skin, but, itself, is faint, the rising of a burning, it is,—and the priest shall pronounce him clean, for only the inflaming of the burning, it is.
text.KJV: 13:28 And if the bright spot stay in his place, and spread not in the skin, but it be somewhat darke; it is a rising of the burning, and the Priest shall pronounce him cleane: for it is an inflammation of the burning.
text.NABRE: 13:28 But if the blotch remains the same without spreading on the skin and is faded, it is merely the spot of the burn; the priest shall therefore declare the person clean, since it is only the scar of the burn.

text.NRSV: When a man or woman has a disease on the head or in the beard,
text.Rotherham: And, when there cometh to be in, any man or woman, a spot,—in the head or in the beard,
text.KJV: 13:29 ¶ If a man or woman hath a plague vpon the head or the beard,
text.NABRE: 13:29 When a man or a woman has an infection on the head or in the beard,

text.NRSV: the priest shall examine the disease. If it appears deeper than the skin and the hair in it is yellow and thin, the priest shall pronounce him unclean; it is a scall, a leprous disease of the head or the beard.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest view the spot and lo! if, the appearance thereof, is deeper than the skin, and, therein, is yellow, thin hair, then shall the priest pronounce him unclean a scall, it is, a leprosy of the head or of the beard, it is.
text.KJV: 13:30 Then the Priest shall see the plague: and behold, if it be in sight deeper then the skin, and there be in it a yellow thin haire, then the Priest shall pronounce him vncleane, it is a dry skall, euen a leprosie vpon the head or beard.
text.NABRE: 13:30 should the priest, upon examination, find that the infection appears to be deeper than the skin and that there is fine yellow hair in it, the priest shall declare the person unclean; it is a scall. It is a scaly infection of the head or beard.

text.NRSV: But if the priest examines the scaly disease, and it does not appear deeper than the skin and there is no black hair in it, the priest shall confine the person with the scaly disease for seven days.
text.Rotherham: But when the priest vieweth the spot, and lo! there is, no appearance, of it deeper than the skin, and, no dark hair, is therein, then shall the priest shut up him that hath the plague-spot of scall, seven days;
text.KJV: 13:31 And if the Priest looke on the plague of the skall, and behold, it be not in sight deeper then the skin, and that there is no blacke haire in it; then the Priest shall shut vp him that hath the plague of the skall, seuen dayes.
text.NABRE: 13:31 But if the priest, upon examining the scall infection, finds that it does not appear to be deeper than the skin, though the hair in it may not be black, the priest shall quarantine the scall-stricken person for seven days.

text.NRSV: On the seventh day the priest shall examine the itch; if the scall has not spread, and there is no yellow hair in it, and the scall does not appear deeper than the skin,
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall view the spot on the seventh day, and lo! if the scall hath not spread, and there hath not come to be therein yellow hair,—and, the appearance of the scall, is not deeper than the skin,
text.KJV: 13:32 And in the seuenth day the Priest shall looke on the plague: and behold, if the skall spread not, and there be in it no yellow haire, and the skall be not in sight deeper then the skin;
text.NABRE: 13:32 Should the priest, upon examining the infection on the seventh day find that the scall has not spread and has no yellow hair in it and does not seem to be deeper than the skin,

text.NRSV: he shall shave, but the scall he shall not shave. The priest shall confine the person with the scall for seven days more.
text.Rotherham: then shall he shave himself, but the scall, shall he not shave, and the priest shall shut up him who hath the scall seven days, more;
text.KJV: 13:33 He shall be shauen, but the skall shall he not shaue: and the Priest shall shut vp him that hath the skall, seuen dayes more.
text.NABRE: 13:33 the person shall shave, but not the scall spot. Then the priest shall quarantine the scall-diseased person for another seven days.

text.NRSV: On the seventh day the priest shall examine the scall; if the scall has not spread in the skin and it appears no deeper than the skin, the priest shall pronounce him clean; he shall wash his clothes, and be clean.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest view the scall, on the seventh day, and lo! if the scall hath not spread in the skin, and, the appearance thereof, is not deeper than the skin, then shall the priest pronounce him clean, and he shall wash his clothes, and be clean.
text.KJV: 13:34 And in the seuenth day the Priest shall looke on the skall: and behold, if the skall be not spread in the skin, nor be in sight deeper then the skin, then the Priest shall pronounce him cleane: and he shall wash his clothes, and be cleane.
text.NABRE: 13:34 If the priest, upon examining the scall on the seventh day, finds that it has not spread on the skin and that it does not appear to be deeper than the skin, he shall declare the person clean; the latter shall wash his garments, and will thus be clean.

text.NRSV: But if the scall spreads in the skin after he has been pronounced clean,
text.Rotherham: But, if the scall do indeed spread in the skin,—after he hath been pronounced clean,
text.KJV: 13:35 But if the skall spread much in the skinne after his cleansing,
text.NABRE: 13:35 But if the scall spreads at all on the skin after the person has been declared clean—

text.NRSV: the priest shall examine him. If the scall has spread in the skin, the priest need not seek for yellow hair; he is unclean.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest take a view, and lo! if the scall hath spread in the skin, the priest shall not search for the yellow hair—unclean, he is.
text.KJV: 13:36 Then the Priest shall looke on him, and behold, if the skall be spread in the skinne, the Priest shall not seeke for yellow haire: he is vncleane.
text.NABRE: 13:36 should the priest, upon examination, find that the scall has indeed spread on the skin, he need not look for yellow hair; the individual is unclean.

text.NRSV: If in his eyes the scall is unchanged and black hair has grown in it, the scall is healed; he is clean; and the priest shall pronounce him clean.
text.Rotherham: But, if in his eyes, the scall is at a stay, and dark hair hath grown up therein, the scall is healed, clean, he is,—and the priest shall pronounce him clean.
text.KJV: 13:37 But if the skall be in his sight at a stay, and that there is blacke haire growen vp therein: the skall is healed, he is cleane, and the Priest shall pronounce him cleane.
text.NABRE: 13:37 If, however, the scall has remained unchanged in color and black hair has grown in it, the disease has been healed; the person is clean, and the priest shall declare the individual clean.

text.NRSV: When a man or a woman has spots on the skin of the body, white spots,
text.Rotherham: And when either, man or woman, hath in the skin of their flesh bright spots,—bright spots that are white,
text.KJV: 13:38 ¶ If a man also or a woman haue in the skinne of their flesh bright spots, euen white bright spots,
text.NABRE: 13:38 When the skin of a man or a woman is spotted with several white blotches,

text.NRSV: the priest shall make an examination, and if the spots on the skin of the body are of a dull white, it is a rash that has broken out on the skin; he is clean.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest take a view and lo! if, in the skin of their flesh, are bright spots that are dull white, dead white spot, it is, that hath broken through in the skin—clean, he is.
text.KJV: 13:39 Then the Priest shall looke: and behold, if the bright spots in the skinne of their flesh bee darkish white, it is a freckled spot that groweth in the skin: he is cleane.
text.NABRE: 13:39 if the priest, upon examination, finds that the blotches on the skin are pale white, it is only tetter that has broken out on the skin, and the person therefore is clean.

text.NRSV: If anyone loses the hair from his head, he is bald but he is clean.
text.Rotherham: And, when, any man’s, head loseth its hair, though, bald, he is, clean.
text.KJV: 13:40 And the man whose haire is fallen off his head, he is bald: yet is hee cleane.
text.NABRE: 13:40 When a man loses the hair of his head, he is simply bald on the crown and not unclean.

text.NRSV: If he loses the hair from his forehead and temples, he has baldness of the forehead but he is clean.
text.Rotherham: And if, in front, his head loseth its hair, though bald in the forehead, he is, clean.
text.KJV: 13:41 And he that hath his haire fallen off from the part of his head toward his face, he is forehead-bald: yet is hee cleane.
text.NABRE: 13:41 So too, if he loses the hair on the front of his head, he is simply bald on the forehead and not unclean.

text.NRSV: But if there is on the bald head or the bald forehead a reddish-white diseased spot, it is a leprous disease breaking out on his bald head or his bald forehead.
text.Rotherham: But, should there be, in the baldness behind, or in the baldness in front, a spot that is reddish white, leprosy broken out, it is, in his baldness behind, or in his baldness in front.
text.KJV: 13:42 And if there be in the bald head, or bald forehead a white reddish sore, it is a leprosie sprung vp in his bald-head, or his bald forehead.
text.NABRE: 13:42 But when there is a reddish white infection on his bald crown or bald forehead, it is a scaly infection that is breaking out there.

text.NRSV: The priest shall examine him; if the diseased swelling is reddish-white on his bald head or on his bald forehead, which resembles a leprous disease in the skin of the body,
text.Rotherham: So the priest shall view it, and lo! if, the rising-spot, be reddish white in his baldness behind or in his baldness in front,—like the appearance of leprosy in the skin of the flesh,
text.KJV: 13:43 Then the Priest shall looke vpon it: and beholde, if the rising of the sore bee white reddish in his balde head, or in his bald forehead, as the leprosie appeareth in the skinne of the flesh,
text.NABRE: 13:43 If the priest, upon examination, finds that the infection spot on the bald area on the crown or forehead has the same reddish white appearance as that of a scaly infection of the skin,

text.NRSV: he is leprous, he is unclean. The priest shall pronounce him unclean; the disease is on his head.
text.Rotherham: a leprous man, is he, unclean, he is,—unclean, shall the priest pronounce him in his head, is his plague.
text.KJV: 13:44 Hee is a leprous man, he is vncleane: the Priest shall pronounce him vtterly vncleane, his plague is in his head.
text.NABRE: 13:44 the man has a scaly infection and is unclean. The priest shall declare him unclean; his infection is on his head.

text.NRSV: The person who has the leprous disease shall wear torn clothes and let the hair of his head be disheveled; and he shall cover his upper lip and cry out, “Unclean, unclean.”
text.Rotherham: Now, as for the leper in whom is the plague, His clothes, shall be rent, And, his head, shall be bare, And, his beard, shall he cover,—And, Unclean! Unclean! shall he cry.
text.KJV: 13:45 And the leper in whom the plague is, his clothes shall be rent, and his head bare, and he shall put a couering vpon his vpper lip, and shall cry, Uncleane, vncleane.
text.NABRE: 13:45 The garments of one afflicted with a scaly infection shall be rent and the hair disheveled, and the mustache covered. The individual shall cry out, “Unclean, unclean!”

text.NRSV: He shall remain unclean as long as he has the disease; he is unclean. He shall live alone; his dwelling shall be outside the camp.
text.Rotherham: All the days that the plague is in him, shall he continue unclean, Unclean, he is,—Alone, shall he remain, Outside the camp, shall be his dwelling.
text.KJV: 13:46 All the dayes wherein the plague shall bee in him, he shall bee defiled, hee is vncleane: he shall dwell alone, without the campe shall his habitation be.
text.NABRE: 13:46 As long as the infection is present, the person shall be unclean. Being unclean, that individual shall dwell apart, taking up residence outside the camp.

text.NRSV: When a garment has a mark of leprosy in it, whether it is a woolen or a linen garment,
text.Rotherham: And, when, in a garment, there is a plague-spot of leprosy,—whether in a garment of wool, or a garment of flax;
text.KJV: 13:47 ¶ The garment also, that the plague of leprosie is in, whether it bee a woollen garment, or a linnen garment,
text.NABRE: 13:47 Fungal Infection of Fabrics and Leather. When a fungal infection is on a garment of wool or of linen,

text.NRSV: in warp or woof of linen or wool, or in skin or in anything made of skin,
text.Rotherham: either in warp or in weft, made with flax, or with wool,—or in a skin, or in anything wrought of skin;
text.KJV: 13:48 Whether it bee in the warpe, or woofe of linnen or of woollen, whether in a skin, or in any thing made of skinne:
text.NABRE: 13:48 or on the warp and woof of linen or wool, or on a hide or anything made of leather,

text.NRSV: if the disease shows greenish or reddish in the garment, whether in warp or woof or in skin or in anything made of skin, it is a leprous disease and shall be shown to the priest.
text.Rotherham: and the spot cometh to be of a greenish yellow or reddish, in the garment or in the skin, whether in warp or in weft, or in any utensil of skin, the plague-spot of leprosy, it is,—and shall be shown unto the priest;
text.KJV: 13:49 And if the plague be greenish or reddish in the garment, or in the skin, either in the warpe, or in the woofe, or in any thing of skinne, it is a plague of leprosie, and shall be shewed vnto the Priest.
text.NABRE: 13:49 if the infection on the garment or hide, or on the warp or woof, or on any leather article is greenish or reddish, the thing is indeed a fungal infection and must be examined by the priest.

text.NRSV: The priest shall examine the disease, and isolate the disease seven days.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall view the spot,—and shall shut up him that is plagued seven days;
text.KJV: 13:50 And the Priest shall looke vpon the plague, and shut vp it that hath the plague, seuen dayes.
text.NABRE: 13:50 Having examined the infection, the priest shall quarantine the infected article for seven days.

text.NRSV: If the disease has spread in the garment, in warp or woof, or in the skin, whatever be the use of the skin, this is a spreading leprous disease; it is unclean.
text.Rotherham: then shall he view the spot, on the seventh day if the spot hath spread in the garment whether in warp or in weft, or in the skin, or anything which may be made of skin for service, the spot is a fretting leprosy, unclean, it is.
text.KJV: 13:51 And he shall looke on the plague on the seuenth day: if the plague be spread in the garment, either in the warpe, or in the woofe, or in a skin, or in any worke that is made of skinne, the plague is a fretting leprosie; it is vncleane.
text.NABRE: 13:51 If the priest, upon inspecting the infection on the seventh day, finds that it has spread on the garment, or on the warp or woof, or on the leather, whatever be its use, the infection is a harmful fungus; the article is unclean.

text.NRSV: He shall burn the garment, whether diseased in warp or woof, woolen or linen, or anything of skin, for it is a spreading leprous disease; it shall be burned in fire.
text.Rotherham: Then shall he burn up the garment whether it be in the warp or the weft in wool or in flax, or any utensil of skin, wherein shall be the plague-spot,—for, a fretting leprosy, it is, in fire, shall it be burnt up.
text.KJV: 13:52 Hee shall therefore burne that garment, whether warpe or woofe, in wollen or in linnen, or any thing of skinne, wherein the plague is: for it is a fretting leprosie; it shall bee burnt in the fire.
text.NABRE: 13:52 He shall therefore burn up the garment, or the warp or woof, be it of wool or linen, or any leather article which is infected; since it is a harmful fungus, it must be destroyed by fire.

text.NRSV: If the priest makes an examination, and the disease has not spread in the garment, in warp or woof or in anything of skin,
text.Rotherham: But if the priest shall take a view, and lo! the spot has not spread, in the garment, either in warp or in weft,—or in any utensil of skin,
text.KJV: 13:53 And if the Priest shall looke, and behold the plague be not spread in the garment, either in the warpe, or in the woofe, or in any thing of skinne;
text.NABRE: 13:53 But if the priest, upon examination, finds that it has not spread on the garment, or on the warp or woof, or on the leather article,

text.NRSV: the priest shall command that the diseased article be washed, and he shall isolate it seven days more.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest give command, and they shall wash that wherein is the spot,—and he shall shut it up seven days more;
text.KJV: 13:54 Then the Priest shall command that they wash the thing wherein the plague is, and he shall shut it vp seuen dayes more.
text.NABRE: 13:54 he shall give orders to have the infected article washed and then quarantined for another seven days.

text.NRSV: Then the priest shall examine the diseased article after it has been washed. If the spot has not changed color, though the disease has not spread, it is unclean; you shall burn it in fire, whether the leprous spot is on the inside or on the outside.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest take a view after the plagued garment hath been washed and lo! if the spot hath not changed its look, then though the spot hath not spread, yet unclean, it is, in the fire, shalt thou burn it up,—a sunken spot, it is, in the back thereof, or in the front thereof.
text.KJV: 13:55 And the Priest shall looke on the plague after that it is washed: and behold, if the plague haue not changed his colour, and the plague be not spread, it is vncleane, thou shalt burne it in the fire, it is fret inward, whether it be bare within or without.
text.NABRE: 13:55 If the priest, upon examination after the infection was washed, finds that it has not changed its color, even though it may not have spread, the article is unclean. You shall burn it with fire. It is a fray, be it on its inner or outer side.

text.NRSV: If the priest makes an examination, and the disease has abated after it is washed, he shall tear the spot out of the garment, in skin, or in warp or woof.
