text.KJV: 16:25 And the fat of the sinne offering shall he burne vpon the Altar.
text.NABRE: 16:25 and also burn the fat of the purification offering on the altar.

text.NRSV: The one who sets the goat free for Azazel shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water, and afterward may come into the camp.
text.Rotherham: And he that sent away the goat unto Azazel, shall wash—his clothes, and bathe his flesh in water,—and, after that, shall he come into the camp.
text.KJV: 16:26 And he that let goe the goat for the Scape-goat, shal wash his clothes, and bathe his flesh in water, and afterward come into the Campe.
text.NABRE: 16:26 The man who led away the goat for Azazel shall wash his garments and bathe his body in water; only then may he enter the camp.

text.NRSV: The bull of the sin offering and the goat of the sin offering, whose blood was brought in to make atonement in the holy place, shall be taken outside the camp; their skin and their flesh and their dung shall be consumed in fire.
text.Rotherham: And the, sin-bearing bullock, and the sin-bearing goat whose blood was brought in to make a propitiatory-covering in the holy place, shall be carried forth outside the camp,—and they shall burn up, in fire, their skins and their flesh, and their dung;
text.KJV: 16:27 And the bullocke for the sinne offering, and the goat for the sin offering, whose blood was brought in, to make atonement in the holy place, shall one cary foorth without the Campe, and they shal burne in the fire their skinnes and their flesh, and their doung.
text.NABRE: 16:27 The bull and the goat of the purification offering whose blood was brought to make atonement in the inner sanctuary, shall be taken outside the camp, where their hides and flesh and dung shall be burned in the fire.

text.NRSV: And the one who burns them shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water, and afterward may come into the camp.
text.Rotherham: and he that hath burned them, shall wash his clothes, and bathe his flesh in water,—and, after that, shall he come into the camp.
text.KJV: 16:28 And he that burneth them, shall wash his clothes, and bathe his flesh in water, and afterward he shall come into the Campe.
text.NABRE: 16:28 The one who burns them shall wash his garments and bathe his body in water; only then may he enter the camp.

text.NRSV: This shall be a statute to you forever: In the seventh month, on the tenth day of the month, you shall deny yourselves, and shall do no work, neither the citizen nor the alien who resides among you.
text.Rotherham: And it shall become unto you, a statute age-abiding,—In the seventh month on the tenth of the month, Shall ye humble you souls And, no work, shall ye do, The home-born, Or the sojourner that sojourneth in your midst;
text.KJV: 16:29 ¶ And this shall be a statute for euer vnto you: that in the seuenth moneth, on the tenth day of the moneth, ye shall afflict your soules, & doe no worke at all, whether it bee one of your owne countrey, or a stranger that soiourneth among you.
text.NABRE: 16:29 The Fast. This shall be an everlasting statute for you: on the tenth day of the seventh month every one of you, whether a native or a resident alien, shall humble yourselves and shall do no work.

text.NRSV: For on this day atonement shall be made for you, to cleanse you; from all your sins you shall be clean before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: For on this day, shall a propitiatory-covering, be put over you, to cleanse you,—From all your sins before Yahweh, shall ye be clean.
text.KJV: 16:30 For on that day shal the Priest make an atonement for you, to cleanse you, that yee may bee cleane from all your sinnes before the Lord.
text.NABRE: 16:30 For on this day atonement is made for you to make you clean; of all your sins you will be cleansed before the LORD.

text.NRSV: It is a sabbath of complete rest to you, and you shall deny yourselves; it is a statute forever.
text.Rotherham: A sabbath of sacred rest, it is unto you, Therefore shall ye humble your souls, A statute age-abiding.
text.KJV: 16:31 It shall be a Sabbath of rest vnto you, and ye shall afflict your soules by a statute for euer.
text.NABRE: 16:31 It shall be a sabbath of complete rest for you, on which you must humble yourselves—an everlasting statute.

text.NRSV: The priest who is anointed and consecrated as priest in his father's place shall make atonement, wearing the linen vestments, the holy vestments.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall the priest who shall be anointed, and installed, to minister as priest in the stead of his father make propitiation,—So then he shall put on the linen garments the holy garments;
text.KJV: 16:32 And the Priest whom he shall anoynt, and whom he shall consecrate to minister in the Priests office in his fathers stead, shall make the atonement, and shal put on the linnen clothes, euen the holy garments.
text.NABRE: 16:32 This atonement is to be made by the priest who has been anointed and ordained to the priesthood in succession to his father. He shall wear the linen garments, the sacred vestments,

text.NRSV: He shall make atonement for the sanctuary, and he shall make atonement for the tent of meeting and for the altar, and he shall make atonement for the priests and for all the people of the assembly.
text.Rotherham: And make propitiation for the holy sanctuary, And for the tent of meeting, and for the altar, shall he make propitiation,—Over the priests also and over all the people of the convocation, shall he put a covering by propitiation.
text.KJV: 16:33 And he shall make an atonement for the holy Sanctuary, and hee shall make an atonement for the Tabernacle of the Congregation, and for the Altar: and he shall make an atonement for the Priests, and for all the people of the Congregation.
text.NABRE: 16:33 and purge the most sacred part of the sanctuary, as well as the tent of meeting, and the altar. He shall also make atonement for the priests and all the people of the assembly.

text.NRSV: This shall be an everlasting statute for you, to make atonement for the people of Israel once in the year for all their sins. And Moses did as the Lord had commanded him.
text.Rotherham: So shall this become unto you an age-abiding statute,—to put a propitiatory-covering over the sons of Israel because of all their sins, Once in the year. And he did, As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: 16:34 And this shall be an euerlasting statute vnto you, to make an atonement for the children of Israel, for all their sinnes once a yeere. And he did as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: 16:34 This, then, shall be an everlasting statute for you: once a year atonement shall be made on behalf of the Israelites for all their sins. And Moses did as the LORD had commanded him.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:—
text.KJV: 17:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 17:1 IV. Holiness Laws Chapter 17 - Sacredness of Blood. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to Aaron and his sons and to all the people of Israel and say to them: This is what the Lord has commanded.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron, and unto his sons and unto all the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—This, is the thing which Yahweh hath commanded, saying:
text.KJV: 17:2 Speake vnto Aaron and vnto his sonnes, and vnto all the children of Israel, and say vnto them; This is the thing which the Lord hath commanded, saying;
text.NABRE: 17:2 Speak to Aaron and his sons, as well as to all the Israelites, and tell them: This is what the LORD has commanded:

text.NRSV: If anyone of the house of Israel slaughters an ox or a lamb or a goat in the camp, or slaughters it outside the camp,
text.Rotherham: What man soever, there be of the house of Israel, who slayeth an ox or lamb or goat, in the camp,—or who slayeth it outside the camp;
text.KJV: 17:3 What man soeuer there bee of the house of Israel, that killeth an oxe, or lambe, or goat in the Campe, or that killeth it out of the Campe,
text.NABRE: 17:3 Any Israelite who slaughters an ox or a sheep or a goat, whether in the camp or outside of it,

text.NRSV: and does not bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting, to present it as an offering to the Lord before the tabernacle of the Lord, he shall be held guilty of bloodshed; he has shed blood, and he shall be cut off from the people.
text.Rotherham: and, unto the entrance of the tent of meeting, bringeth it not in, to present it as an oblation unto Yahweh, before the habitation of Yahweh, blood, shall be imputed to that man—blood, hath he shed, therefore shall that man be cut off from the midst of his people:
text.KJV: 17:4 And bringeth it not vnto the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation, to offer an offering vnto the Lord before the Tabernacle of the Lord, blood shall be imputed vnto that man; he hath shed blood, and that man shall be cut off from among his people:
text.NABRE: 17:4 without first bringing it to the entrance of the tent of meeting to present it as an offering to the LORD in front of the LORD’s tabernacle, shall be judged guilty of bloodshed—that individual has shed blood, and shall be cut off from the people.

text.NRSV: This is in order that the people of Israel may bring their sacrifices that they offer in the open field, that they may bring them to the Lord, to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and offer them as sacrifices of well-being to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: to the end that the sons of Israel may bring in their sacrifices which they are offering upon the face of the field, that they may bring them in unto Yahweh—unto the entrance of the tent of meeting, unto the priest,—and that so as peace-offerings unto Yahweh, they may offer them.
text.KJV: 17:5 To the end that the children of Israel may bring their sacrifices, which they offer in the open field, euen that they may bring them vnto the Lord, vnto the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation vnto the Priest, and offer them for peace offerings vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 17:5 This is so that such sacrifices as they used to offer in the open field the Israelites shall henceforth bring to the LORD at the entrance of the tent of meeting, to the priest, and sacrifice them there as communion sacrifices to the LORD.

text.NRSV: The priest shall dash the blood against the altar of the Lord at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and turn the fat into smoke as a pleasing odor to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest dash the blood against the altar of Yahweh, at the entrance of the tent of meeting,—and shall make a perfume of the fat, as a satisfying odour unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: 17:6 And the Priest shall sprinckle the blood vpon the Altar of the Lord, at the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation, and burne the fat for a sweet sauour vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 17:6 The priest will splash the blood on the altar of the LORD at the entrance of the tent of meeting and burn the fat for an odor pleasing to the LORD.

text.NRSV: so that they may no longer offer their sacrifices for goat-demons, to whom they prostitute themselves. This shall be a statute forever to them throughout their generations.
text.Rotherham: so shall they no more offer their sacrifices unto demons after whom they are unchastely going away,—a statute age-abiding, shall this be to them unto their generations.
text.KJV: 17:7 And they shall no more offer their sacrifices vnto deuils, after whom they haue gone a whoring: This shall be a statute for euer vnto them throughout their generations.
text.NABRE: 17:7 No longer shall they offer their sacrifices to the demons with whom they prostituted themselves. This shall be an everlasting statute for them and their descendants.

text.NRSV: And say to them further: Anyone of the house of Israel or of the aliens who reside among them who offers a burnt offering or sacrifice,
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, unto them, shalt thou say: What man soever, there may be of the house of Israel, or of the sojourners that sojourn in their midst,—who causeth to go up an ascending-offering, or a sacrifice;
text.KJV: 17:8 ¶ And thou shalt say vnto them, whatsoeuer man there be of the house of Israel, or of the strangers which soiourne among you, that offreth a burnt offering or sacrifice,
text.NABRE: 17:8 Tell them, therefore: Anyone, whether of the house of Israel or of the aliens residing among them, who offers a burnt offering or sacrifice

text.NRSV: and does not bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting, to sacrifice it to the Lord, shall be cut off from the people.
text.Rotherham: and unto the entrance of the tent of meeting, doth not bring it in, to offer it unto Yahweh, then shall that man be cut off from among his kinsfolk.
text.KJV: 17:9 And bringeth it not vnto the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation, to offer it vnto the Lord, euen that man shall be cut off from among his people.
text.NABRE: 17:9 without bringing it to the entrance of the tent of meeting to offer it to the LORD, shall be cut off from the people.

text.NRSV: If anyone of the house of Israel or of the aliens who reside among them eats any blood, I will set my face against that person who eats blood, and will cut that person off from the people.
text.Rotherham: And, what man soever, there may be of the house of Israel, or of the sojourners that sojourn in their midst, that partaketh of any manner of blood, then will I set my face against the person that partaketh of the blood, and will cut him off from the midst of his people.
text.KJV: 17:10 ¶ And whatsoeuer man there be of the house of Israel, or of the strangers that soiourne among you, that eateth any maner of blood, I will euen set my face against that soule that eateth blood, and will cut him off from among his people.
text.NABRE: 17:10 As for anyone, whether of the house of Israel or of the aliens residing among them, who consumes any blood, I will set myself against that individual and will cut that person off from among the people,

text.NRSV: For the life of the flesh is in the blood; and I have given it to you for making atonement for your lives on the altar; for, as life, it is the blood that makes atonement.
text.Rotherham: For as for the life of the flesh, in the blood, it is, therefore have, I, given it unto you upon the altar, to put a propitiatory-covering over your lives,—for the blood, it is, which, by virtue of the life, maketh propitiation.
text.KJV: 17:11 For the life of the flesh is in the blood, and I haue giuen it to you vpon the Altar, to make an atonement for your soules: for it is the blood, that maketh an atonement for the soule.
text.NABRE: 17:11 since the life of the flesh is in the blood, and I have given it to you to make atonement on the altar for yourselves, because it is the blood as life that makes atonement.

text.NRSV: Therefore I have said to the people of Israel: No person among you shall eat blood, nor shall any alien who resides among you eat blood.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, have I said unto the sons of Israel, Not a person from among you shall partake of blood,—Even the sojourner that sojourneth in your midst, shall not partake of blood.
text.KJV: 17:12 Therefore I said vnto the children of Israel, No soule of you shall eat blood, neither shall any stranger that soiourneth among you, eat blood.
text.NABRE: 17:12 That is why I have told the Israelites: No one among you, not even a resident alien, may consume blood.

text.NRSV: And anyone of the people of Israel, or of the aliens who reside among them, who hunts down an animal or bird that may be eaten shall pour out its blood and cover it with earth.
text.Rotherham: And, what man soever, there may be of the sons of Israel, or of the sojourners that sojourn in their midst, who taketh by hunting any wild-beast or bird that may be eaten, then shall he pour out the blood thereof, and cover it with dust;
text.KJV: 17:13 And whatsoeuer man there be of the children of Israel, or of the strangers that soiourne among you, which hunteth and catcheth any beast or foule that may be eaten, he shall euen powre out the blood thereof, and couer it with dust.
text.NABRE: 17:13 Anyone hunting, whether of the Israelites or of the aliens residing among them, who catches an animal or a bird that may be eaten, shall pour out its blood and cover it with earth,

text.NRSV: For the life of every creature—its blood is its life; therefore I have said to the people of Israel: You shall not eat the blood of any creature, for the life of every creature is its blood; whoever eats it shall be cut off.
text.Rotherham: for, as for the life of all flesh, the blood thereof, for the life thereof, standeth, therefore have I said unto the sons of Israel—Of the blood of no manner of flesh, shall ye partake, For, the life of all flesh is the blood thereof; whoso partaketh thereof, shall be cut off.
text.KJV: 17:14 For it is the life of all flesh, the blood of it is for the life thereof: therefore I said vnto the children of Israel, Ye shall not eat the blood of no maner of flesh: for the life of all flesh is the blood thereof: whosoeuer eateth it, shalbe cut off.
text.NABRE: 17:14 since the life of all flesh is its blood. I have told the Israelites: You shall not consume the blood of any flesh. Since the life of all flesh is its blood, anyone who consumes it shall be cut off.

text.NRSV: All persons, citizens or aliens, who eat what dies of itself or what has been torn by wild animals, shall wash their clothes, and bathe themselves in water, and be unclean until the evening; then they shall be clean.
text.Rotherham: And, in the case of any person who eateth that which died of itself or was torn in pieces, whether he be home-born, or a sojourner, then shall he wash his clothes, and bathe in water, and be unclean until the evening, and then be clean.
text.KJV: 17:15 And euery soule that eateth that which died of it selfe, or that which was torne with beasts, whether it bee one of your owne countrey, or a stranger, he shall both wash his clothes, and bathe himselfe in water, and be vncleane vntill the Euen: then shall he be cleane.
text.NABRE: 17:15 Everyone, whether a native or an alien, who eats of an animal that died of itself or was killed by a wild beast, shall wash his garments, bathe in water, and be unclean until evening, and then become clean.

text.NRSV: But if they do not wash themselves or bathe their body, they shall bear their guilt.
text.Rotherham: But, if he wash them not, and his flesh, he do not bathe, then shall he bear his iniquity.
text.KJV: 17:16 But if he wash them not, nor bathe his flesh, then he shal beare his iniquity.
text.NABRE: 17:16 If one does not wash his garments and bathe, that person shall bear the penalty.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 18:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 18:1 Chapter 18 - Laws Concerning Sexual Behavior. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—I—Yahweh, am your God:—
text.KJV: 18:2 Speake vnto the children of Israel, and say vnto them, I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 18:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: I, the LORD, am your God.

text.NRSV: You shall not do as they do in the land of Egypt, where you lived, and you shall not do as they do in the land of Canaan, to which I am bringing you. You shall not follow their statutes.
text.Rotherham: After the doings of the land of Egypt wherein ye dwelt, shall ye not do,—And after the doings of the land of Canaan whither, I, am bringing you in, shall ye not do, And, in their statutes, shall ye not walk:
text.KJV: 18:3 After the doings of the land of Egypt wherein ye dwelt, shal ye not doe: and after the doings of land of Canaan whither I bring you, shall ye not doe: neither shall yee walke in their ordinances.
text.NABRE: 18:3 You shall not do as they do in the land of Egypt, where you once lived, nor shall you do as they do in the land of Canaan, where I am bringing you; do not conform to their customs.

text.NRSV: My ordinances you shall observe and my statutes you shall keep, following them: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: My regulations, shall ye do, And, my statutes, shall ye observe to walk therein,—I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 18:4 Ye shall doe my iudgements, and keepe mine ordinances, to walke therein: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 18:4 My decrees you shall carry out, and my statutes you shall take care to follow. I, the LORD, am your God.

text.NRSV: You shall keep my statutes and my ordinances; by doing so one shall live: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye observe my statutes, and my regulations, Which if the son of earth shall do them, Then shall he live in them,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 18:5 Yee shall therefore keepe my statutes, and my iudgements: which if a man doe, hee shall liue in them: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 18:5 Keep, then, my statutes and decrees, for the person who carries them out will find life through them. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: None of you shall approach anyone near of kin to uncover nakedness: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: No person whatsoever, unto any of the near kin of his own flesh, shall approach to uncover the parts of shame,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 18:6 ¶ None of you shall approche to any that is neere of kinne to him, to vncouer their nakednesse: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 18:6 None of you shall approach a close relative to have sexual intercourse. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father, which is the nakedness of your mother; she is your mother, you shall not uncover her nakedness.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy father, even the shame of thy mother, shalt thou not uncover,—thy mother, she is, thou shalt not uncover her shame.
text.KJV: 18:7 The nakednesse of thy father, or the nakednesse of thy mother, shalt thou not vncouer: she is thy mother, thou shalt not vncouer her nakednesse.
text.NABRE: 18:7 You shall not disgrace your father by having intercourse with your mother. She is your own mother; you shall not have intercourse with her.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s wife; it is the nakedness of your father.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy father’s wife, shalt thou not uncover,—thy father’s shame, it is.
text.KJV: 18:8 The nakednesse of thy fathers wife shalt thou not vncouer: it is thy fathers nakednesse.
text.NABRE: 18:8 You shall not have intercourse with your father’s wife, for that would be a disgrace to your father.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your sister, your father’s daughter or your mother’s daughter, whether born at home or born abroad.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy sister daughter of thy father or daughter of thy mother, born at home or born abroad, thou shalt not uncover their shame.
text.KJV: 18:9 The nakednesse of thy sister, the daughter of thy father, or daughter of thy mother, whether shee be borne at home, or borne abroad, euen their nakednesse thou shalt not vncouer.
text.NABRE: 18:9 You shall not have intercourse with your sister, your father’s daughter or your mother’s daughter, whether she was born in your own household or born elsewhere.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your son’s daughter or of your daughter’s daughter, for their nakedness is your own nakedness.
text.Rotherham: The shame of the daughter of thy son or the daughter of thy daughter, thou shalt not uncover their shame,—for, thine own shame, they are.
text.KJV: 18:10 The nakednesse of thy sonnes daughter, or of thy daughters daughter, euen their nakednesse thou shalt not vncouer: for theirs is thine owne nakednesse.
text.NABRE: 18:10 You shall not have intercourse with your son’s daughter or with your daughter’s daughter, for that would be a disgrace to you.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s wife’s daughter, begotten by your father, since she is your sister.
text.Rotherham: The shame of the daughter of thy father’s wife, born to thy father, she being, thy sister, thou shalt not uncover her shame.
text.KJV: 18:11 The nakednesse of thy fathers wiues daughter, begotten of thy father, (she is thy sister,) thou shalt not vncouer her nakednesse.
text.NABRE: 18:11 You shall not have intercourse with the daughter whom your father’s wife bore to him in his household, since she, too, is your sister.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s sister; she is your father’s flesh.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy father’s sister, shalt thou not uncover,—thy father’s near of kin, she is.
text.KJV: 18:12 Thou shalt not vncouer the nakednesse of thy fathers sister: she is thy fathers neere kinswoman.
text.NABRE: 18:12 You shall not have intercourse with your father’s sister, since she is your father’s relative.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother’s sister, for she is your mother’s flesh.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy mother’s sister, shalt thou not uncover,—for, thy mother’s near of kin, she is.
text.KJV: 18:13 Thou shalt not vncouer the nakednesse of thy mothers sister: for she is thy mothers neere kinswoman.
text.NABRE: 18:13 You shall not have intercourse with your mother’s sister, since she is your mother’s relative.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s brother, that is, you shall not approach his wife; she is your aunt.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy father’s brother, shalt thou not uncover unto his wife, shalt thou not approach, thine aunt, she is.
text.KJV: 18:14 Thou shalt not vncouer the nakednesse of thy fathers brother, thou shalt not approche to his wife: shee is thine aunt.
text.NABRE: 18:14 You shall not disgrace your father’s brother by having sexual relations with his wife, since she, too, is your aunt.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your daughter-in-law: she is your son’s wife; you shall not uncover her nakedness.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy daughter-in-law, shalt thou not uncover,—thy son’s wife, she is, thou shalt not uncover her shame.
text.KJV: 18:15 Thou shalt not vncouer the nakednesse of thy daughter in law: shee is thy sonnes wife, thou shalt not vncouer her nakednesse.
text.NABRE: 18:15 You shall not have intercourse with your daughter-in-law; she is your son’s wife; you shall not have intercourse with her.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your brother’s wife; it is your brother’s nakedness.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy brother’s wife, shalt thou not uncover,—the shame of thy brother, it is.
text.KJV: 18:16 Thou shalt not vncouer the nakednesse of thy brothers wife: it is thy brothers nakednesse.
text.NABRE: 18:16 You shall not have intercourse with your brother’s wife; that would be a disgrace to your brother.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of a woman and her daughter, and you shall not take her son’s daughter or her daughter’s daughter to uncover her nakedness; they are your flesh; it is depravity.
text.Rotherham: The shame of a woman, and of her daughter, shalt thou not uncover,—neither the daughter of her son nor the daughter of her daughter, shalt thou take, to uncover her shame, near of kin, they are, wickedness, it is.
text.KJV: 18:17 Thou shalt not vncouer the nakednesse of a woman and her daughter, neither shalt thou take her sonnes daughter, or her daughters daughter, to vncouer her nakednesse: For they are her neere kinsewomen: it is wickednesse.
text.NABRE: 18:17 You shall not have intercourse with a woman and also with her daughter, nor shall you marry and have intercourse with her son’s daughter or her daughter’s daughter; they are related to her. This would be shameful.

text.NRSV: And you shall not take a woman as a rival to her sister, uncovering her nakedness while her sister is still alive.
text.Rotherham: And, a woman unto her sister, shalt thou not take,—to cause rivalry, by uncovering her shame besides her own while she is living.
text.KJV: 18:18 Neither shalt thou take a wife to her sister, to vexe her, to vncouer her nakednes besides the other, in her life time.
text.NABRE: 18:18 While your wife is still living you shall not marry her sister as her rival and have intercourse with her.

text.NRSV: You shall not approach a woman to uncover her nakedness while she is in her menstrual uncleanness.
text.Rotherham: And unto a woman during her removal for uncleanness, shalt thou not approach, to uncover her shame.
text.KJV: 18:19 Also thou shalt not approche vnto a woman to vncouer her nakednes, as long as shee is put apart for her vncleannesse.
text.NABRE: 18:19 You shall not approach a woman to have intercourse with her while she is in her menstrual uncleanness.

text.NRSV: You shall not have sexual relations with your kinsman’s wife, and defile yourself with her.
text.Rotherham: And of the wife of thy neighbour, shalt thou not have carnal knowledge,—to commit uncleanness with her.
text.KJV: 18:20 Moreouer, thou shalt not lie carnally with thy neighbours wife, to defile thy selfe with her.
text.NABRE: 18:20 You shall not have sexual relations with your neighbor’s wife, defiling yourself with her.

text.NRSV: You shall not give any of your offspring to sacrifice them to Molech, and so profane the name of your God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And none of thy seed, shalt thou deliver up, to cause to pass through to Molech,—that thou profane not the name of thy God, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 18:21 And thou shalt not let any of thy seed passe through the fire to Molech, neither shalt thou prophane the Name of thy God: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 18:21 You shall not offer any of your offspring for immolation to Molech, thus profaning the name of your God. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: You shall not lie with a male as with a woman; it is an abomination.
text.Rotherham: And, with mankind, shalt thou not lie as with womankind,—an abomination, it is.
text.KJV: 18:22 Thou shalt not lie with mankinde, as with womankinde: it is abomination.
text.NABRE: 18:22 You shall not lie with a male as with a woman; such a thing is an abomination.

text.NRSV: You shall not have sexual relations with any animal and defile yourself with it, nor shall any woman give herself to an animal to have sexual relations with it: it is perversion.
text.Rotherham: And of no beast, shalt thou have carnal knowledge to commit uncleanness therewith,—neither shall a woman present herself to a beast to couch down thereto,—confusion, it is.
text.KJV: 18:23 Neither shalt thou lie with any beast, to defile thy selfe therewith: neither shall any woman stand before a beast to lie downe thereto: It is confusion.
text.NABRE: 18:23 You shall not have sexual relations with an animal, defiling yourself with it; nor shall a woman set herself in front of an animal to mate with it; that is perverse.

text.NRSV: Do not defile yourselves in any of these ways, for by all these practices the nations I am casting out before you have defiled themselves.
text.Rotherham: Do not make yourselves unclean in any of these things,—For in all these things, have the nations made themselves unclean, whom, I, am sending out from before you.
text.KJV: 18:24 Defile not you your selues in any of these things: for in all these, the nations are defiled which I cast out before you.
text.NABRE: 18:24 Do not defile yourselves by any of these things, because by them the nations whom I am driving out of your way have defiled themselves.

text.NRSV: Thus the land became defiled; and I punished it for its iniquity, and the land vomited out its inhabitants.
text.Rotherham: Therefore hath the land become unclean, and I have visited the iniquity thereof upon it,—and the land hath vomited her inhabitants.
text.KJV: 18:25 And the land is defiled: Therefore I doe visit the iniquitie thereof vpon it, and the land it selfe vomiteth out her inhabitants.
text.NABRE: 18:25 And so the land has become defiled, and I have punished it for its wickedness, and the land has vomited out its inhabitants.

text.NRSV: But you shall keep my statutes and my ordinances and commit none of these abominations, either the citizen or the alien who resides among you
text.Rotherham: Ye, therefore, shall observe my statutes and my regulations, and have nothing to do with any of these abominations,—whether the home-born, or the sojourner that sojourneth in your midst;
text.KJV: 18:26 Ye shall therefore keepe my Statutes and my Iudgements, and shall not commit any of these abominations; neither any of your owne nation, nor any stranger that soiourneth among you:
text.NABRE: 18:26 You, however, must keep my statutes and decrees, avoiding all these abominations, both the natives and the aliens resident among you—

text.NRSV: (for the inhabitants of the land, who were before you, committed all of these abominations, and the land became defiled);
text.Rotherham: For all these abominations, have the men of the land done, who were before you; And so the land hath become unclean:
text.KJV: 18:27 (For all these abominations haue the men of the land done, which were before you, and the land is defiled.)
text.NABRE: 18:27 because the previous inhabitants did all these abominations and the land became defiled;

text.NRSV: otherwise the land will vomit you out for defiling it, as it vomited out the nation that was before you.
text.Rotherham: So shall the land not vomit you, through your making it unclean,—as it vomited the nation which was before you.
text.KJV: 18:28 That the land spew not you out also, when ye defile it, as it spewed out the nations that were before you.
text.NABRE: 18:28 otherwise the land will vomit you out also for having defiled it, just as it vomited out the nations before you.

text.NRSV: For whoever commits any of these abominations shall be cut off from their people.
text.Rotherham: For whosoever shall have anything to do with any of these abominations, the persons who have, shall be cut off, out of the midst of their people.
text.KJV: 18:29 For whosoeuer shall commit any of these abominations, euen the soules that commit them, shall be cut off from among their people.
text.NABRE: 18:29 For whoever does any of these abominations shall be cut off from the people.

text.NRSV: So keep my charge not to commit any of these abominations that were done before you, and not to defile yourselves by them: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye keep my charge, So that ye may have nothing to do with any of the abominable statutes with which they have had to do before you, So shall ye not make yourselves unclean thereby,—I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 18:30 Therefore shal ye keepe mine Ordinance, that ye commit not any one of these abominable customes, which were committed before you, and that ye defile not your selues therein: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 18:30 Heed my charge, then, not to observe the abominable customs that have been observed before your time, and thus become impure by them. I, the LORD, am your God.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 19:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 19:1 Chapter 19 - Various Rules of Conduct. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to all the congregation of the people of Israel and say to them: You shall be holy, for I the Lord your God am holy.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel and thou shalt say unto them—Holy, shall ye be,—For holy, am I—Yahweh—your God
text.KJV: 19:2 Speake vnto all the Congregation of the children of Israel, and say vnto them, Ye shalbe holy: for I the Lord your God am holy.
text.NABRE: 19:2 Speak to the whole Israelite community and tell them: Be holy, for I, the LORD your God, am holy.

text.NRSV: You shall each revere your mother and father, and you shall keep my sabbaths: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall, every one, revere, his father and his mother, And my sabbaths, shall ye observe,—I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 19:3 ¶ Yee shall feare euery man his mother, and his father, and keepe my Sabbaths: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 19:3 Each of you revere your mother and father, and keep my sabbaths. I, the LORD, am your God.

text.NRSV: Do not turn to idols or make cast images for yourselves: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Do not turn unto things of nought, And, molten gods, shall ye not make to yourselves,—I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 19:4 ¶ Turne ye not vnto idoles, nor make to your selues molten gods: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 19:4 Do not turn aside to idols, nor make molten gods for yourselves. I, the LORD, am your God.

text.NRSV: When you offer a sacrifice of well-being to the Lord, offer it in such a way that it is acceptable in your behalf.
text.Rotherham: And when ye would offer a peace-offering unto Yahweh, ye shall offer it that ye may be accepted.
text.KJV: 19:5 ¶ And if ye offer a sacrifice of peace offerings vnto the Lord, ye shall offer it, at your owne will.
text.NABRE: 19:5 When you sacrifice your communion sacrifice to the LORD, you shall sacrifice it so that it is acceptable on your behalf.

text.NRSV: It shall be eaten on the same day you offer it, or on the next day; and anything left over until the third day shall be consumed in fire.
text.Rotherham: On the day, ye offer it, shall it be eaten, and on the morrow,—but, that which remaineth until the third day, in fire, shall be consumed.
text.KJV: 19:6 It shall be eaten the same day ye offer it, and on the morrow: and if ought remaine vntill the third day, it shalbe burnt in the fire.
text.NABRE: 19:6 It must be eaten on the day of your sacrifice or on the following day. Whatever is left over until the third day shall be burned in fire.

text.NRSV: If it is eaten at all on the third day, it is an abomination; it will not be acceptable.
text.Rotherham: And, if it, be eaten, on the third day, unclean, it is it shall not be accepted,
text.KJV: 19:7 And if it be eaten at all on the third day, it is abominable; it shall not be accepted.
text.NABRE: 19:7 If any of it is eaten on the third day, it will be a desecrated offering and not be accepted;

text.NRSV: All who eat it shall be subject to punishment, because they have profaned what is holy to the Lord; and any such person shall be cut off from the people.
text.Rotherham: But he that eateth it, his iniquity, shall bear, because that which had been hallowed unto Yahweh, hath he profaned,—so then that person shall be cut off from among his kinsfolk.
text.KJV: 19:8 Therefore euery one that eateth it, shal beare his iniquitie, because he hath prophaned the halowed thing of the Lord; and that soule shalbe cut off from among his people.
text.NABRE: 19:8 whoever eats of it then shall bear the penalty for having profaned what is sacred to the LORD. Such a one shall be cut off from the people.

text.NRSV: When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not reap to the very edges of your field, or gather the gleanings of your harvest.
text.Rotherham: And when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not wholly clear the border of thy field, in reaping,—nor shalt thou gather up the gleaning of thy harvest.
text.KJV: 19:9 ¶ And when ye reape the haruest of your land, thou shalt not wholly reape the corners of thy field, neither shalt thou gather the gleanings of thy haruest.
text.NABRE: 19:9 When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not be so thorough that you reap the field to its very edge, nor shall you gather the gleanings of your harvest.

text.NRSV: You shall not strip your vineyard bare, or gather the fallen grapes of your vineyard; you shall leave them for the poor and the alien: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And, thy vineyard, shalt thou not go over again, nor gather, every single grape: for the poor and for the sojourner, shalt thou leave them, I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 19:10 And thou shalt not gleane thy vineyard, neither shalt thou gather euery grape of thy vineyard; thou shalt leaue them for the poore and stranger: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 19:10 Likewise, you shall not pick your vineyard bare, nor gather up the grapes that have fallen. These things you shall leave for the poor and the alien. I, the LORD, am your God.

text.NRSV: You shall not steal; you shall not deal falsely; and you shall not lie to one another.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not steal,—nor deceive nor lie one man to another;
text.KJV: 19:11 ¶ Ye shall not steale, neither deale falsly, neither lie one to another.
text.NABRE: 19:11 You shall not steal. You shall not deceive or speak falsely to one another.

text.NRSV: And you shall not swear falsely by my name, profaning the name of your God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Nor swear by my name falsely,—And so profane the name of thy God: I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 19:12 ¶ And ye shall not sweare by my Name falsly, neither shalt thou prophane the Name of thy God: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 19:12 You shall not swear falsely by my name, thus profaning the name of your God. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: You shall not defraud your neighbor; you shall not steal; and you shall not keep for yourself the wages of a laborer until morning.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not oppress thy neighbour nor rob [him],—The wages of him that is hired shall not tarry with thee, until the morning.
text.KJV: 19:13 ¶ Thou shalt not defraud thy neighbour, neither rob him: the wages of him that is hired, shal not abide with thee all night, vntill the morning.
text.NABRE: 19:13 You shall not exploit your neighbor. You shall not commit robbery. You shall not withhold overnight the wages of your laborer.

text.NRSV: You shall not revile the deaf or put a stumbling block before the blind; you shall fear your God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not curse the deaf, Nor, before the blind, place a stumbling-block,—So shalt thou stand in awe of thy God I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 19:14 ¶ Thou shalt not curse the deafe, nor put a stumbling blocke before the blind, but shalt feare thy God: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 19:14 You shall not insult the deaf, or put a stumbling block in front of the blind, but you shall fear your God. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: You shall not render an unjust judgment; you shall not be partial to the poor or defer to the great: with justice you shall judge your neighbor.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not act perversely in giving judgment, Thou shalt neither respect the person of the poor, Nor prefer the person of the great,—In righteousness, shalt thou judge thy neighbour.
text.KJV: 19:15 ¶ Ye shall doe no vnrighteousnes in iudgement; thou shalt not respect the person of the poore, nor honour the person of the mightie: but in righteousnesse shalt thou iudge thy neighbour.
text.NABRE: 19:15 You shall not act dishonestly in rendering judgment. Show neither partiality to the weak nor deference to the mighty, but judge your neighbor justly.

text.NRSV: You shall not go around as a slanderer among your people, and you shall not profit by the blood of your neighbor: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not go about talebearing, among thy people Thou shalt not stand by, over the blood of thy neighbour: I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 19:16 ¶ Thou shalt not goe vp and downe as a tale-bearer among thy people: neither shalt thou stand against the blood of thy neighbour: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 19:16 You shall not go about spreading slander among your people; nor shall you stand by idly when your neighbor’s life is at stake. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: You shall not hate in your heart anyone of your kin; you shall reprove your neighbor, or you will incur guilt yourself.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not hate thy brother in thy heart,—Thou shalt, faithfully reprove, thy neighbour, and not countenance him in sin:
text.KJV: 19:17 ¶ Thou shalt not hate thy brother in thine heart: thou shalt in any wise rebuke thy neighbour, and not suffer sinne vpon him.
text.NABRE: 19:17 You shall not hate any of your kindred in your heart. Reprove your neighbor openly so that you do not incur sin because of that person.

text.NRSV: You shall not take vengeance or bear a grudge against any of your people, but you shall love your neighbor as yourself: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not take vengeance neither shalt thou cherish anger against the sons of thy people, So shalt thou love thy neighbour as thyself,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 19:18 ¶ Thou shalt not auenge nor beare any grudge against the children of thy people, but thou shalt loue thy neighbor as thy selfe: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 19:18 Take no revenge and cherish no grudge against your own people. You shall love your neighbor as yourself. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: You shall keep my statutes. You shall not let your animals breed with a different kind; you shall not sow your field with two kinds of seed; nor shall you put on a garment made of two different materials.
text.Rotherham: My statutes, shall ye observe, Thy beasts, shalt thou not cause to breed in two kinds, Thy field, shalt thou not sow with two sorts of seed,—And a garment woven of diverse threads, shalt thou not suffer to come upon thee.
text.KJV: 19:19 ¶ Yee shall keepe my Statutes: Thou shalt not let thy cattell gender with a diuerse kinde: Thou shalt not sowe thy field with mingled seed: Neither shall a garment mingled of linnen and woollen come vpon thee.
text.NABRE: 19:19 Keep my statutes: do not breed any of your domestic animals with others of a different species; do not sow a field of yours with two different kinds of seed; and do not put on a garment woven with two different kinds of thread.

text.NRSV: If a man has sexual relations with a woman who is a slave, designated for another man but not ransomed or given her freedom, an inquiry shall be held. They shall not be put to death, since she has not been freed;
text.Rotherham: And whosoever lieth carnally with a woman, she being a bondmaid, acquired for a husband, and neither, redeemed, nor, freedom, given her, inquisition, shall be made they shall not be put to death, because she was not free;
text.KJV: 19:20 ¶ And whosoeuer lieth carnally with a woman that is a bondmaid, she shall be scourged: they shall not be put to death, because she was not free:
text.NABRE: 19:20 If a man has sexual relations with a female slave who has been acquired by another man but has not yet been redeemed or given her freedom, an investigation shall be made. They shall not be put to death, because she has not been freed.

text.NRSV: but he shall bring a guilt offering for himself to the Lord, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, a ram as guilt offering.
text.Rotherham: but he shall bring in his guilt-bearer unto Yahweh, unto the entrance of the tent of meeting,—even a ram as a guilt-bearer;
text.KJV: 19:21 And he shall bring his trespasse offering vnto the Lord, vnto the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation, euen a ramme for a trespasse offering.
text.NABRE: 19:21 The man shall bring to the entrance of the tent of meeting as his reparation to the LORD a ram as a reparation offering.

text.NRSV: And the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of guilt offering before the Lord for his sin that he committed; and the sin he committed shall be forgiven him.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall put a propitiatory-covering over him with the guilt-bearing ram before Yahweh, on account of his sin which he hath committed,—so shall he have forgiveness, from his sin which he hath committed.
text.KJV: 19:22 And the Priest shall make an atonement for him with the ramme of the trespasse offering before the Lord for his sinne which hee hath done: and the sinne which he hath done shall bee forgiuen him.
text.NABRE: 19:22 With the ram of the reparation offering the priest shall make atonement before the LORD for the wrong the man has committed, so that he will be forgiven for the wrong he has committed.

text.NRSV: When you come into the land and plant all kinds of trees for food, then you shall regard their fruit as forbidden; three years it shall be forbidden to you, it must not be eaten.
text.Rotherham: And when ye come into the land, and plant any manner of fruit-tree, then shall ye count as uncircumcised the fruit thereof, three years, shall it be to you as if unclean, it shall not be eaten.
text.KJV: 19:23 ¶ And when yee shall come in to the land, and shall haue planted all maner of trees for food, then ye shall count the fruit therof as vncircumcised: three yeeres shall it be as vncircumcised vnto you: it shall not be eaten of.
text.NABRE: 19:23 When you come into the land and plant any fruit tree there, first look upon its fruit as if it were uncircumcised. For three years, it shall be uncircumcised for you; it may not be eaten.

text.NRSV: In the fourth year all their fruit shall be set apart for rejoicing in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But, in the fourth year, all the fruit thereof shall be hallowed for a festival of thanksgiving unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 19:24 But in the fourth yeere all the fruit thereof shall be holy to praise the Lord withall.
text.NABRE: 19:24 In the fourth year, however, all of its fruit shall be dedicated to the LORD in joyous celebration.

text.NRSV: But in the fifth year you may eat of their fruit, that their yield may be increased for you: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And in the fifth year, shall ye eat the fruit thereof, that it may enrich you with its increase,—I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 19:25 And in the fift yeere shall ye eate of the fruit thereof, that it may yeelde vnto you the increase thereof: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 19:25 Not until the fifth year may you eat its fruit, to increase the yield for you. I, the LORD, am your God.

text.NRSV: You shall not eat anything with its blood. You shall not practice augury or witchcraft.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall eat nothing with the blood thereof,—Ye shall not practise divination neither shall ye use magic.
text.KJV: 19:26 ¶ Ye shall not eate any thing with the blood, neither shall ye vse inchantment, nor obserue times.
text.NABRE: 19:26 Do not eat anything with the blood still in it. Do not recite charms or practice soothsaying.

text.NRSV: You shall not round off the hair on your temples or mar the edges of your beard.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not shave in a circle around your head,—nor shalt thou disfigure the fringe of thy beard.
text.KJV: 19:27 Ye shall not round the corners of your heads, neither shalt thou marre the corners of thy beard.
text.NABRE: 19:27 Do not clip your hair at the temples, nor spoil the edges of your beard.

text.NRSV: You shall not make any gashes in your flesh for the dead or tattoo any marks upon you: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Cuttings for a dead person, shall ye not make in your flesh. And punctures in your persons, shall ye not print,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 19:28 Ye shall not make any cuttings in your flesh for the dead, nor print any markes vpon you: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 19:28 Do not lacerate your bodies for the dead, and do not tattoo yourselves. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: Do not profane your daughter by making her a prostitute, that the land not fall into whoredom and the land become full of depravity.
text.Rotherham: Do not profane thy daughter by causing her to be unchaste,—lest the land fall to unchastity, and so the land be filled with wickedness.
text.KJV: 19:29 ¶ Doe not prostitute thy daughter, to cause her to be a whore, lest the land fall to whoredome, and the land become full of wickednesse.
text.NABRE: 19:29 You shall not degrade your daughter by making a prostitute of her; otherwise the land will prostitute itself and become full of lewdness.

text.NRSV: You shall keep my sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: My sabbaths, shall ye observe, And my sanctuary, shall ye revere,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 19:30 ¶ Ye shall keepe my Sabbaths, and reuerence my Sanctuary: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 19:30 Keep my sabbaths, and reverence my sanctuary. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: Do not turn to mediums or wizards; do not seek them out, to be defiled by them: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Do not turn unto mediums, Nor, for oracles, make search, To render yourselves unclean with them, I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 19:31 ¶ Regard not them that haue familiar spirits, neither seeke after Wizards, to be defiled by them: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 19:31 Do not turn to ghosts or consult spirits, by which you will be defiled. I, the LORD, am your God.

text.NRSV: You shall rise before the aged, and defer to the old; and you shall fear your God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Before a hoary head, shalt thou rise up, And shalt honour the presence of an elder,—So shalt thou stand in awe of thy God—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 19:32 ¶ Thou shalt rise vp before the hoary head, and honour the face of the old man, and feare thy God: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 19:32 Stand up in the presence of the aged, show respect for the old, and fear your God. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: When an alien resides with you in your land, you shall not oppress the alien.
text.Rotherham: And, when there sojourneth with thee a sojourner in your land, ye shall not oppress him:
text.KJV: 19:33 ¶ And if a stranger soiourne with thee in your land, yee shall not vexe him.
text.NABRE: 19:33 When an alien resides with you in your land, do not mistreat such a one.

text.NRSV: The alien who resides with you shall be to you as the citizen among you; you shall love the alien as yourself, for you were aliens in the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: As one home born from among yourselves, shall be unto you the sojourner who sojourneth with you, So shalt thou love him as thyself, For, sojourners, became ye, in the land of Egypt,—I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 19:34 But the stranger that dwelleth with you, shalbe as one borne amongst you, and thou shalt loue him as thy selfe, for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 19:34 You shall treat the alien who resides with you no differently than the natives born among you; you shall love the alien as yourself; for you too were once aliens in the land of Egypt. I, the LORD, am your God.

text.NRSV: You shall not cheat in measuring length, weight, or quantity.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not act perversely in giving judgment,—in measures of extent, in weights in measures of capacity:
text.KJV: 19:35 ¶ Ye shall doe no vnrighteousnes in iudgment, in meteyard, in weight, or in measure.
text.NABRE: 19:35 Do not act dishonestly in using measures of length or weight or capacity.

text.NRSV: You shall have honest balances, honest weights, an honest ephah, and an honest hin: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Just balances, just weights, a just ephah and a just hin, shall ye have,—I—Yahweh, am your God, who brought you forth out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: 19:36 Iust ballances, iust weights, a iust Ephah, and a iust Hin shall ye haue: I am the Lord your God, which brought you out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: 19:36 You shall have a true scale and true weights, an honest ephah and an honest hin. I, the LORD, am your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt.

text.NRSV: You shall keep all my statutes and all my ordinances, and observe them: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye observe all my statutes and all my regulations and do them,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 19:37 Therefore shall ye obserue all my Statutes, and all my Iudgements, and doe them: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 19:37 Be careful, then, to observe all my statutes and decrees. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 20:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 20:1 Chapter 20 - Penalties for Various Sins. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Say further to the people of Israel: Any of the people of Israel, or of the aliens who reside in Israel, who give any of their offspring to Molech shall be put to death; the people of the land shall stone them to death.
text.Rotherham: Unto the sons of Israel, therefore shalt thou say, What man soever, there may be of the sons of Israel, or of the sojourners that sojourn in Israel, that giveth of his seed unto Molech, he shall, surely be put to death,—the people of the land shall stone him with stones;
text.KJV: 20:2 Againe, thou shalt say to the children of Israel; whosoeuer he be of the children of Israel, or of the strangers that soiourne in Israel, that giueth any of his seed vnto Molech, he shall surely be put to death: the people of the land shall stone him with stones.
text.NABRE: 20:2 Tell the Israelites: Anyone, whether an Israelite or an alien residing in Israel, who gives offspring to Molech shall be put to death. The people of the land shall stone that person.

text.NRSV: I myself will set my face against them, and will cut them off from the people, because they have given of their offspring to Molech, defiling my sanctuary and profaning my holy name.
text.Rotherham: I, also, will set my face against that man, and will cut him off out of the midst of his people,—because of his seed, hath he given unto Molech, seeing that he hath made unclean my sanctuary, even to the extent of profaning my holy name.
text.KJV: 20:3 And I wil set my face against that man, and will cut him off from among his people: because he hath giuen of his seed vnto Molech, to defile my Sanctuary, and to prophane my holy Name.
text.NABRE: 20:3 I myself will turn against and cut off that individual from among the people; for in the giving of offspring to Molech, my sanctuary was defiled and my holy name was profaned.

text.NRSV: And if the people of the land should ever hide their eyes from those persons, when they give of their offspring to Molech, and do not put them to death,
text.Rotherham: But, if the people of the land, do even hide, their eyes from that man, when he giveth of his seed unto Molech,—so as not to put him to death,
text.KJV: 20:4 And if the people of the land doe any wayes hide their eyes from the man, when he giueth of his seed vnto Molech, and kill him not:
text.NABRE: 20:4 If the people of the land condone the giving of offspring to Molech, by failing to put the wrongdoer to death,

text.NRSV: I myself will set my face against them and against their family, and will cut them off from their people, them and all who follow them in prostituting themselves to Molech.
text.Rotherham: then will I myself, set my face against that man, and against his family,—and will cut him off, and all that follow unchastely after him—in going unchastely after Molech—out of the midst of their people.
text.KJV: 20:5 Then I will set my face against that man, and against his family, and will cut him off, and all that goe a whoring after him, to commit whoredome with Molech, from among their people.
text.NABRE: 20:5 I myself will turn against that individual and his or her family, and I will cut off from their people both the wrongdoer and all who follow this person by prostituting themselves with Molech.

text.NRSV: If any turn to mediums and wizards, prostituting themselves to them, I will set my face against them, and will cut them off from the people.
text.Rotherham: The person also that turneth unto mediums and unto oracles, in going unchastely after them, then will I set my face against that person, and will cut him off out of the midst of his people.
text.KJV: 20:6 ¶ And the soule that turneth after such as haue familiar spirits, and after wizards, to goe a whoring after them, I will euen set my face against that soule, and will cut him off from among his people.
text.NABRE: 20:6 Should anyone turn to ghosts and spirits and prostitute oneself with them, I will turn against that person and cut such a one off from among the people.

text.NRSV: Consecrate yourselves therefore, and be holy; for I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye hallow yourselves and be holy,—Because I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 20:7 ¶ Sanctifie your selues therefore, and bee yee holy: for I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 20:7 Sanctify yourselves, then, and be holy; for I, the LORD, your God, am holy.

text.NRSV: Keep my statutes, and observe them; I am the Lord; I sanctify you.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye observe my statutes, and do them,—I—Yahweh, am he that is hallowing you.
text.KJV: 20:8 And ye shall keepe my Statutes, and do them: I am the Lord which sanctifie you.
text.NABRE: 20:8 Be careful, therefore, to observe my statutes. I, the LORD, make you holy.

text.NRSV: All who curse father or mother shall be put to death; having cursed father or mother, their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: When, any man whatsoever, curseth his father or his mother, he shall surely be put to death,—his father or his mother, hath he cursed, his blood, shall be upon himself.
text.KJV: 20:9 ¶ For euery one that curseth his father or his mother, shalbe surely put to death: hee hath cursed his father or his mother; his blood shalbe vpon him.
text.NABRE: 20:9 Anyone who curses father or mother shall be put to death; and having cursed father or mother, such a one will bear the bloodguilt.

text.NRSV: If a man commits adultery with the wife of his neighbor, both the adulterer and the adulteress shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who committeth adultery with the wife of any other man, he that committeth adultery with the wife of his neighbour shall surely be put to death—the adulterer, and the adulteress.
text.KJV: 20:10 ¶ And the man that committeth adulterie with another mans wife, euen he that committeth adulterie with his neighbours wife, the adulterer, and the adulteresse shall surely bee put to death.
text.NABRE: 20:10 If a man commits adultery with his neighbor’s wife, both the adulterer and the adulteress shall be put to death.

text.NRSV: The man who lies with his father’s wife has uncovered his father’s nakedness; both of them shall be put to death; their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: And any man who lieth with his father’s wife, the shame of his father, hath uncovered,—they both shall, surely be put to death—their blood shall be upon themselves.
text.KJV: 20:11 And the man that lieth with his fathers wife, hath vncouered his fathers nakednesse: both of them shalbe put to death; their blood shalbe vpon them.
text.NABRE: 20:11 If a man disgraces his father by lying with his father’s wife, the two of them shall be put to death; their bloodguilt is upon them.

text.NRSV: If a man lies with his daughter-in-law, both of them shall be put to death; they have committed perversion, their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: And any man who lieth with his daughter-in-law, they both shall, surely be put to death, confusion, have they wrought—their blood shall be upon themselves.
text.KJV: 20:12 And if a man lie with his daughter in law, both of them shall surely be put to death: they haue wrought confusion; their blood shall be vpon them.
text.NABRE: 20:12 If a man lies with his daughter-in-law, both of them shall be put to death; they have done what is perverse; their bloodguilt is upon them.

text.NRSV: If a man lies with a male as with a woman, both of them have committed an abomination; they shall be put to death; their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: And any man who lieth with mankind as with womankind, an abomination, have both of them wrought,—they shall, surely be put to death,—their blood, shall be upon themselves.
text.KJV: 20:13 If a man also lie with mankind, as hee lyeth with a woman, both of them haue committed an abomination: they shall surely be put to death; their blood shalbe vpon them.
text.NABRE: 20:13 If a man lies with a male as with a woman, they have committed an abomination; the two of them shall be put to death; their bloodguilt is upon them.

text.NRSV: If a man takes a wife and her mother also, it is depravity; they shall be burned to death, both he and they, that there be no depravity among you.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who taketh a woman and her mother, wickedness, it is,—in fire, shall both he and they be consumed, that wickedness be not in your midst.
text.KJV: 20:14 And if a man take a wife, and her mother, it is wickednesse: They shalbe burnt with fire, both he and they, that there be no wickednesse among you.
text.NABRE: 20:14 If a man marries a woman and her mother also, that is shameful conduct; the man and the two women as well shall be burned to death, so that shamefulness may not be found among you.

text.NRSV: If a man has sexual relations with an animal, he shall be put to death; and you shall kill the animal.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who hath carnal knowledge of a beast, shall, surely be put to death,—and, the beast, shall ye slay.
text.KJV: 20:15 And if a man lie with a beast, he shall surely be put to death: and ye shall slay the beast.
text.NABRE: 20:15 If a man has sexual relations with an animal, the man shall be put to death, and you shall kill the animal.

text.NRSV: If a woman approaches any animal and has sexual relations with it, you shall kill the woman and the animal; they shall be put to death, their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: And, a woman, who approacheth unto any beast, to couch down thereto, then shalt thou slay the woman and the beast,—they shall surely be put to death,—their blood, shall be upon themselves.
text.KJV: 20:16 And if a woman approch vnto any beast, and lie downe thereto, thou shalt kill the woman and the beast: they shall surely be put to death, their blood shalbe vpon them.
text.NABRE: 20:16 If a woman goes up to any animal to mate with it, you shall kill the woman and the animal; they shall both be put to death; their bloodguilt is upon them.

text.NRSV: If a man takes his sister, a daughter of his father or a daughter of his mother, and sees her nakedness, and she sees his nakedness, it is a disgrace, and they shall be cut off in the sight of their people; he has uncovered his sister’s nakedness, he shall bear his iniquity.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who taketh his sister—his father’s daughter or his mother’s daughter, and vieweth her shame, and, she, vieweth his shame, a disgrace, it is,—they shall therefore be cut off in the sight of the sons of their people,—the shame of his sister, hath he uncovered—his iniquity, shall he bear.
text.KJV: 20:17 And if a man shall take his sister, his fathers daughter, or his mothers daughter, and see her nakednesse, and she see his nakednesse, it is a wicked thing, and they shall bee cut off in the sight of their people: he hath vncouered his sisters nakednesse, he shall beare his iniquitie.
text.NABRE: 20:17 If a man marries his sister, his father’s daughter or his mother’s daughter, and they have intercourse with each other, that is disgraceful; they shall be publicly cut off from the people; the man shall bear the penalty of having had intercourse with his own sister.

text.NRSV: If a man lies with a woman having her sickness and uncovers her nakedness, he has laid bare her flow and she has laid bare her flow of blood; both of them shall be cut off from their people.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who lieth with a woman having her sickness, and uncovereth her shame, her fountain, hath he exposed, she, also hath uncovered her fountain of blood,—they shall therefore both be cut off out of the midst of their people.
text.KJV: 20:18 And if a man shall lie with a woman hauing her sickenesse, and shal vncouer her nakednesse: he hath discouered her fountaine, and she hath vncouered the fountaine of her blood: and both of them shall bee cut off from among their people.
text.NABRE: 20:18 If a man lies with a woman during her menstrual period and has intercourse with her, he has laid bare the source of her flow and she has uncovered it. The two of them shall be cut off from the people.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother’s sister or of your father’s sister, for that would uncover one’s own flesh; they shall bear their iniquity.
text.Rotherham: And the shame of thy mother’s sister, or of thy father’s sister, shalt thou not uncover,—for his near of kin, hath he exposed—their iniquity, shall they bear.
text.KJV: 20:19 And thou shalt not vncouer the nakednesse of thy mothers sister, nor of thy fathers sister: for hee vncouereth his neere kinne: they shall beare their iniquitie.
text.NABRE: 20:19 You shall not have intercourse with your mother’s sister or your father’s sister, because that dishonors one’s own flesh; they shall bear their penalty.

text.NRSV: If a man lies with his uncle’s wife, he has uncovered his uncle’s nakedness; they shall bear their sin, they shall die childless.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who lieth with his uncle’s wife, the shame of his uncle, hath he uncovered,—their sin, shall they bear—childless, shall they die.
text.KJV: 20:20 And if a man shall lie with his vncles wife, he hath vncouered his vncles nakednesse: they shall beare their sinne, they shall die childlesse.
text.NABRE: 20:20 If a man lies with his uncle’s wife, he disgraces his uncle; they shall bear the penalty; they shall die childless.

text.NRSV: If a man takes his brother’s wife, it is impurity; he has uncovered his brother’s nakedness; they shall be childless.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who taketh his brother’s wife, impurity, it is,—the shame of his brother, hath he uncovered—childless, shall they remain.
text.KJV: 20:21 And if a man shall take his brothers wife, it is an vncleane thing: hee hath vncouered his brothers nakednesse, they shall be childlesse.
text.NABRE: 20:21 If a man takes his brother’s wife, it is severe defilement and he has disgraced his brother; they shall be childless.

text.NRSV: You shall keep all my statutes and all my ordinances, and observe them, so that the land to which I bring you to settle in does not vomit you out.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye observe all my statutes and all my regulations, and do them,—So shall the land whereinto I am bringing you to dwell therein, not vomit you forth;
text.KJV: 20:22 ¶ Ye shall therefore keepe all my Statutes, and all my Iudgements, and doe them: that the lande whither I bring you to dwell therein, spue you not out.
text.NABRE: 20:22 Be careful to observe all my statutes and all my decrees; otherwise the land where I am bringing you to dwell will vomit you out.

text.NRSV: You shall not follow the practices of the nation that I am driving out before you. Because they did all these things, I abhorred them.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye not walk in the—statutes of the nations, which I am casting out from before you,—For all these things, had they done, Therefore I abhorred them;
text.KJV: 20:23 And ye shall not walke in the maners of the nation, which I cast out before you: for they committed all these things, & therefore I abhorred them.
text.NABRE: 20:23 Do not conform, therefore, to the customs of the nations whom I am driving out of your way, because all these things that they have done have filled me with disgust for them.

text.NRSV: But I have said to you: You shall inherit their land, and I will give it to you to possess, a land flowing with milk and honey. I am the Lord your God; I have separated you from the peoples.
text.Rotherham: And said unto you—Ye, shall possess their soil, Yea, I myself, will give it you to possess it, A land flowing with milk and honey,—I—Yahweh, am your God, who have distinguished you from the peoples;
text.KJV: 20:24 But I haue said vnto you, Yee shall inherit their land, and I will giue it vnto you, to possesse it, a land that floweth with milke and hony: I am the Lord your God, which haue separated you from other people.
text.NABRE: 20:24 But to you I have said: You shall take possession of their land. I am giving it to you to possess, a land flowing with milk and honey. I, the LORD, am your God, who have set you apart from other peoples.

text.NRSV: You shall therefore make a distinction between the clean animal and the unclean, and between the unclean bird and the clean; you shall not bring abomination on yourselves by animal or by bird or by anything with which the ground teems, which I have set apart for you to hold unclean.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye make a distinction—Between the clean beasts, and the unclean,—And between the unclean birds and the clean,—So shall ye not make your persons abominable with beast, or with bird or with anything which creepeth upon the ground, which I have distinguished for you, as unclean.
text.KJV: 20:25 Ye shall therefore put difference betweene cleane beasts, and vncleane, and betweene vncleane foules, and cleane: & ye shall not make your soules abominable by beast or by foule, or by any maner of liuing thing, that creepeth on the ground, which I haue separated from you as vncleane.
text.NABRE: 20:25 You, too, must set apart, then, the clean animals from the unclean, and the clean birds from the unclean, so that you do not make yourselves detestable through any beast or bird or any creature which creeps on the ground that I have set apart for you as unclean.

text.NRSV: You shall be holy to me; for I the Lord am holy, and I have separated you from the other peoples to be mine.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye be unto me, holy persons, for, holy, am, I—Yahweh,—Therefore have I distinguished you from the peoples, that ye may be mine.
text.KJV: 20:26 And ye shal be holy vnto me: for I the Lord am holy, & haue seuered you from other people, that ye should be mine.
text.NABRE: 20:26 To me, therefore, you shall be holy; for I, the LORD, am holy, and I have set you apart from other peoples to be my own.

text.NRSV: A man or a woman who is a medium or a wizard shall be put to death; they shall be stoned to death, their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: And as touching man or woman, when there shall be in them a familiar spirit, or the spirit of an oracle, they shall, surely be put to death,—with stones, shall they be stoned—their blood, shall be upon themselves.
text.KJV: 20:27 ¶ A man also or woman that hath a familiar spirit, or that is a wizzard, shall surely be put to death: they shall stone them with stones: their blood shalbe vpon them.
text.NABRE: 20:27 A man or a woman who acts as a medium or clairvoyant shall be put to death. They shall be stoned to death; their bloodguilt is upon them.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses: Speak to the priests, the sons of Aaron, and say to them: No one shall defile himself for any dead person among his kin,
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses, Say unto the priests, the sons of Aaron,—yea thou shalt say unto them, For a dead person, shall no one make himself unclean among his people:
text.KJV: 21:1 And the Lord said vnto Moses; Speake vnto the Priests the sonnes of Aaron, and say vnto them, There shall none be defiled for the dead among his people:
text.NABRE: 21:1 Chapter 21 - Sanctity of the Priesthood. The LORD said to Moses: Speak to the priests, Aaron’s sons, and tell them: None of you shall make himself unclean for any dead person among his kindred,

text.NRSV: except for his nearest kin: his mother, his father, his son, his daughter, his brother;
text.Rotherham: Saving for his kin, that are near unto him, for his mother or for his father, or for his son or for his daughter, or for his brother;
text.KJV: 21:2 But for his kinne, that is neere vnto him, that is, for his mother, and for his father, and for his sonne, and for his daughter, and for his brother,
text.NABRE: 21:2 except for his nearest relatives, his mother or father, his son or daughter, his brother

text.NRSV: likewise, for a virgin sister, close to him because she has had no husband, he may defile himself for her.
text.Rotherham: or for his sister, a virgin who is near unto him, who belongeth not unto a husband,—for her, he may make himself unclean:
text.KJV: 21:3 And for his sister a virgin, that is nigh vnto him, which hath had no husband: for her may he be defiled.
text.NABRE: 21:3 or his unmarried sister, who is of his own family while she remains single; for these he may make himself unclean.

text.NRSV: But he shall not defile himself as a husband among his people and so profane himself.
text.Rotherham: He shall not make himself unclean being a chief among his people,—by profaning himself:
text.KJV: 21:4 But hee shall not defile himselfe being a chiefe man among his people, to prophane himselfe.
text.NABRE: 21:4 But as a husband among his kindred he shall not make himself unclean and thus profane himself.

text.NRSV: They shall not make bald spots upon their heads, or shave off the edges of their beards, or make any gashes in their flesh.
text.Rotherham: They shall not make a baldness behind in their head, And the border of their beard, shall they not cut off,—And, in their flesh, shall they not make incisions.
text.KJV: 21:5 They shall not make baldnesse vpon their head, neither shall they shaue off the corner of their beard, nor make any cuttings in their flesh:
text.NABRE: 21:5 The priests shall not make bald the crown of their head, nor shave the edges of their beard, nor lacerate their body.

text.NRSV: They shall be holy to their God, and not profane the name of their God; for they offer the Lord’s offerings by fire, the food of their God, and therefore they shall be holy.
text.Rotherham: Holy persons, shall they be unto their God, and not profane the name of their God,—for, the altar-flames of Yahweh, the food of their God, do they bring near—Therefore shall they be holy.
text.KJV: 21:6 They shalbe holy vnto their God, and not profane the name of their God: for the offrings of the Lord made by fire, and the bread of their God they doe offer: therefore they shall be holy.
text.NABRE: 21:6 They shall be holy to their God, and shall not profane their God’s name, since they offer the oblations of the LORD, the food of their God; so they must be holy.

text.NRSV: They shall not marry a prostitute or a woman who has been defiled; neither shall they marry a woman divorced from her husband. For they are holy to their God,
text.Rotherham: A woman that is unchaste or dishonoured, shall they not take, And a woman divorced from her husband, shall they not take,—For holy, he is unto his God.
text.KJV: 21:7 They shall not take a wife that is a whore, or profane, neither shall they take a woman put away from her husband: for he is holy vnto his God.
text.NABRE: 21:7 A priest shall not marry a woman debased by prostitution, nor a woman who has been divorced by her husband; for the priest is holy to his God.

text.NRSV: and you shall treat them as holy, since they offer the food of your God; they shall be holy to you, for I the Lord, who sanctify you, am holy.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shalt thou hold him as holy, for the food of thy God, doth he bring near,—Holy, shall he be to thee, For, holy, am I—Yahweh, who am making you holy.
text.KJV: 21:8 Thou shalt sanctifie him therfore, for he offereth the bread of thy God: he shalbe holy vnto thee: for I the Lord which sanctifie you, am holy.
text.NABRE: 21:8 Honor him as holy for he offers the food of your God; he shall be holy to you, because I, the LORD, am holy who make you holy.

text.NRSV: When the daughter of a priest profanes herself through prostitution, she profanes her father; she shall be burned to death.
text.Rotherham: And, the daughter of any priest, when she profaneth herself by unchastity, Her father, she, doth profane, with fire, shall she be consumed.
