text.KJV: 21:9 ¶ And the daughter of any Priest, if she profane her selfe, by playing the whore, she profaneth her father: shee shall be burnt with fire.
text.NABRE: 21:9 If a priest’s daughter debases herself by prostitution, she thereby debases her father; she shall be burned with fire.

text.NRSV: The priest who is exalted above his fellows, on whose head the anointing oil has been poured and who has been consecrated to wear the vestments, shall not dishevel his hair, nor tear his vestments.
text.Rotherham: And, as for the high priest from among his brethren upon whose head is poured the anointing oil, and who is installed, by putting on the garments, His head, shall he not bare, And, his garments, shall he not rend;
text.KJV: 21:10 And he that is the high Priest among his brethren, vpon whose head the anointing oyle was powred, and that is consecrated to put on the garments, shall not vncouer his head, nor rent his clothes:
text.NABRE: 21:10 The most exalted of the priests, upon whose head the anointing oil has been poured and who has been ordained to wear the special vestments, shall not dishevel his hair or rend his garments,

text.NRSV: He shall not go where there is a dead body; he shall not defile himself even for his father or mother.
text.Rotherham: And, unto no persons of the dead, shall he go in,—For his father or for his mother, shall he not make himself unclean;
text.KJV: 21:11 Neither shall he goe in to any dead body, nor defile himselfe for his father, or for his mother:
text.NABRE: 21:11 nor shall he go near any dead person. Not even for his father or mother may he thus become unclean;

text.NRSV: He shall not go outside the sanctuary and thus profane the sanctuary of his God; for the consecration of the anointing oil of his God is upon him: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, out of the sanctuary, shall he not go forth, so shall he not profane the sanctuary of his God,—for the consecration of the anointing oil of his God is upon him—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 21:12 Neither shall hee goe out of the Sanctuary, nor prophane the Sanctuary of his God; for the crowne of the anointing oile of his God is vpon him: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 21:12 nor shall he leave the sanctuary and profane the sanctuary of his God, for the consecration of the anointing oil of his God is upon him. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: He shall marry only a woman who is a virgin.
text.Rotherham: But, he, shall take a woman in her virginity:
text.KJV: 21:13 And he shall take a wife in her virginitie.
text.NABRE: 21:13 He shall marry only a woman who is a virgin.

text.NRSV: A widow, or a divorced woman, or a woman who has been defiled, a prostitute, these he shall not marry. He shall marry a virgin of his own kin,
text.Rotherham: A widow or a divorced woman or one dishonoured—an unchaste woman, these, shall he not take, But, a virgin from among his own kinsfolk, shall he take to wife;
text.KJV: 21:14 A widow, or a diuorced woman, or prophane, or an harlot, these shall he not take: but he shall take a virgine of his owne people to wife.
text.NABRE: 21:14 He shall not marry a widow or a woman who has been divorced or one who has been debased by prostitution, but only a virgin, taken from his kindred, he shall marry,

text.NRSV: that he may not profane his offspring among his kin; for I am the Lord; I sanctify him.
text.Rotherham: So shall he not profane his seed, among his kinsfolk,—For I—Yahweh, do hallow him.
text.KJV: 21:15 Neither shal he prophane his seed among his people: for I the Lord doe sanctifie him.
text.NABRE: 21:15 so that he not profane his offspring among his kindred. I, the LORD, make him holy. Priestly Blemishes.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 21:16 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 21:16 The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to Aaron and say: No one of your offspring throughout their generations who has a blemish may approach to offer the food of his God.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron, saying,—No man of thy seed, unto their generations in whom shall be any blemish, shall come near, to present the food of his God.
text.KJV: 21:17 Speake vnto Aaron, saying, Whosoeuer he be of thy seed in their generations, that hath any blemish, let him not approche to offer the bread of his God:
text.NABRE: 21:17 Say to Aaron: None of your descendants, throughout their generations, who has any blemish shall come forward to offer the food of his God.

text.NRSV: For no one who has a blemish shall draw near, one who is blind or lame, or one who has a mutilated face or a limb too long,
text.Rotherham: Surely, no man in whom is any blemish, shall come near,—No man who is blind or lame, or hath a flat nose, or is lanky;
text.KJV: 21:18 For whatsoeuer man hee be that hath a blemish, he shall not approche: a blind man, or a lame, or he that hath a flat nose, or any thing superfluous,
text.NABRE: 21:18 Anyone who has any of the following blemishes may not come forward: he who is blind, or lame, or who has a split lip, or a limb too long,

text.NRSV: or one who has a broken foot or a broken hand,
text.Rotherham: nor any man who hath a broken foot,—or a broken hand;
text.KJV: 21:19 Or a man that is broken footed, or broken handed,
text.NABRE: 21:19 or a broken leg or arm,

text.NRSV: or a hunchback, or a dwarf, or a man with a blemish in his eyes or an itching disease or scabs or crushed testicles.
text.Rotherham: or is hump-backed or a dwarf, or hath defective vision,—or hath scurvy or scab, or is crushed in the stones:
text.KJV: 21:20 Or crooke backt, or a dwarfe, or that hath a blemish in his eye, or be scuruy, or scabbed, or hath his stones broken:
text.NABRE: 21:20 or who is a hunchback or dwarf or has a growth in the eye, or who is afflicted with sores, scabs, or crushed testicles.

text.NRSV: No descendant of Aaron the priest who has a blemish shall come near to offer the Lord’s offerings by fire; since he has a blemish, he shall not come near to offer the food of his God.
text.Rotherham: No, man in whom is a blemish of the seed of Aaron the priest, shall approach, to bring near the altar-flames of Yahweh,—a blemish, is in him, the food of his God, shall he not approach to bring near:
text.KJV: 21:21 No man that hath a blemish, of the seed of Aaron the Priest, shall come nigh to offer the offrings of the Lord made by fire: he hath a blemish; he shall not come nigh to offer the bread of his God.
text.NABRE: 21:21 No descendant of Aaron the priest who has any such blemish may draw near to offer the oblations of the LORD; on account of his blemish he may not draw near to offer the food of his God.

text.NRSV: He may eat the food of his God, of the most holy as well as of the holy,
text.Rotherham: Of the food of his God, both of the most holy,—and the holy, may he eat:
text.KJV: 21:22 He shall eat the bread of his God, both of the most Holy, and of the holy:
text.NABRE: 21:22 He may, however, eat the food of his God: of the most sacred as well as sacred offerings.

text.NRSV: but he shall not come near the curtain or approach the altar, because he has a blemish, that he may not profane my sanctuaries; for I am the Lord; I sanctify them.
text.Rotherham: Only, unto the veil, shall he not come in and unto the altar, shall he not approach—because, a blemish, is in him, so shall he not profane my sanctuaries, For, I—Yahweh, am hallowing them.
text.KJV: 21:23 Onely he shall not goe in vnto the Uaile, nor come nigh vnto the Altar, because he hath a blemish, that he prophane not my Sanctuaries: for I the Lord doe sanctifie them.
text.NABRE: 21:23 Only, he may not enter through the veil nor draw near to the altar on account of his blemish; he shall not profane my sacred precincts, for it is I, the LORD, who make them holy.

text.NRSV: Thus Moses spoke to Aaron and to his sons and to all the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Moses spake [these things] unto Aaron, and unto his sons,—and unto all the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 21:24 And Moses told it vnto Aaron, and to his sonnes, and vnto all the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: 21:24 Moses, therefore, told this to Aaron and his sons and to all the Israelites.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 22:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 22:1 Chapter 22 - Priestly Purity. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Direct Aaron and his sons to deal carefully with the sacred donations of the people of Israel, which they dedicate to me, so that they may not profane my holy name; I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron, and unto his sons, That they hold sacredly aloof from the holy things of the sons of Israel, so as not to profane my holy name,—in the things which they are hallowing unto me—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 22:2 Speake vnto Aaron, and to his sonnes, that they separate themselues from the holy things of the children of Israel, and that they prophane not my holy Name, in those things which they halow vnto me: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 22:2 Tell Aaron and his sons to treat with respect the sacred offerings which the Israelites consecrate to me; otherwise they will profane my holy name. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: Say to them: If anyone among all your offspring throughout your generations comes near the sacred donations, which the people of Israel dedicate to the Lord, while he is in a state of uncleanness, that person shall be cut off from my presence: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Say unto them—Unto your generations, as touching any man who cometh near from among any of your seed, unto the hallowed things which the sons of Israel may hallow unto Yahweh, while his uncleanness is upon him, then shall that person be cut off from before me—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 22:3 Say vnto them, Whosoeuer he be of all your seed, among your generations, that goeth vnto the holy things, which the children of Israel hallow vnto the Lord, hauing his vncleannesse vpon him, that soule shalbe cut off from my presence: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 22:3 Tell them: If any one of you, or of your descendants in any future generation, dares, while he is in a state of uncleanness, to draw near the sacred offerings which the Israelites consecrate to the LORD, such a one shall be cut off from my presence. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: No one of Aaron’s offspring who has a leprous disease or suffers a discharge may eat of the sacred donations until he is clean. Whoever touches anything made unclean by a corpse or a man who has had an emission of semen,
text.Rotherham: No man soever of the seed of Aaron who is a leper, or hath a flux, shall eat of the holy things, until he be clean,—And as for him who toucheth anything that is unclean by the dead, or a man whose seed goeth from him;
text.KJV: 22:4 What man soeuer of the seed of Aaron is a leper, or hath a running issue, he shall not eat of the holy things, vntill he be cleane. And who so toucheth any thing that is vncleane by the dead, or a man whose seed goeth from him:
text.NABRE: 22:4 No descendant of Aaron who is stricken with a scaly infection, or who suffers from a genital discharge, may eat of the sacred offerings, until he again becomes clean. Moreover, if anyone touches a person who has become unclean by contact with a corpse, or if anyone has had an emission of semen,

text.NRSV: and whoever touches any swarming thing by which he may be made unclean or any human being by whom he may be made unclean—whatever his uncleanness may be—
text.Rotherham: or a man who toucheth any creeping thing, which is unclean to him,—or [toucheth] any human being who hath uncleanness, to the extent of any thing that maketh him unclean,
text.KJV: 22:5 Or whosoeuer toucheth any creeping thing, whereby he may be made vncleane, or a man of whom hee may take vncleannesse, whatsoeuer vncleannesse he hath:
text.NABRE: 22:5 or if anyone touches any swarming creature whose uncleanness is contagious or any person whose uncleanness, of whatever kind it may be, is contagious—

text.NRSV: the person who touches any such shall be unclean until evening and shall not eat of the sacred donations unless he has washed his body in water.
text.Rotherham: any person who toucheth any such, shall then be unclean until the evening,—and shall not eat of the hallowed things, unless he bathe his flesh in water.
text.KJV: 22:6 The soule which hath touched any such, shalbe vncleane vntill Euen, and shall not eate of the holy things, vnlesse he wash his flesh with water.
text.NABRE: 22:6 the one who touches such as these shall be unclean until evening and may not eat of the sacred portions until he has first bathed his body in water.

text.NRSV: When the sun sets he shall be clean; and afterward he may eat of the sacred donations, for they are his food.
text.Rotherham: When the sun tooth in, then is he clean,—and thereafter, he may eat of the hallowed things, for, his food, it is.
text.KJV: 22:7 And when the Sunne is downe, he shall be cleane, and shall afterward eate of the holy things, because it is his food.
text.NABRE: 22:7 Then, when the sun sets, he shall be clean. Only then may he eat of the sacred offerings, for they are his food.

text.NRSV: That which died or was torn by wild animals he shall not eat, becoming unclean by it: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: That which dieth of itself or is torn in pieces, shall he not eat to make himself unclean thereby,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 22:8 That which dieth of it selfe, or is torne with beasts, hee shall not eate to defile himselfe therewith: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 22:8 He shall not make himself unclean by eating of any animal that has died of itself or has been killed by wild beasts. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: They shall keep my charge, so that they may not incur guilt and die in the sanctuary for having profaned it: I am the Lord; I sanctify them.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall they keep my charge, and shall not bear, for it, sin, and die therein, when they profane it,—I—Yahweh, am hallowing them.
text.KJV: 22:9 They shall therefore keepe mine Ordinance, lest they beare sinne for it, and die therefore, if they prophane it: I the Lord doe sanctifie them.
text.NABRE: 22:9 They shall keep my charge so that they will not bear the punishment in this matter and die for their profanation. I am the LORD who makes them holy.

text.NRSV: No lay person shall eat of the sacred donations. No bound or hired servant of the priest shall eat of the sacred donations;
text.Rotherham: And, no stranger, shall eat what is hallowed,—neither, one who dwelleth with a priest nor a hireling shall eat what is hallowed.
text.KJV: 22:10 There shall no stranger eat of the holy thing; a soiourner of the Priests, or an hired seruant shall not eate of the holy thing.
text.NABRE: 22:10 Neither an unauthorized person nor a priest’s tenant or laborer may eat of any sacred offering.

text.NRSV: but if a priest acquires anyone by purchase, the person may eat of them; and those that are born in his house may eat of his food.
text.Rotherham: But when, a priest, buyeth any person, as the purchase of his silver, he may eat thereof,—and, the children of his household, they, may eat of his food.
text.KJV: 22:11 But if the Priest buy any soule with his money, he shall eat of it, and he that is borne in his house: they shall eat of his meat.
text.NABRE: 22:11 But a slave whom a priest acquires by purchase or who is born in his house may eat of his food.

text.NRSV: If a priest’s daughter marries a layman, she shall not eat of the offering of the sacred donations;
text.Rotherham: And, when, a priest’s daughter, belongeth to a husband, who is a stranger, she, of the heave-offering of the hallowed things, may not eat.
text.KJV: 22:12 If the Priests daughter also bee married vnto a stranger, she may not eate of an offering of the holy things.
text.NABRE: 22:12 A priest’s daughter who is married to an unauthorized person may not eat of the sacred contributions.

text.NRSV: but if a priest’s daughter is widowed or divorced, without offspring, and returns to her father’s house, as in her youth, she may eat of her father’s food. No lay person shall eat of it.
text.Rotherham: But, when a priest’s daughter, cometh to be a widow or divorced and hath no seed, and so she returneth unto the house of her father, as in her youth, of the food of her father, she may eat,—but no stranger, shall eat thereof.
text.KJV: 22:13 But if the Priests daughter be a widow, or diuorced, and haue no childe, and is returned vnto her fathers house, as in her youth, she shall eat of her fathers meat, but there shall no stranger eate thereof.
text.NABRE: 22:13 But if a priest’s daughter is widowed or divorced and, having no children, returns to her father’s house, she may then eat of her father’s food as in her youth. No unauthorized person, however, may eat of it.

text.NRSV: If a man eats of the sacred donation unintentionally, he shall add one-fifth of its value to it, and give the sacred donation to the priest.
text.Rotherham: And, when, any man, eateth what is hallowed, by mistake, then shall he add the fifth part of it thereunto, and give unto the priest the hallowed thing:
text.KJV: 22:14 ¶ And if a man eate of the holy thing vnwittingly, then he shall put the fift part thereof vnto it; and shall giue it vnto the Priest, with the holy thing.
text.NABRE: 22:14 If such a one eats of a sacred offering through inadvertence, that person shall make restitution to the priest for the sacred offering, with an increment of one fifth of the amount.

text.NRSV: No one shall profane the sacred donations of the people of Israel, which they offer to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: So shall they not profane the hallowed things of the sons of Israel,—which they heave up unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: 22:15 And they shall not profane the holy things of the children of Israel, which they offer vnto the Lord:
text.NABRE: 22:15 The priests shall not allow the sacred offerings which the Israelites contribute to the LORD to be profaned

text.NRSV: causing them to bear guilt requiring a guilt offering, by eating their sacred donations: for I am the Lord; I sanctify them.
text.Rotherham: nor cause them to bear guilty iniquity, when they eat their hallowed things,—For, I—Yahweh, am hallowing them.
text.KJV: 22:16 Or suffer them to beare the iniquitie of trespasse, when they eate their holy things: for I the Lord do sanctifie them.
text.NABRE: 22:16 nor make them incur a penalty when they eat their sacred offerings. For I, the LORD, make them holy.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 22:17 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 22:17 Unacceptable Victims. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to Aaron and his sons and all the people of Israel and say to them: When anyone of the house of Israel or of the aliens residing in Israel presents an offering, whether in payment of a vow or as a freewill offering that is offered to the Lord as a burnt offering,
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron, and unto his sons, and unto all the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them—What man, soever, of the house of Israel, or of the sojourners in Israel, may bring near his oblation, as regardeth any of their vows, or any of their freewill offerings which they may bring near unto Yahweh, as an ascending-sacrifice,
text.KJV: 22:18 Speake vnto Aaron and to his sonnes, and vnto all the children of Israel, and say vnto them, Whatsoeuer he be of the house of Israel, or of the strangers in Israel, that will offer his oblation for all his vowes, and for all his free will offerings, which they will offer vnto the Lord for a burnt offering:
text.NABRE: 22:18 Speak to Aaron and his sons and to all the Israelites, and tell them: When anyone of the house of Israel, or any alien residing in Israel, who presents an offering, brings a burnt offering as a votive offering or as a voluntary offering to the LORD,

text.NRSV: to be acceptable in your behalf it shall be a male without blemish, of the cattle or the sheep or the goats.
text.Rotherham: that ye may be accepted, [it must be] a male without defect, of the beeves, or of the sheep, or of the goats:
text.KJV: 22:19 Ye shal offer at your owne wil a male without blemish, of the beeues, of the sheepe, or of the goats.
text.NABRE: 22:19 if it is to be acceptable for you, it must be an unblemished male of the herd, of the sheep or of the goats.

text.NRSV: You shall not offer anything that has a blemish, for it will not be acceptable in your behalf.
text.Rotherham: whatsoever hath in it a blemish, shall ye not bring near,—for it shall not be accepted for you.
text.KJV: 22:20 But whatsoeuer hath a blemish, that shall ye not offer: for it shall not be acceptable for you.
text.NABRE: 22:20 You shall not offer one that has any blemish, for such a one would not be acceptable on your behalf.

text.NRSV: When anyone offers a sacrifice of well-being to the Lord, in fulfillment of a vow or as a freewill offering, from the herd or from the flock, to be acceptable it must be perfect; there shall be no blemish in it.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever would bring near a peace-offering unto Yahweh, to consecrate a vow or as a freewill-offering with a bullock or a sheep, without defect, shall it be, to be accepted, no, blemish, shall be therein.
text.KJV: 22:21 And whosoeuer offereth a sacrifice of peace offerings vnto the Lord, to accomplish his vow, or a free will offring in beeues or sheepe, it shalbe perfect, to be accepted: there shall be no blemish therein.
text.NABRE: 22:21 When anyone presents a communion sacrifice to the LORD from the herd or the flock in fulfillment of a vow, or as a voluntary offering, if it is to find acceptance, it must be unblemished; it shall not have any blemish.

text.NRSV: Anything blind, or injured, or maimed, or having a wen, or mangy, or scabbed—you shall not offer to the Lord or put any of them on the altar as offerings by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Blind, or broken, or rent, or having a running sore, or scurvy, or scab, ye shall not bring these near unto Yahweh,—and no, altar-flame, shall ye present therefrom upon the altar, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 22:22 Blind, or broken, or maimed, or hauing a wenne, or scuruie, or scabbed, ye shal not offer these vnto the Lord, nor make an offring by fire of them vpon the Altar vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 22:22 One that is blind or lame or maimed, or one that has running lesions or sores or scabs, you shall not offer to the LORD; do not put such an animal on the altar as an oblation to the LORD.

text.NRSV: An ox or a lamb that has a limb too long or too short you may present for a freewill offering; but it will not be accepted for a vow.
text.Rotherham: Whether an ox or a lamb long or short limb, as a free-will offering, thou mayest offer it, but, for a vow, it shall not be accepted.
text.KJV: 22:23 Either a bullocke, or a lambe that hath any thing superfluous or lacking in his parts, that mayest thou offer for a free will offring: but for a vow it shal not be accepted.
text.NABRE: 22:23 An ox or a sheep that has a leg that is too long or is stunted you may indeed present as a voluntary offering, but it will not be acceptable as a votive offering.

text.NRSV: Any animal that has its testicles bruised or crushed or torn or cut, you shall not offer to the Lord; such you shall not do within your land,
text.Rotherham: But that which is bruised in the stones or broken therein, or torn or cut, shall ye neither bring ear unto Yahweh, nor on your own land, shall ye offer.
text.KJV: 22:24 Ye shal not offer vnto the Lord that which is bruised, or crushed, or broken, or cut, neither shall you make any offering thereof in your land.
text.NABRE: 22:24 One that has its testicles bruised or crushed or torn out or cut off you shall not offer to the LORD. You shall neither do this in your own land

text.NRSV: nor shall you accept any such animals from a foreigner to offer as food to your God; since they are mutilated, with a blemish in them, they shall not be accepted in your behalf.
text.Rotherham: Even at the hand of the son of a stranger, shall ye not bring near the food of your God of any of these,—for, their damage is in them, a blemish, is in them, they shall not be accepted for you.
text.KJV: 22:25 Neither from a strangers hand shall ye offer the bread of your God of any of these; because their corruption is in them, and blemishes bee in them: they shall not be accepted for you.
text.NABRE: 22:25 nor receive from a foreigner any such animals to offer up as the food of your God; since they are deformed or blemished, they will not be acceptable on your behalf.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:—
text.KJV: 22:26 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 22:26 The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: When an ox or a sheep or a goat is born, it shall remain seven days with its mother, and from the eighth day on it shall be acceptable as the Lord’s offering by fire.
text.Rotherham: When, a beeve or a sheep or a goat, is brought forth, then shall it be seven days under its dam,—and, from the eighth day and onwards, it shall be accepted as an oblation of an altar-flame unto Yahweh,
text.KJV: 22:27 When a bullocke, or a sheepe, or a goat is brought forth, then it shall bee seuen dayes vnder the damme, and from the eight day and thencefoorth, it shal be accepted for an offering made by fire vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 22:27 When an ox or a lamb or a goat is born, it shall remain with its mother for seven days; only from the eighth day onward will it be acceptable, to be offered as an oblation to the LORD.

text.NRSV: But you shall not slaughter, from the herd or the flock, an animal with its young on the same day.
text.Rotherham: Whether cow or ewe—it and its young, shall ye not slaughter on one day.
text.KJV: 22:28 And whether it be cowe or ewe, ye shall not kill it, and her yong, both in one day.
text.NABRE: 22:28 You shall not slaughter an ox or a sheep on one and the same day with its young.

text.NRSV: When you sacrifice a thanksgiving offering to the Lord, you shall sacrifice it so that it may be acceptable in your behalf.
text.Rotherham: And when ye would offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving unto Yahweh, for your acceptance, shall ye offer it.
text.KJV: 22:29 And when yee will offer a sacrifice of thankesgiuing vnto the Lord, offer it at your owne will.
text.NABRE: 22:29 Whenever you offer a thanksgiving sacrifice to the LORD, so offer it that it may be acceptable on your behalf;

text.NRSV: It shall be eaten on the same day; you shall not leave any of it until morning: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: On the same day, shall it be eaten, ye shall not leave thereof until morning,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 22:30 On the same day it shall be eaten vp, ye shall leaue none of it vntill the morrow: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 22:30 it must be eaten on the same day; none of it shall be left over until morning. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: Thus you shall keep my commandments and observe them: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So then ye shall observe my commandments, and do them,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 22:31 Therefore shall ye keepe my Commandements, and doe them: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 22:31 Be careful to observe my commandments. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: You shall not profane my holy name, that I may be sanctified among the people of Israel: I am the Lord; I sanctify you,
text.Rotherham: So shall ye not profane my holy name, So shall I be hallowed in the midst of the sons of Israel,—I, am Yahweh, who am hallowing you;
text.KJV: 22:32 Neither shal ye profane my holy Name, but I will be hallowed among the children of Israel: I am the Lord which hallow you,
text.NABRE: 22:32 Do not profane my holy name, that in the midst of the Israelites I may be hallowed. I, the LORD, make you holy,

text.NRSV: I who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Who brought you forth out of the land of Egypt, that I might become your God,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 22:33 That brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 22:33 who led you out of the land of Egypt to be your God. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 23:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 23:1 Chapter 23 - Holy Days. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: These are the appointed festivals of the Lord that you shall proclaim as holy convocations, my appointed festivals.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel and thou shalt say unto them, As for the appointed seasons of Yahweh, which ye shall proclaim as holy convocations, these, are they—my appointed seasons:—
text.KJV: 23:2 Speake vnto the children of Israel, and say vnto them, Concerning the feasts of the Lord, which yee shall proclaime to be holy conuocations, euen these are my feasts.
text.NABRE: 23:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: The following are the festivals of the LORD, which you shall declare holy days. These are my festivals:

text.NRSV: Six days shall work be done; but the seventh day is a sabbath of complete rest, a holy convocation; you shall do no work: it is a sabbath to the Lord throughout your settlements.
text.Rotherham: Six days, shall work be done, but, on the seventh day, shall there be a sabbath of sacred rest a holy convocation, no work, shall ye do,—a sabbath, shall it be unto Yahweh, in all your dwellings.
text.KJV: 23:3 Sixe dayes shall worke be done, but the seuenth day is the Sabbath of rest, an holy conuocation; ye shall doe no worke therein: it is the Sabbath of the Lord in all your dwellings.
text.NABRE: 23:3 For six days work may be done; but the seventh day is a sabbath of complete rest, a declared holy day; you shall do no work. It is the LORD’s sabbath wherever you dwell.

text.NRSV: These are the appointed festivals of the Lord, the holy convocations, which you shall celebrate at the time appointed for them.
text.Rotherham: These, are the appointed seasons of Yahweh, holy convocations,—which ye shall proclaim in their appointed season:—
text.KJV: 23:4 ¶ These are the feastes of the Lord, euen holy conuocations, which ye shall proclaime in their seasons.
text.NABRE: 23:4 Passover. These are the festivals of the LORD, holy days which you shall declare at their proper time.

text.NRSV: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, at twilight, there shall be a passover offering to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: In the first month, on the fourteenth of the month, between the evenings, is a Passover unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: 23:5 In the fourteenth day of the first moneth at euen, is the Lords Passeouer.
text.NABRE: 23:5 The Passover of the LORD falls on the fourteenth day of the first month, at the evening twilight.

text.NRSV: and on the fifteenth day of the same month is the festival of unleavened bread to the Lord; seven days you shall eat unleavened bread.
text.Rotherham: and on the fifteenth day of this month, is the festival of unleavened cakes unto Yahweh,—seven days—unleavened cakes, shall ye eat.
text.KJV: 23:6 And on the fifteenth day of the same moneth, is the feast of vnleauened bread vnto the Lord: seuen dayes ye must eate vnleauened bread.
text.NABRE: 23:6 The fifteenth day of this month is the LORD’s feast of Unleavened Bread. For seven days you shall eat unleavened bread.

text.NRSV: On the first day you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: On the first day—a holy convocation, shall there be to you: no laborious work, shall ye do.
text.KJV: 23:7 In the first day ye shall haue an holy conuocation: ye shall do no seruile worke therein.
text.NABRE: 23:7 On the first of these days you will have a declared holy day; you shall do no heavy work.

text.NRSV: For seven days you shall present the Lord’s offerings by fire; on the seventh day there shall be a holy convocation: you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: So then ye shall bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh, seven days,—on the seventh day, [shall there be] a holy convocation, no laborious work, shall ye do.
text.KJV: 23:8 But ye shal offer an offring made by fire vnto the Lord seuen dayes: in the seuenth day is an holy conuocation, Ye shall doe no seruile worke therein.
text.NABRE: 23:8 On each of the seven days you shall offer an oblation to the LORD. Then on the seventh day you will have a declared holy day; you shall do no heavy work.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:
text.KJV: 23:9 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 23:9 The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When you enter the land that I am giving you and you reap its harvest, you shall bring the sheaf of the first fruits of your harvest to the priest.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them:—When ye enter into the land which, I, am giving you, and ye reap the harvest thereof, then shall ye bring in a sheaf of the first-ripe corn of your harvest, unto the priest;
text.KJV: 23:10 Speake vnto the children of Israel, and say vnto them, When yee be come into the land which I giue vnto you, and shal reape the haruest thereof, then ye shall bring a sheafe of the first fruits of your haruest vnto the Priest:
text.NABRE: 23:10 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you come into the land which I am giving you, and reap its harvest, you shall bring the first sheaf of your harvest to the priest,

text.NRSV: He shall raise the sheaf before the Lord, that you may find acceptance; on the day after the sabbath the priest shall raise it.
text.Rotherham: and he shall wave the sheaf before Yahweh, that ye may be accepted,—on the morrow of the sabbath, shall the priest wave it.
text.KJV: 23:11 And hee shall waue the sheafe before the Lord to be accepted for you: on the morrow after the Sabbath the Priest shall waue it.
text.NABRE: 23:11 who shall elevate the sheaf before the LORD that it may be acceptable on your behalf. On the day after the sabbath the priest shall do this.

text.NRSV: On the day when you raise the sheaf, you shall offer a lamb a year old, without blemish, as a burnt offering to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall offer on the day when ye wave the sheaf, a he-lamb without defect, the finest of its year for an ascending-sacrifice unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: 23:12 And ye shall offer that day, when ye waue the sheafe, an hee lambe without blemish of the first yeere, for a burnt offering vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 23:12 On this day, when your sheaf is elevated, you shall offer to the LORD for a burnt offering an unblemished yearling lamb.

text.NRSV: And the grain offering with it shall be two-tenths of an ephah of choice flour mixed with oil, an offering by fire of pleasing odor to the Lord; and the drink offering with it shall be of wine, one-fourth of a hin.
text.Rotherham: and the, meal-offering thereof shall be two-tenth parts of fine meal overflowed with oil, as an altar-flame unto Yahweh a satisfying odour,—and, the drink-offering thereof, [shall be] mine, the fourth of a hin.
text.KJV: 23:13 And the meat offring thereof shall be two tenth deales of fine flowre, mingled with oile, an offering made by fire vnto the Lord, for a sweet sauour: and the drinke offering thereof shalbe of wine, the fourth part of an Hin.
text.NABRE: 23:13 Its grain offering shall be two tenths of an ephah of bran flour mixed with oil, as a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD; and its libation shall be a fourth of a hin of wine.

text.NRSV: You shall eat no bread or parched grain or fresh ears until that very day, until you have brought the offering of your God: it is a statute forever throughout your generations in all your settlements.
text.Rotherham: And neither bread, nor roasted corn, nor garden-land grain, shall ye eat, until this selfsame day, until ye have brought in the oblation of your God,—an age-abiding statute unto your generations, in all your dwellings.
text.KJV: 23:14 And ye shall eate neither bread, nor parched corne, nor greene eares, vntill the selfe same day that yee haue brought an offering vnto your God: It shalbe a statute for euer, throughout your generations, in all your dwellings.
text.NABRE: 23:14 You shall not eat any bread or roasted grain or fresh kernels until this day, when you bring the offering for your God. This shall be a perpetual statute throughout your generations wherever you dwell.

text.NRSV: And from the day after the sabbath, from the day on which you bring the sheaf of the elevation offering, you shall count off seven weeks; they shall be complete.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye keep count to yourselves from the morrow of the sabbath, from the day ye brought in the wave sheaf,—seven sabbaths complete, shall there be:
text.KJV: 23:15 ¶ And ye shall count vnto you from the morrow after the Sabbath, from the day that ye brought the sheafe of the waue offering; seuen Sabbaths shalbe complete.
text.NABRE: 23:15 Pentecost. Beginning with the day after the sabbath, the day on which you bring the sheaf for elevation, you shall count seven full weeks;

text.NRSV: You shall count until the day after the seventh sabbath, fifty days; then you shall present an offering of new grain to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: until on the morrow of the seventh sabbath, ye count fifty days,—then shall ye bring near a new meal-offering unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 23:16 Euen vnto the morrow after the seuenth Sabbath, shall ye number fifty dayes, and ye shall offer a new meat offering vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 23:16 you shall count to the day after the seventh week, fifty days. Then you shall present a new grain offering to the LORD.

text.NRSV: You shall bring from your settlements two loaves of bread as an elevation offering, each made of two-tenths of an ephah; they shall be of choice flour, baked with leaven, as first fruits to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Out of your dwellings, shall ye bring in two wave loaves of two-tenths of an ephah, of fine meal, shall they be, with leaven, shall they be baked,—first-fruits unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 23:17 Ye shall bring out of your habitations two waue-loaues, of two tenth deales: they shalbe of fine flowre, they shall be baken with leauen, they are the first fruits vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 23:17 For the elevated offering of your first-ripened fruits to the LORD, you shall bring with you from wherever you live two loaves of bread made of two tenths of an ephah of bran flour and baked with leaven.

text.NRSV: You shall present with the bread seven lambs a year old without blemish, one young bull, and two rams; they shall be a burnt offering to the Lord, along with their grain offering and their drink offerings, an offering by fire of pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye bring near with the bread seven he-lambs without defect of the first year, and one young bullock, and two rams,—they shall be an ascending-sacrifice unto Yahweh, with their meal-offering, and their drink-offerings, an altar-flame of satisfying odour unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 23:18 And ye shall offer with the bread seuen lambes without blemish, of the first yeere, and one yong bullocke and two rammes: they shall be for a burnt offering vnto the Lord, with their meat offring and their drinke offrings, euen an offering made by fire of sweet sauour vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 23:18 Besides the bread, you shall offer to the LORD a burnt offering of seven unblemished yearling lambs, one bull of the herd, and two rams, along with their grain offering and libations, as a sweet-smelling oblation to the LORD.

text.NRSV: You shall also offer one male goat for a sin offering, and two male lambs a year old as a sacrifice of well-being.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye offer one he-goat, as a sin-bearer,—and two he-lambs, of the first year, as a peace-offering.
text.KJV: 23:19 Then ye shall sacrifice one kid of the goates, for a sinne offering, and two lambes of the first yeere, for a sacrifice of peace offerings.
text.NABRE: 23:19 One male goat shall be sacrificed as a purification offering, and two yearling lambs as a communion sacrifice.

text.NRSV: The priest shall raise them with the bread of the first fruits as an elevation offering before the Lord, together with the two lambs; they shall be holy to the Lord for the priest.
text.Rotherham: And the priest shall wave them, with the firstfruits’ bread, as a wave-offering before Yahweh, with the two he-lambs,—holy, shall they be unto Yahweh, for the priest.
text.KJV: 23:20 And the Priest shall waue them with the bread of the first fruits, for a waue-offring before the Lord, with the two lambs: they shalbe holy to the Lord for the Priests.
text.NABRE: 23:20 The priest shall elevate them—that is, the two lambs—with the bread of the first-ripened fruits as an elevated offering before the LORD; these shall be sacred to the LORD and belong to the priest.

text.NRSV: On that same day you shall make proclamation; you shall hold a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations. This is a statute forever in all your settlements throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall make proclamation on this self-same day—a holy convocation, shall it be unto you; no laborious work, shall ye do,—an age-abiding statute in all your dwellings, unto your generations.
text.KJV: 23:21 And ye shal proclaime on the selfe same day, that it may be an holy conuocation vnto you: ye shall doe no seruile worke therein: it shall be a statute for euer in all your dwellings throughout your generations.
text.NABRE: 23:21 On this same day you shall make a proclamation: there shall be a declared holy day for you; no heavy work may be done. This shall be a perpetual statute through all your generations wherever you dwell.

text.NRSV: When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not reap to the very edges of your field, or gather the gleanings of your harvest; you shall leave them for the poor and for the alien: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And, when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not wholly clear the border of thy field when thou reapest, and, the gleanings of thy field, shalt thou not glean,—for the poor and for the sojourner, shalt thou leave them. I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 23:22 ¶ And when ye reape the haruest of your land, thou shalt not make cleane riddance of the corners of the field, when thou reapest, neither shalt thou gather any gleaning of thy haruest: thou shalt leaue them vnto the poore, and to the stranger: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 23:22 When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not be so thorough that you reap the field to its very edge, nor shall you gather the gleanings of your harvest. These things you shall leave for the poor and the alien. I, the LORD, am your God.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 23:23 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 23:23 New Year’s Day. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: In the seventh month, on the first day of the month, you shall observe a day of complete rest, a holy convocation commemorated with trumpet blasts.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, saying:—In the seventh month on the first of the month, shall there be unto you a sacred rest, a trumpet-blowing commemoration a holy convocation:
text.KJV: 23:24 Speake vnto the children of Israel, saying, In the seuenth moneth, in the first day of the moneth shall yee haue a Sabbath, a memoriall of blowing of trumpets, an holy conuocation.
text.NABRE: 23:24 Tell the Israelites: On the first day of the seventh month you will have a sabbath rest, with trumpet blasts as a reminder, a declared holy day;

text.NRSV: You shall not work at your occupations; and you shall present the Lord’s offering by fire.
text.Rotherham: no laborious work, shall ye do,—but ye shall bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 23:25 Ye shall do no seruile worke therein; but ye shall offer an offering made by fire vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 23:25 you shall do no heavy work, and you shall offer an oblation to the LORD.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying,
text.KJV: 23:26 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 23:26 The Day of Atonement. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Now, the tenth day of this seventh month is the day of atonement; it shall be a holy convocation for you: you shall deny yourselves and present the Lord’s offering by fire;
text.Rotherham: Surely on the tenth of this seventh month, is, the Day of Propitiation, a holy convocation, shall it be to you, therefore shall ye humble your souls,—and bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 23:27 Also on the tenth day of this seuenth moneth, there shalbe a day of atonement, it shalbe an holy conuocation vnto you, & ye shall afflict your soules, and offer an offering made by fire vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 23:27 Now the tenth day of this seventh month is the Day of Atonement. You will have a declared holy day. You shall humble yourselves and offer an oblation to the LORD.

text.NRSV: you shall do no work during that entire day; for it is a day of atonement, to make atonement on your behalf before the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And, no work, shall ye do, on this selfsame day,—for a day of propitiation, it is, to put a propitiatory-covering over you before Yahweh your God.
text.KJV: 23:28 And ye shall doe no worke in that same day: for it is a day of atonement, to make an atonement for you, before the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 23:28 On this day you shall not do any work, because it is the Day of Atonement, when atonement is made for you before the LORD, your God.

text.NRSV: For any person who is not denied in self on that very day shall be cut off from the people.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever be the person that shall not be humbled on this selfsame day, then shall he be cut off from among his kinsfolk.
text.KJV: 23:29 For whatsoeuer soule it bee that shall not bee afflicted in that same day, hee shall bee cut off from among his people.
text.NABRE: 23:29 Those who do not humble themselves on this day shall be cut off from the people.

text.NRSV: And any person who does any work during that entire day, such a person I will destroy from the midst of the people.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever be the person that shall do any work on this selfsame day, then will I destroy that person from the midst of his people.
text.KJV: 23:30 And whatsoeuer soule it bee that doeth any worke in that same day, the same soule will I destroy from among his people.
text.NABRE: 23:30 If anyone does any work on this day, I will remove that person from the midst of the people.

text.NRSV: You shall do no work: it is a statute forever throughout your generations in all your settlements.
text.Rotherham: No work, shall ye do,—an age-abiding statute to your generations, in all your dwellings.
text.KJV: 23:31 Ye shall doe no maner of worke: it shall be a statute for euer throughout your generations, in all your dwellings.
text.NABRE: 23:31 You shall do no work; this is a perpetual statute throughout your generations wherever you dwell;

text.NRSV: It shall be to you a sabbath of complete rest, and you shall deny yourselves; on the ninth day of the month at evening, from evening to evening you shall keep your sabbath.
text.Rotherham: A sabbath of sacred rest, shall it be unto you, so shall ye humble your souls,—on the ninth of the month in the evening, from evening to evening, shall ye keep your sabbath.
text.KJV: 23:32 It shalbe vnto you a Sabbath of rest, and yee shall afflict your soules in the ninth day of the moneth at Euen, from Euen vnto Euen shall ye celebrate your Sabbath.
text.NABRE: 23:32 it is a sabbath of complete rest for you. You shall humble yourselves. Beginning on the evening of the ninth of the month, you shall keep your sabbath from evening to evening.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 23:33 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 23:33 The Feast of Booths. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: On the fifteenth day of this seventh month, and lasting seven days, there shall be the festival of booths to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, saying:—On the fifteenth day of this seventh month, shall be the festival of booths, for seven days, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 23:34 Speake vnto the children of Israel, saying, The fifteenth day of this seuenth moneth, shall be the feast of Tabernacles for seuen dayes vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 23:34 Tell the Israelites: The fifteenth day of this seventh month is the LORD’s feast of Booths, which shall continue for seven days.

text.NRSV: The first day shall be a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: On the first day, [shall be] a holy convocation,—no laborious work, shall ye do.
text.KJV: 23:35 On the first day shalbe an holy conuocation: ye shall doe no seruile worke therein.
text.NABRE: 23:35 On the first day, a declared holy day, you shall do no heavy work.

text.NRSV: Seven days you shall present the Lord’s offerings by fire; on the eighth day you shall observe a holy convocation and present the Lord’s offerings by fire; it is a solemn assembly; you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: Seven days, shall ye bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh: on the eighth day—a holy convocation, shall there be unto you so shall ye bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh—the closing of the feast, it is, no laborious work, shall ye do.
text.KJV: 23:36 Seuen dayes ye shall offer an offring made by fire vnto the Lord, on the eight day shall be an holy conuocation vnto you, and ye shall offer an offering made by fire vnto the Lord: It is a solemne assembly, and ye shall doe no seruile worke therein.
text.NABRE: 23:36 For seven days you shall offer an oblation to the LORD, and on the eighth day you will have a declared holy day. You shall offer an oblation to the LORD. It is the festival closing. You shall do no heavy work.

text.NRSV: These are the appointed festivals of the Lord, which you shall celebrate as times of holy convocation, for presenting to the Lord offerings by fire—burnt offerings and grain offerings, sacrifices and drink offerings, each on its proper day—
text.Rotherham: These, are the appointed seasons of Yahweh, which ye shall proclaim, as holy convocations,—for bringing near as an altar-flame unto Yahweh, an ascending-sacrifice, and a meal-offering, a sacrifice and a drink-offering, each day’s appointment on its own day:—
text.KJV: 23:37 These are the feasts of the Lord which ye shall proclaime to be holy conuocations, to offer an offering made by fire vnto the Lord, a burnt offering, and a meat offering, a sacrifice, & drinke offerings, euery thing vpon his day;
text.NABRE: 23:37 These, therefore, are the festivals of the LORD which you shall declare holy days, in order to offer as an oblation to the LORD burnt offerings and grain offerings, sacrifices and libations, as prescribed for each day,

text.NRSV: apart from the sabbaths of the Lord, and apart from your gifts, and apart from all your votive offerings, and apart from all your freewill offerings, which you give to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: besides the sabbaths of Yahweh, and besides your presents, and besides all your vows, and besides all your freewill offerings which ye shall give unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 23:38 Beside the Sabbaths of the Lord, and beside your gifts, and beside all your vowes, and beside all your free will offerings, which ye giue vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 23:38 in addition to the LORD’s sabbaths, your donations, your various votive offerings, and the voluntary offerings that you present to the LORD.

text.NRSV: Now, the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when you have gathered in the produce of the land, you shall keep the festival of the Lord, lasting seven days; a complete rest on the first day, and a complete rest on the eighth day.
text.Rotherham: Surely, on the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when ye have gathered in the yield of the land, shall ye celebrate the festival of Yahweh seven days,—on the first day, a sacred rest, and, on the eighth day, a sacred rest.
text.KJV: 23:39 Also in the fifteenth day of the seuenth moneth when yee haue gathered in the fruit of the land, ye shall keepe a feast vnto the Lord seuen dayes. On the first day shall bee a Sabbath, and on the eight day shall bee a Sabbath.
text.NABRE: 23:39 On the fifteenth day, then, of the seventh month, when you have gathered in the produce of the land, you shall celebrate the feast of the LORD for a whole week. The first and the eighth day shall be days of rest.

text.NRSV: On the first day you shall take the fruit of majestic trees, branches of palm trees, boughs of leafy trees, and willows of the brook; and you shall rejoice before the Lord your God for seven days.
text.Rotherham: So then ye shall take you, on the first day, boughs of goodly trees, branches, of palm-trees, and boughs of thick trees, and poplars of the ravine,—and shall rejoice before Yahweh your God, seven days.
text.KJV: 23:40 And ye shall take you on the first day the boughes of goodly trees, branches of Palme trees, and the boughes of thicke trees, and willowes of the brooke, and yee shall reioyce before the Lord your God seuen dayes.
text.NABRE: 23:40 On the first day you shall gather fruit of majestic trees, branches of palms, and boughs of leafy trees and valley willows. Then for a week you shall make merry before the LORD, your God.

text.NRSV: You shall keep it as a festival to the Lord seven days in the year; you shall keep it in the seventh month as a statute forever throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye celebrate it as a festival unto Yahweh, seven days in the year,—a statute age-abiding, to your generations. In the seventh month, shall ye celebrate it;
text.KJV: 23:41 And yee shall keepe it a feast vnto the Lord seuen dayes in the yeere: It shalbe a Statute for euer in your generations, ye shall celebrate it in the seuenth moneth.
text.NABRE: 23:41 You shall keep this feast of the LORD for one whole week in the year. By perpetual statute throughout your generations in the seventh month of the year, you shall keep it.

text.NRSV: You shall live in booths for seven days; all citizens in Israel shall live in booths,
text.Rotherham: In booths, shall ye dwell seven days;—All the home-born in Israel, shall dwell in booths:
text.KJV: 23:42 Ye shall dwell in boothes seuen dayes: all that are Israelites borne, shall dwell in boothes;
text.NABRE: 23:42 You shall dwell in booths for seven days; every native-born Israelite shall dwell in booths,

text.NRSV: so that your generations may know that I made the people of Israel live in booths when I brought them out of the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: To the end that your generations may know, that, in booths, made I the sons of Israel to dwell, when I brought them forth out of the land of Egypt,—I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 23:43 That your generations may know that I made the children of Israel to dwell in boothes, when I brought them out of the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 23:43 that your descendants may realize that, when I led the Israelites out of the land of Egypt, I made them dwell in booths. I, the LORD, am your God.

text.NRSV: Thus Moses declared to the people of Israel the appointed festivals of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses declared, the appointed seasons of Yahweh, unto the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 23:44 And Moses declared vnto the children of Israel the feastes of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 23:44 Thus did Moses announce to the Israelites the festivals of the LORD.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 24:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 24:1 Chapter 24 - The Sanctuary Light. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Command the people of Israel to bring you pure oil of beaten olives for the lamp, that a light may be kept burning regularly.
text.Rotherham: Command the sons of Israel, That they bring unto thee—pure oil olive, beaten, for giving light,—to cause the lamp to burn up continually.
text.KJV: 24:2 Command the children of Israel, that they bring vnto thee pure oyle Oliue, beaten, for the light, to cause the lampes to burne continually.
text.NABRE: 24:2 Order the Israelites to bring you clear oil of crushed olives for the light, so that you may keep the lamp burning regularly.

text.NRSV: Aaron shall set it up in the tent of meeting, outside the curtain of the covenant, to burn from evening to morning before the Lord regularly; it shall be a statute forever throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: Outside the veil of the testimony, in the tent of meeting, shall Aaron order it, from evening until morning, before Yahweh continually,—a statute age-abiding, to your generations.
text.KJV: 24:3 Without the Uaile of the Testimonie, in the Tabernacle of the Congregation, shal Aaron order it from the euening vnto the morning, before the Lord continually: It shall be a Statute for euer in your generations.
text.NABRE: 24:3 In the tent of meeting, outside the veil that hangs in front of the covenant, Aaron shall set up the lamp to burn before the LORD regularly, from evening till morning, by a perpetual statute throughout your generations.

text.NRSV: He shall set up the lamps on the lampstand of pure gold before the Lord regularly.
text.Rotherham: Upon the pure lampstand, shall he order the lamps,—before Yahweh, continually.
text.KJV: 24:4 He shall order the lampes vpon the pure Candlesticke before the Lord continually.
text.NABRE: 24:4 He shall set up the lamps on the pure gold menorah to burn regularly before the LORD. The Showbread.

text.NRSV: You shall take choice flour, and bake twelve loaves of it; two-tenths of an ephah shall be in each loaf.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt take fine meal, and bake it, in twelve cakes,—of two-tenths, shall each cake be.
text.KJV: 24:5 ¶ And thou shalt take fine flowre, and bake twelue cakes thereof: two tenth deales shall be in one cake.
text.NABRE: 24:5 You shall take bran flour and bake it into twelve cakes, using two tenths of an ephah of flour for each cake.

text.NRSV: You shall place them in two rows, six in a row, on the table of pure gold.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt set them in two rows, six in a row,—upon the pure table, before Yahweh.
text.KJV: 24:6 And thou shalt set them in two rowes, sixe on a row vpon the pure Table, before the Lord.
text.NABRE: 24:6 These you shall place in two piles, six in each pile, on the pure gold table before the LORD.

text.NRSV: You shall put pure frankincense with each row, to be a token offering for the bread, as an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt put upon each row pure frankincense,—so shall it belong unto the bread as a memorial, an altar-flame unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 24:7 And thou shalt put pure frankincense vpon ech row, that it may bee on the bread for a memorial, euen an offering made by fire vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 24:7 With each pile put some pure frankincense, which shall serve as an oblation to the LORD, a token of the bread offering.

text.NRSV: Every sabbath day Aaron shall set them in order before the Lord regularly as a commitment of the people of Israel, as a covenant forever.
text.Rotherham: Sabbath day by sabbath day, shall he order it before Yahweh continually,—from the sons of Israel as an age-abiding covenant:
text.KJV: 24:8 Euery Sabbath he shall set it in order before the Lord continually, being taken from the children of Israel by an euerlasting couenant.
text.NABRE: 24:8 Regularly on each sabbath day the bread shall be set out before the LORD on behalf of the Israelites by an everlasting covenant.

text.NRSV: They shall be for Aaron and his descendants, who shall eat them in a holy place, for they are most holy portions for him from the offerings by fire to the Lord, a perpetual due.
text.Rotherham: so shall it be for Aaron and for his sons, and they shall eat it in a holy place,—for most holy, shall it be unto him from among the altar-flames of Yahweh, a statute age-abiding.
text.KJV: 24:9 And it shall be Aarons and his sonnes, and they shall eate it in the holy place: for it is most holy vnto him, of the offerings of the Lord made by fire, by a perpetuall statute.
text.NABRE: 24:9 It shall belong to Aaron and his sons, who must eat it in a sacred place, since it is most sacred, his as a perpetual due from the oblations to the LORD. Punishment of Blasphemy.

text.NRSV: A man whose mother was an Israelite and whose father was an Egyptian came out among the people of Israel; and the Israelite woman’s son and a certain Israelite began fighting in the camp.
text.Rotherham: And forth went the son of a woman of Israel—he being the son of a man of Egypt—in the midst of the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: 24:10 ¶ And the sonne of an Israelitish woman, whose father was an Egyptian, went out among the children of Israel: and this sonne of the Israelitish woman, and a man of Israel stroue together in the campe.
text.NABRE: 24:10 A man born of an Israelite mother and an Egyptian father went out among the Israelites, and in the camp a fight broke out between the son of the Israelite woman and an Israelite man.

text.NRSV: The Israelite woman’s son blasphemed the Name in a curse. And they brought him to Moses—his mother’s name was Shelomith daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan—
text.Rotherham: and the son of the woman of Israel, and a man of Israel, reviled one another in the camp. And the son of the Israelitish woman uttered contemptuously the Name, and reviled. So they brought him in unto Moses,—now, the name of his mother, was Shelomith, daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan;
text.KJV: 24:11 And the Israelitish womans sonne blasphemed the name of the Lord, and cursed, and they brought him vnto Moses: and his mothers name was Shelomith, the daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan.
text.NABRE: 24:11 The son of the Israelite woman uttered the LORD’s name in a curse and blasphemed. So he was brought to Moses—now his mother’s name was Shelomith, daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan—

text.NRSV: and they put him in custody, until the decision of the Lord should be made clear to them.
text.Rotherham: and they put him in ward,—that a clear answer might be given to them at the mouth of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 24:12 And they put him in ward, that the minde of the Lord might bee shewed them.
text.NABRE: 24:12 and he was kept in custody till a decision from the LORD should settle the case for them.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 24:13 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 24:13 The LORD then said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Take the blasphemer outside the camp; and let all who were within hearing lay their hands on his head, and let the whole congregation stone him.
text.Rotherham: Bring forth him that reviled unto the outside of the camp, then shall all that heard [him] lean their hands upon his head,—and all the assembly shall stone him.
text.KJV: 24:14 Bring forth him that hath cursed, without the Campe, and let all that heard him, lay their hands vpon his head, and let all the Congregation stone him.
text.NABRE: 24:14 Take the blasphemer outside the camp, and when all who heard him have laid their hands on his head, let the whole community stone him.

text.NRSV: And speak to the people of Israel, saying: Anyone who curses God shall bear the sin.
text.Rotherham: And, unto the sons of Israel, shalt thou speak saying,—What man soever curseth his God shall bear his sin.
text.KJV: 24:15 And thou shalt speake vnto the children of Israel, saying, Whosoeuer curseth his God, shall beare his sinne.
text.NABRE: 24:15 Tell the Israelites: Anyone who blasphemes God shall bear the penalty;

text.NRSV: One who blasphemes the name of the Lord shall be put to death; the whole congregation shall stone the blasphemer. Aliens as well as citizens, when they blaspheme the Name, shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And, he that contemptuously uttereth the name of Yahweh, shall be, surely put to death, all the assembly shall, surely stone, him,—as the sojourner so the home-born, when he contemptuously uttereth the Name, he shall be put to death.
text.KJV: 24:16 And hee that blasphemeth the Name of the Lord, he shall surely be put to death, and all the Congregation shall certainely stone him: Aswell the stranger, as he that is borne in the land, when he blasphemeth the Name of the Lord, shall be put to death.
text.NABRE: 24:16 whoever utters the name of the LORD in a curse shall be put to death. The whole community shall stone that person; alien and native-born alike must be put to death for uttering the LORD’s name in a curse.

text.NRSV: Anyone who kills a human being shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And, when, any man, by smiting taketh the life of any human being, he shall be surely put to death,
text.KJV: 24:17 ¶ And he that killeth any man, shall surely be put to death.
text.NABRE: 24:17 Whoever takes the life of any human being shall be put to death;

text.NRSV: Anyone who kills an animal shall make restitution for it, life for life.
text.Rotherham: And he that by smiting taketh away the life of a beast, shall make it good,—life for life.
text.KJV: 24:18 And he that killeth a beast, shall make it good; beast for beast.
text.NABRE: 24:18 whoever takes the life of an animal shall make restitution of another animal, life for a life.

text.NRSV: Anyone who maims another shall suffer the same injury in return:
text.Rotherham: And, when, any man, inflicteth a blemish upon his neighbour, as he hath done, so, shall it be done to him:
text.KJV: 24:19 And if a man cause a blemish in his neighbour; as he hath done, so shal it be done to him:
text.NABRE: 24:19 Anyone who inflicts a permanent injury on his or her neighbor shall receive the same in return:

text.NRSV: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth; the injury inflicted is the injury to be suffered.
text.Rotherham: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, as he inflicteth a blemish upon a human being, so, shall one be inflicted upon him.
text.KJV: 24:20 Breach, for breach, eye for eye, tooth for tooth: as he hath caused a blemish in a man, so shall it be done to him againe.
text.NABRE: 24:20 fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth. The same injury that one gives another shall be inflicted in return.

text.NRSV: One who kills an animal shall make restitution for it; but one who kills a human being shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And he that killeth a beast, shall make it good,-and he that killeth a human being, shall be put to death.
text.KJV: 24:21 And hee that killeth a beast, hee shall restore it: and hee that killeth a man, he shall be put to death.
text.NABRE: 24:21 Whoever takes the life of an animal shall make restitution, but whoever takes a human life shall be put to death.

text.NRSV: You shall have one law for the alien and for the citizen: for I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: One rule, shall ye have, as the sojourner, so the home-born, shall be,—For, I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 24:22 Ye shall haue one maner of law, aswell for the stranger, as for one of your owne countrey: for I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 24:22 You shall have but one rule, for alien and native-born alike. I, the LORD, am your God.

text.NRSV: Moses spoke thus to the people of Israel; and they took the blasphemer outside the camp, and stoned him to death. The people of Israel did as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses spake unto the sons of Israel, and they took forth the reviler, unto the outside of the camp, and stoned him with stones. Thus, the sons of Israel did, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: 24:23 ¶ And Moses spake to the children of Israel, that they should bring foorth him that had cursed, out of the Campe, and stone him with stones: and the children of Israel did as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: 24:23 When Moses told this to the Israelites, they took the blasphemer outside the camp and stoned him; they did just as the LORD commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses in Mount Sinai, saying—
text.KJV: 25:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses in Mount Sinai, saying,
text.NABRE: 25:1 Chapter 25 - The Sabbatical Year. The LORD said to Moses on Mount Sinai:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When you enter the land that I am giving you, the land shall observe a sabbath for the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them:—When ye enter into the land which, I, am giving you, then shall the land keep a sabbath unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 25:2 Speake vnto the children of Israel, and say vnto them: when yee come into the land which I giue you, then shall the land keepe a Sabbath vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 25:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you enter the land that I am giving you, let the land, too, keep a sabbath for the LORD.

text.NRSV: Six years you shall sow your field, and six years you shall prune your vineyard, and gather in their yield;
text.Rotherham: Six years, shalt thou sow thy field, and, six years, shalt thou prune thy vineyard,—and gather the increase thereof;
text.KJV: 25:3 Sixe yeeres thou shalt sow thy field, and sixe yeeres thou shalt prune thy Uineyard, and gather in the fruit thereof.
text.NABRE: 25:3 For six years you may sow your field, and for six years prune your vineyard, gathering in their produce.

text.NRSV: but in the seventh year there shall be a sabbath of complete rest for the land, a sabbath for the Lord: you shall not sow your field or prune your vineyard.
text.Rotherham: but, in the seventh year—a sabbath of sacred rest, shall there be unto the land, a sabbath unto Yahweh: thy field, shalt thou not sow, and, thy vineyard, shalt thou not prune;
text.KJV: 25:4 But in the seuenth yeere shalbe a Sabbath of rest vnto the land, a Sabbath for the Lord: thou shalt neither sow thy field, nor prune thy Uineyard.
text.NABRE: 25:4 But during the seventh year the land shall have a sabbath of complete rest, a sabbath for the LORD, when you may neither sow your field nor prune your vineyard.

text.NRSV: You shall not reap the aftergrowth of your harvest or gather the grapes of your unpruned vine: it shall be a year of complete rest for the land.
text.Rotherham: that which groweth of itself of thy harvest, shalt thou not reap; and the grapes of thine unpruned vines, shalt thou not cut off: a year of sacred rest, shall there be to the land.
text.KJV: 25:5 That which groweth of it owne accord of thy haruest, thou shalt not reape, neither gather the grapes of thy Uine vndressed: for it is a yeere of rest vnto the land.
text.NABRE: 25:5 The aftergrowth of your harvest you shall not reap, nor shall you pick the grapes of your untrimmed vines. It shall be a year of rest for the land.

text.NRSV: You may eat what the land yields during its sabbath—you, your male and female slaves, your hired and your bound laborers who live with you;
text.Rotherham: So shall the sabbath of the land be unto you for food: unto thee, and unto thy servant and unto thy handmaid,—and unto thy hireling, and unto thy settlers that are sojourning with thee;
text.KJV: 25:6 And the Sabbath of the land shall be meat for you; for thee, and for thy seruant, and for thy mayd, and for thy hired seruant, and for the stranger that soiourneth with thee,
text.NABRE: 25:6 While the land has its sabbath, all its produce will be food to eat for you yourself and for your male and female slave, for your laborer and the tenant who live with you,

text.NRSV: for your livestock also, and for the wild animals in your land all its yield shall be for food.
text.Rotherham: and unto thy tame-beasts, and unto the wild-beasts that are in thy land, shall belong all the increase thereof for food.
text.KJV: 25:7 And for thy cattel, and for the beast that are in thy land, shall all the encrease thereof be meat.
text.NABRE: 25:7 and likewise for your livestock and for the wild animals on your land.

text.NRSV: You shall count off seven weeks of years, seven times seven years, so that the period of seven weeks of years gives forty-nine years.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt count to thee seven weeks of years, seven years, seven times,—so shall the days of the seven weeks of years become to thee forty-nine years.
text.KJV: 25:8 ¶ And thou shalt number seuen Sabbaths of yeeres vnto thee, seuen times seuen yeeres, and the space of the seuen Sabbaths of yeeres, shall be vnto thee fourtie and nine yeeres.
text.NABRE: 25:8 The Jubilee Year. You shall count seven weeks of years—seven times seven years—such that the seven weeks of years amount to forty-nine years.

text.NRSV: Then you shall have the trumpet sounded loud; on the tenth day of the seventh month—on the day of atonement—you shall have the trumpet sounded throughout all your land
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou cause a signal-horn to pass through in the seventh month, on the tenth of the month: on the Day of Propitiation, shall ye cause a horn to pass throughout all your land.
text.KJV: 25:9 Then shalt thou cause the trumpet of the Iubile to sound, on the tenth day of the seuenth moneth; in the day of atonement shall ye make the trumpet sound throughout all your land.
text.NABRE: 25:9 Then, on the tenth day of the seventh month let the ram’s horn resound; on this, the Day of Atonement, the ram’s horn blast shall resound throughout your land.

text.NRSV: and you shall hallow the fiftieth year and you shall proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee for you: you shall return every one of you to your property and every one of you to your family.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye hallow the fiftieth year, and proclaim freedom throughout the land to all the dwellers thereof,—a jubilee, shall it be unto you, and ye shall return, every man unto his possession, and every man unto his family, shall ye return.
text.KJV: 25:10 And ye shall hallow the fiftieth yeere, and proclaime libertie throughout all the land, vnto al the inhabitants thereof: It shalbe a Iubile vnto you, and ye shall returne euery man vnto his possession, and ye shall returne euery man vnto his family.
text.NABRE: 25:10 You shall treat this fiftieth year as sacred. You shall proclaim liberty in the land for all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee for you, when each of you shall return to your own property, each of you to your own family.

text.NRSV: That fiftieth year shall be a jubilee for you: you shall not sow, or reap the aftergrowth, or harvest the unpruned vines.
text.Rotherham: A jubilee, shall that fiftieth year be unto you,—ye shall not sow, neither shall ye reap the self-grown corn thereof, nor cut off the grapes of the unpruned vines thereof.
text.KJV: 25:11 A Iubile shall that fiftieth yeere be vnto you: Ye shall not sow, neither reape that which groweth of it selfe in it, nor gather the grapes in it of thy Uine vndressed.
text.NABRE: 25:11 This fiftieth year is your year of jubilee; you shall not sow, nor shall you reap the aftergrowth or pick the untrimmed vines,

text.NRSV: For it is a jubilee; it shall be holy to you: you shall eat only what the field itself produces.
text.Rotherham: For, a jubilee, it is, holy, shall it be unto you,—out of the field, shall ye eat her increase.
text.KJV: 25:12 For it is the Iubile, it shall be holy vnto you: ye shall eate the encrease thereof out of the field.
text.NABRE: 25:12 since this is the jubilee. It shall be sacred for you. You may only eat what the field yields of itself.

text.NRSV: In this year of jubilee you shall return every one of you to your property.
text.Rotherham: In this same jubilee year, shall ye return every man unto his possession.
text.KJV: 25:13 In the yeere of this Iubile yee shall returne euery man vnto his possession.
text.NABRE: 25:13 In this year of jubilee, then, each of you shall return to your own property.

text.NRSV: When you make a sale to your neighbor or buy from your neighbor, you shall not cheat one another.
text.Rotherham: And when ye sell anything to thy neighbour, or buy aught at thy neighbour’s hand, do not overreach one another.
text.KJV: 25:14 And if thou sell ought vnto thy neighbour, or buyest ought of thy neighbours hand, ye shall not oppresse one another.
text.NABRE: 25:14 Therefore, when you sell any land to your neighbor or buy any from your neighbor, do not deal unfairly with one another.

text.NRSV: When you buy from your neighbor, you shall pay only for the number of years since the jubilee; the seller shall charge you only for the remaining crop years.
text.Rotherham: By the number of years after the jubilee, shalt thou buy of thy neighbour,—by the number of the years of increase, shall he sell unto thee;
text.KJV: 25:15 According to the number of yeres after the Iubile, thou shalt buy of thy neighbour, and according vnto the number of yeeres of the fruits, he shall sell vnto thee.
text.NABRE: 25:15 On the basis of the number of years since the last jubilee you shall purchase the land from your neighbor; and so also, on the basis of the number of years of harvest, that person shall sell it to you.

text.NRSV: If the years are more, you shall increase the price, and if the years are fewer, you shall diminish the price; for it is a certain number of harvests that are being sold to you.
text.Rotherham: according to the multitude of the years, shalt thou increase the price thereof, and, according to the fewness of the years, shalt thou diminish the price thereof,—because the sum of the increase, it is that he selleth thee.
text.KJV: 25:16 According to the multitude of yeeres, thou shalt encrease the price thereof, and according to the fewnesse of yeeres, thou shalt diminish the price of it: for according to the number of the yeeres of the fruites doeth hee sell vnto thee.
text.NABRE: 25:16 When the years are many, the price shall be so much the more; when the years are few, the price shall be so much the less. For it is really the number of harvests that the person sells you.

text.NRSV: You shall not cheat one another, but you shall fear your God; for I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: So then ye shall not overreach one another; but thou shalt stand in awe of thy God,—for, I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 25:17 Yee shall not therefore oppresse one another; but thou shalt feare thy God: For I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 25:17 Do not deal unfairly with one another, then; but stand in fear of your God. I, the LORD, am your God.

text.NRSV: You shall observe my statutes and faithfully keep my ordinances, so that you may live on the land securely.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore ye shall do my statutes, and my regulations, shall ye observe and do them,—so shall ye dwell upon the land with confidence;
text.KJV: 25:18 ¶ Wherefore ye shall do my Statutes, and keepe my Iudgements, and doe them, and ye shall dwell in the land in safetie.
