text.NABRE: 25:18 Observe my statutes and be careful to keep my ordinances, so that you will dwell securely in the land.

text.NRSV: The land will yield its fruit, and you will eat your fill and live on it securely.
text.Rotherham: and the land shall yield her fruit; and ye shall eat to the full,—and shall dwell with confidence thereupon.
text.KJV: 25:19 And the land shall yeeld her fruit, and ye shal eat your fill, and dwell therin in safetie.
text.NABRE: 25:19 The land will yield its fruit and you will eat your fill, and live there securely.

text.NRSV: Should you ask, “What shall we eat in the seventh year, if we may not sow or gather in our crop?”
text.Rotherham: And since ye may say, What shall we eat in the seventh year? Lo! we are not to sow, neither are we to gather our increase!
text.KJV: 25:20 And if ye shall say, What shall we eate the seuenth yeere? Behold, we shall not sow, nor gather in our increase:
text.NABRE: 25:20 And if you say, “What shall we eat in the seventh year, if we do not sow or reap our crop?”

text.NRSV: I will order my blessing for you in the sixth year, so that it will yield a crop for three years.
text.Rotherham: Therefore will I command my blessing upon you, in the sixth year,—and it shall make the increase of three years;
text.KJV: 25:21 Then I will command my blessing vpon you in the sixt yeere, and it shall bring forth fruit for three yeeres.
text.NABRE: 25:21 I will command such a blessing for you in the sixth year that there will be crop enough for three years,

text.NRSV: When you sow in the eighth year, you will be eating from the old yield; until the ninth year, when its produce comes in, you shall eat the old.
text.Rotherham: and ye shall sow, the eighth year, and eat of old store,—until the ninth year until the coming in of the increase thereof, shall ye eat old store.
text.KJV: 25:22 And ye shall sow the eight yeere, and eat yet of old fruit, vntill the ninth yeere: vntill her fruits come in, ye shall eate of the old store.
text.NABRE: 25:22 and when you sow in the eighth year, you will still be eating from the old crop; even into the ninth year, until the crop comes in, you will still be eating from the old crop. Redemption of Property.

text.NRSV: The land shall not be sold in perpetuity, for the land is mine; with me you are but aliens and tenants.
text.Rotherham: The land moreover shall not be sold beyond recovery, for, mine, is the land,—for, sojourners and settlers, ye are with me.
text.KJV: 25:23 ¶ The land shall not be sold for euer: for the land is mine, for ye were strangers and soiourners with me.
text.NABRE: 25:23 The land shall not be sold irrevocably; for the land is mine, and you are but resident aliens and under my authority.

text.NRSV: Throughout the land that you hold, you shall provide for the redemption of the land.
text.Rotherham: And, in all the land of your possession, a right of redemption, shall ye give to the land.
text.KJV: 25:24 And in all the land of your possession, ye shall grant a redemption for the land.
text.NABRE: 25:24 Therefore, in every part of the country that you occupy, you must permit the land to be redeemed.

text.NRSV: If anyone of your kin falls into difficulty and sells a piece of property, then the next of kin shall come and redeem what the relative has sold.
text.Rotherham: When thy brother waxeth poor, and so selleth aught of his possession, then may his kinsman that is near unto him come in, and redeem that which was sold by his brother.
text.KJV: 25:25 ¶ If thy brother be waxen poore, and hath sold away some of his possession, and if any of his kinne come to redeeme it, then shall hee redeeme that which his brother sold.
text.NABRE: 25:25 When one of your kindred is reduced to poverty and has to sell some property, that person’s closest relative, who has the duty to redeem it, shall come and redeem what the relative has sold.

text.NRSV: If the person has no one to redeem it, but then prospers and finds sufficient means to do so,
text.Rotherham: And, when, any man, hath no kinsman,—but his own hand getteth enough, so that he findeth what is needed to redeem it,
text.KJV: 25:26 And if the man haue none to redeeme it, and himselfe bee able to redeeme it:
text.NABRE: 25:26 If, however, the person has no relative to redeem it, but later on acquires sufficient means to redeem it,

text.NRSV: the years since its sale shall be computed and the difference shall be refunded to the person to whom it was sold, and the property shall be returned.
text.Rotherham: then shall he reckon the years since he sold it, and restore the overplus to the man to whom he sold it,—and shall return to his possession.
text.KJV: 25:27 Then let him count the yeeres of the sale therof, and restore the ouerplus vnto the man, to whom he sold it, that he may returne vnto his possession.
text.NABRE: 25:27 the person shall calculate the years since the sale, return the balance to the one to whom it was sold, and thus regain the property.

text.NRSV: But if there are not sufficient means to recover it, what was sold shall remain with the purchaser until the year of jubilee; in the jubilee it shall be released, and the property shall be returned.
text.Rotherham: But, if his hand have not found enough to get it back unto him, then shall that which he sold remain in the hand of him that bought it, until the year of the jubilee,—and shall go out in the jubilee, and he shall return unto his possession.
text.KJV: 25:28 But if he be not able to restore it to him, then that which is sold, shall remaine in the hand of him that hath bought it, vntill the yeere of Iubile: and in the Iubile it shall goe out, and he shall returne vnto his possession.
text.NABRE: 25:28 But if the person does not acquire sufficient means to buy back the land, what was sold shall remain in the possession of the purchaser until the year of the jubilee, when it must be released and returned to the original owner.

text.NRSV: If anyone sells a dwelling house in a walled city, then the right of redemption shall be limited to a full year after its sale; the right of redemption lasts a full year.
text.Rotherham: And, when, any man, selleth a dwelling-house in a walled city, then shall his right of redemption remain until the completion of a year after he sold it,—for, [a year of] days, shall his right of redemption remain.
text.KJV: 25:29 And if a man sell a dwelling house in a walled citie, then he may redeeme it within a whole yeere after it is solde: within a full yeere may he redeeme it.
text.NABRE: 25:29 When someone sells a dwelling in a walled town, it can be redeemed up to a full year after its sale—the redemption period is one year.

text.NRSV: If it is not redeemed before a full year has elapsed, a house that is in a walled city shall pass in perpetuity to the purchaser, throughout the generations; it shall not be released in the jubilee.
text.Rotherham: But, if it be not redeemed before the end of a full year, then shall the house that is in the city that hath walls be confirmed, beyond recovery, to him who bought it, unto his generations,—it shall not go out in the jubilee.
text.KJV: 25:30 And if it be not redeemed within the space of a full yeere, then the house that is in the walled citie, shall be stablished for euer to him that bought it, throughout his generations: it shall not goe out in the Iubile.
text.NABRE: 25:30 But if such a house in a walled town has not been redeemed at the end of a full year, it shall belong irrevocably to the purchaser throughout the generations; it shall not be released in the jubilee.

text.NRSV: But houses in villages that have no walls around them shall be classed as open country; they may be redeemed, and they shall be released in the jubilee.
text.Rotherham: But as for the houses of villages which have no wall round about them, with the fields of land, shall it be reckoned,—a right of redemption, shall belong to it, and, in the jubilee, shall it go out.
text.KJV: 25:31 But the houses of the villages which haue no walles round about them, shall bee counted as the fields of the countrey: they may ee redeemed, and they shall goe out in the Iubile.
text.NABRE: 25:31 However, houses in villages that are not encircled by walls shall be reckoned as part of the surrounding farm land; they may be redeemed, and in the jubilee they must be released.

text.NRSV: As for the cities of the Levites, the Levites shall forever have the right of redemption of the houses in the cities belonging to them.
text.Rotherham: And as for the cities of the Levites, the houses of the cities of their possession, an age-abiding right of redemption, shall pertain unto the Levites.
text.KJV: 25:32 Notwithstanding, the cities of the Leuites, and the houses of the cities of their possession, may the Leuites redeeme at any time.
text.NABRE: 25:32 In levitical cities the Levites shall always have the right to redeem the houses in the cities that are in their possession.

text.NRSV: Such property as may be redeemed from the Levites—houses sold in a city belonging to them—shall be released in the jubilee; because the houses in the cities of the Levites are their possession among the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And, if one of the Levites should not redeem, then shall the sale of the house and the city of his possession go out in the jubilee; for, the houses of the cities of the Levites are their possession, in the midst of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 25:33 And if a man purchase of the Leuites, then the house that was sold, and the citie of his possession shall goe out in the yeere of Iubile: for the houses of the cities of the Leuites are their possession among the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: 25:33 As for levitical property that goes unredeemed—houses sold in cities of their possession shall be released in the jubilee; for the houses in levitical cities are their possession in the midst of the Israelites.

text.NRSV: But the open land around their cities may not be sold; for that is their possession for all time.
text.Rotherham: But the field of the pasture-land of their cities, shall not be sold,—for an age-abiding possession, it is unto them.
text.KJV: 25:34 But the field of the suburbs of their cities may not be sold, for it is their perpetuall possession.
text.NABRE: 25:34 Moreover, the pasture land belonging to their cities shall not be sold at all; it must always remain their possession.

text.NRSV: If any of your kin fall into difficulty and become dependent on you, you shall support them; they shall live with you as though resident aliens.
text.Rotherham: And, when thy brother waxeth poor, and his hand becometh feeble with thee, then shalt thou strengthen him, as a sojourner and a settler, so shall he live with thee.
text.KJV: 25:35 ¶ And if thy brother bee waxen poore, and fallen in decay with thee, then thou shalt relieue him, yea though he be a stranger, or a soiourner, that hee may liue with thee.
text.NABRE: 25:35 When one of your kindred is reduced to poverty and becomes indebted to you, you shall support that person like a resident alien; let your kindred live with you.

text.NRSV: Do not take interest in advance or otherwise make a profit from them, but fear your God; let them live with you.
text.Rotherham: Do not accept from him interest or profit, but stand thou in awe of thy God,—so shall thy brother live with thee.
text.KJV: 25:36 Take thou no vsurie of him, or increase: but feare thy God, that thy brother may liue with thee.
text.NABRE: 25:36 Do not exact interest in advance or accrued interest, but out of fear of God let your kindred live with you.

text.NRSV: You shall not lend them your money at interest taken in advance, or provide them food at a profit.
text.Rotherham: Thy silver, shalt thou not give him on interest,—neither, for profit, shalt thou give him thy food.
text.KJV: 25:37 Thou shalt not giue him thy money vpon vsurie, nor lend him thy victuals for increase.
text.NABRE: 25:37 Do not give your money at interest or your food at a profit.

text.NRSV: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, to give you the land of Canaan, to be your God.
text.Rotherham: I—Yahweh, am your God, who brought you forth out of the land of Egypt,—to give unto you the land of Canaan, to become your God,
text.KJV: 25:38 I am the Lord your God, which brought you foorth out of the land of Egypt, to giue you the land of Canaan, and to be your God.
text.NABRE: 25:38 I, the LORD, am your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to give you the land of Canaan and to be your God.

text.NRSV: If any who are dependent on you become so impoverished that they sell themselves to you, you shall not make them serve as slaves.
text.Rotherham: And when thy brother waxeth poor with thee, and so selleth himself unto thee, thou shalt not bind him with the bondage of a bondman:
text.KJV: 25:39 ¶ And if thy brother that dwelleth by thee be waxen poore, and be sold vnto thee, thou shalt not compell him to serue as a bond seruant.
text.NABRE: 25:39 When your kindred with you, having been so reduced to poverty, sell themselves to you, do not make them work as slaves.

text.NRSV: They shall remain with you as hired or bound laborers. They shall serve with you until the year of the jubilee.
text.Rotherham: as a hired servant, as a settler, shall he remain with thee,—until the year of the jubilee, shall he serve with thee:
text.KJV: 25:40 But as an hired seruant, and as a soiourner he shall be with thee, and shall serue thee vnto the yere of Iubile.
text.NABRE: 25:40 Rather, let them be like laborers or like your tenants, working with you until the jubilee year,

text.NRSV: Then they and their children with them shall be free from your authority; they shall go back to their own family and return to their ancestral property.
text.Rotherham: then shall he go forth from thee, he and his sons with him,—and shall return unto his family, and unto the possession of his fathers, shall he return.
text.KJV: 25:41 And then shall hee depart from thee, both he and his children with him, and shall returne vnto his owne familie, and vnto the possession of his fathers shall he returne.
text.NABRE: 25:41 when, together with any children, they shall be released from your service and return to their family and to their ancestral property.

text.NRSV: For they are my servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt; they shall not be sold as slaves are sold.
text.Rotherham: For, my bondmen, they are, whom I brought forth out of the land of Egypt,—they shall not sell themselves with the sale of a bondman.
text.KJV: 25:42 For they are my seruants, which I brought forth out of the land of Egypt: they shall not be sold as bond men.
text.NABRE: 25:42 Since they are my servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt, they shall not sell themselves as slaves are sold.

text.NRSV: You shall not rule over them with harshness, but shall fear your God.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not rule over him with rigour,—so shalt thou stand in awe of thy God.
text.KJV: 25:43 Thou shalt not rule ouer him with rigour, but shalt feare thy God.
text.NABRE: 25:43 Do not lord it over them harshly, but stand in fear of your God.

text.NRSV: As for the male and female slaves whom you may have, it is from the nations around you that you may acquire male and female slaves.
text.Rotherham: And as for thy bondman and thy bond-maid which thou shalt have, of the nations that are round about you—from them, may ye buy bondman and bond-maid.
text.KJV: 25:44 Both thy bondmen, and thy bondmaids, which thou shalt haue, shall be of the Heathen, that are round about you: of them shall ye buy bondmen and bondmaids.
text.NABRE: 25:44 The male and female slaves that you possess—these you shall acquire from the nations round about you.

text.NRSV: You may also acquire them from among the aliens residing with you, and from their families that are with you, who have been born in your land; and they may be your property.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, of the sons of the settlers who are sojourning with you—of them, may ye buy, and of their families that are with you, which they have begotten in your land,—so shall they become yours, as a possession;
text.KJV: 25:45 Moreouer, of the children of the strangers that do soiourne among you, of them shall ye buy, and of their families that are with you, which they begat in your land: and they shalbe your possession.
text.NABRE: 25:45 You may also acquire them from among the resident aliens who reside with you, and from their families who are with you, those whom they bore in your land. These you may possess,

text.NRSV: You may keep them as a possession for your children after you, for them to inherit as property. These you may treat as slaves, but as for your fellow Israelites, no one shall rule over the other with harshness.
text.Rotherham: and ye may take them as an inheritance for your sons after you to inherit as a possession, unto times age-abiding, of them, may ye take to be bondmen,—but, over your brethren the sons of Israel—a man over his brother, ye shall not rule, over him with rigour.
text.KJV: 25:46 And ye shall take them as an inheritance for your children after you, to inherite them for a possession, they shal bee your bondmen for euer: but ouer your brethren the children of Israel, ye shall not rule one ouer another with rigour.
text.NABRE: 25:46 and bequeath to your children as their hereditary possession forever. You may treat them as slaves. But none of you shall lord it harshly over any of your fellow Israelites.

text.NRSV: If resident aliens among you prosper, and if any of your kin fall into difficulty with one of them and sell themselves to an alien, or to a branch of the alien’s family,
text.Rotherham: And, when the hand of the sojourner and settler with thee getteth possessions, and thy brother with him, waxeth poor,—and so he selleth himself to the sojourner [who is] a settler with thee, or to one who hath taken root, of the family of the sojourner,
text.KJV: 25:47 ¶ And if a soiourner or stranger waxe rich by thee, and thy brother that dwelleth by him waxe poore, and sell himselfe vnto the stranger or soiourner by thee, or to the stocke of the strangers family:
text.NABRE: 25:47 When your kindred, having been so reduced to poverty, sell themselves to a resident alien who has become wealthy or to descendants of a resident alien’s family,

text.NRSV: after they have sold themselves they shall have the right of redemption; one of their brothers may redeem them,
text.Rotherham: after that he hath sold himself, a right of redemption, pertaineth to him,—one of his brethren, may redeem him;
text.KJV: 25:48 After that he is sold, hee may be redeemed againe: one of his brethren may redeeme him.
text.NABRE: 25:48 even after having sold themselves, they still may be redeemed by one of their kindred,

text.NRSV: or their uncle or their uncle’s son may redeem them, or anyone of their family who is of their own flesh may redeem them; or if they prosper they may redeem themselves.
text.Rotherham: or, his uncle or his uncle’s son, may redeem him, or, a near flesh-relation of his, of his family, may redeem him, or, his own hand may have gotten enough, and, so he may redeem himself.
text.KJV: 25:49 Either his vncle, or his vncles sonne may redeeme him, or any that is nigh of kinne vnto him, of his family, may redeeme him: or if he be able, hee may redeeme himselfe.
text.NABRE: 25:49 by an uncle or cousin, or by some other relative from their family; or, having acquired the means, they may pay the redemption price themselves.

text.NRSV: They shall compute with the purchaser the total from the year when they sold themselves to the alien until the jubilee year; the price of the sale shall be applied to the number of years: the time they were with the owner shall be rated as the time of a hired laborer.
text.Rotherham: Then shall he reckon with him that bought him, from the year that he was sold to him, unto the year of the jubilee,—and the silver for which he was sold shall be by the number of years, according to the days of a hired servant, shall he be with him.
text.KJV: 25:50 And he shall reckon with him that bought him, from the yeere that he was sold to him, vnto the yeere of Iubile, and the price of his sale shalbe according vnto the number of yeeres, according to the time of an hired seruant shall it be with him.
text.NABRE: 25:50 With the purchaser they shall compute the years from the sale to the jubilee, distributing the sale price over these years as though they had been hired as laborers.

text.NRSV: If many years remain, they shall pay for their redemption in proportion to the purchase price;
text.Rotherham: If there is yet a multitude of years, according to them, shall he return, as his redemption price, of the silver of him that bought him.
text.KJV: 25:51 If there be yet many yeeres behinde, according vnto them hee shall giue againe the price of his redemption, out of the money that hee was bought for.
text.NABRE: 25:51 The more years there are, the more of the sale price they shall pay back as the redemption price;

text.NRSV: and if few years remain until the jubilee year, they shall compute thus: according to the years involved they shall make payment for their redemption.
text.Rotherham: Or, if [there is] but a small remainder of years, until the year of the jubilee, then shall he reckon to himself, according to the years thereof, shall he return his price of redemption.
text.KJV: 25:52 And if there remaine but few yeeres vnto the yeere of Iubile, then he shall count with him, and according vnto his yeeres shall he giue him againe the price of his redemption.
text.NABRE: 25:52 the fewer years there are before the jubilee year, the more they have as credit; in proportion to the years of service they shall pay the redemption price.

text.NRSV: As a laborer hired by the year they shall be under the alien’s authority, who shall not, however, rule with harshness over them in your sight.
text.Rotherham: As a servant hired year by year, shall he be with him, he shall not rule over him with rigour, before thine eyes.
text.KJV: 25:53 And as a yeerely hired seruant shall he be with him: and the other shall not rule with rigour ouer him in thy sight.
text.NABRE: 25:53 The tenant alien shall treat those who sold themselves as laborers hired on an annual basis, and the alien shall not lord it over them harshly before your very eyes.

text.NRSV: And if they have not been redeemed in any of these ways, they and their children with them shall go free in the jubilee year.
text.Rotherham: But if he be not redeemed in any of these ways, then shall he go out in the jubilee year, he, and his sons with him.
text.KJV: 25:54 And if hee be not redeemed in these yeeres, then he shall goe out in the yeere of Iubile, both he, and his children with him.
text.NABRE: 25:54 And if they are not redeemed by these means, they shall nevertheless be released, together with any children, in the jubilee year.

text.NRSV: For to me the people of Israel are servants; they are my servants whom I brought out from the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: For, unto me, are the sons of Israel, bondmen, my bondmen, they are, whom I brought forth out of the land of Egypt. I, Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 25:55 For vnto me the children of Israel are seruants, they are my seruants whom I brought forth out of the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 25:55 For the Israelites belong to me as servants; they are my servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt, I, the LORD, your God.

text.NRSV: You shall make no idols and erect no carved image or pillar, and you shall not place figured stone in your land, to worship at it; for I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not make unto you idols,—neither image nor pillar, shall ye set up for yourselves, nor sculptured stone, shall ye place in your land, to bow yourselves down thereunto,-For, I Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 26:1 Yee shall make you no Idoles nor grauen Image, neither reare you vp a standing image, neither shall yee set vp any Image of stone in your land, to bow downe vnto it: For I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: 26:1 Chapter 26 - The Reward of Obedience. Do not make idols for yourselves. You shall not erect a carved image or a sacred stone for yourselves, nor shall you set up a carved stone for worship in your land; for I, the LORD, am your God.

text.NRSV: You shall keep my sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: My sabbaths, shall ye observe, And, my sanctuary, shall ye revere,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 26:2 Ye shal keepe my Sabbaths, and reuerence my Sanctuary: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 26:2 Keep my sabbaths, and reverence my sanctuary. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: If you follow my statutes and keep my commandments and observe them faithfully,
text.Rotherham: If, in my statutes, ye will walk,—And my commandments, ye will observe, and do them,
text.KJV: 26:3 ¶ If ye walke in my Statutes, and keepe my Commandements, and doe them;
text.NABRE: 26:3 If you live in accordance with my statutes and are careful to observe my commandments,

text.NRSV: I will give you your rains in their season, and the land shall yield its produce, and the trees of the field shall yield their fruit.
text.Rotherham: Then will I give your rains in their season,—And the land shall yield her increase, And, the trees of the field, shall yield their fruit.
text.KJV: 26:4 Then I will giue you raine in due season, and the land shall yeeld her increase, and the trees of the field shall yeeld their fruit.
text.NABRE: 26:4 I will give you your rains in due season, so that the land will yield its crops, and the trees their fruit;

text.NRSV: Your threshing shall overtake the vintage, and the vintage shall overtake the sowing; you shall eat your bread to the full, and live securely in your land.
text.Rotherham: Then shall your threshing reach unto the vintage, And, the vintage, shall reach unto the seedtime,—And ye shall eat your food to the full, And shall dwell securely in your land.
text.KJV: 26:5 And your threshing shall reach vnto the vintage, and the vintage shall reach vnto the sowing time: and yee shal eat your bread to the full, and dwel in your land safely.
text.NABRE: 26:5 your threshing will last till vintage time, and your vintage till the time for sowing, and you will eat your fill of food, and live securely in your land.

text.NRSV: And I will grant peace in the land, and you shall lie down, and no one shall make you afraid; I will remove dangerous animals from the land, and no sword shall go through your land.
text.Rotherham: And I will give peace in the land, And ye shall lie down, and nothing shall make you afraid; And I will take away vicious beasts out of the land; And, the sword, shall not go through your land.
text.KJV: 26:6 And I wil giue peace in the land, and ye shall lye downe, and none shall make you afraid: and I will rid euill beasts out of the land, neither shall the sword goe through your land.
text.NABRE: 26:6 I will establish peace in the land, and you will lie down to rest with no one to cause you anxiety. I will rid the country of ravenous beasts, and no sword shall sweep across your land.

text.NRSV: You shall give chase to your enemies, and they shall fall before you by the sword.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall chase your foes,—And they shall fall before you by the sword;
text.KJV: 26:7 And ye shall chase your enemies, and they shall fall before you by the sword.
text.NABRE: 26:7 You will rout your enemies, and they shall fall before your sword.

text.NRSV: Five of you shall give chase to a hundred, and a hundred of you shall give chase to ten thousand; your enemies shall fall before you by the sword.
text.Rotherham: And, five, of you shall chase, a hundred, And, a hundred, of you shall put ten thousand to flight,—So shall your foes fall before you, by the sword.
text.KJV: 26:8 And fiue of you shal chase an hundred, and an hundred of you shall put ten thousand to flight: and your enemies shall fall before you by the sword.
text.NABRE: 26:8 Five of you will put a hundred of your foes to flight, and a hundred of you will put to flight ten thousand, till your enemies fall before your sword.

text.NRSV: I will look with favor upon you and make you fruitful and multiply you; and I will maintain my covenant with you.
text.Rotherham: And I will turn unto you, And make you fruitful,—And multiply you, And will establish my covenant with you.
text.KJV: 26:9 For I wil haue respect vnto you, and make you fruitfull, and multiply you, & establish my couenant with you.
text.NABRE: 26:9 I will look with favor upon you, and make you fruitful and numerous, as I carry out my covenant with you.

text.NRSV: You shall eat old grain long stored, and you shall have to clear out the old to make way for the new.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall eat old store well seasoned,—And the old from before the new, shall ye put forth.
text.KJV: 26:10 And yee shall eate old store, and bring forth the old, because of the new.
text.NABRE: 26:10 You shall eat the oldest stored harvest, and have to discard it to make room for the new.

text.NRSV: I will place my dwelling in your midst, and I shall not abhor you.
text.Rotherham: And I will set my habitation in your midst,—And my soul shall not abhor you;
text.KJV: 26:11 And I will set my Tabernacle amongst you: and my soule shall not abhorre you.
text.NABRE: 26:11 I will set my tabernacle in your midst, and will not loathe you.

text.NRSV: I will walk among you, and will be your God, and you shall be my people.
text.Rotherham: But I will walk to and fro in your midst, And will be unto you a God, And, ye, shall be unto me a people.
text.KJV: 26:12 And I will walke among you, and will be your God, and ye shall be my people.
text.NABRE: 26:12 Ever present in your midst, I will be your God, and you will be my people;

text.NRSV: I am the Lord your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be their slaves no more; I have broken the bars of your yoke and made you walk erect.
text.Rotherham: I—Yahweh, am your God who brought you forth out of the land of Egypt, that ye should not be their bondmen; so I brake in pieces the staves of your yoke, and caused you to walk, erect.
text.KJV: 26:13 I am the Lord your God, which brought you forth out of the land of Egypt, that yee should not be their bondmen, & I haue broken the bandes of your yoke, and made you go vpright.
text.NABRE: 26:13 I, the LORD, am your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be their slaves no more, breaking the bars of your yoke and making you walk erect. The Punishment of Disobedience.

text.NRSV: But if you will not obey me, and do not observe all these commandments,
text.Rotherham: But, if ye will not hearken unto me, And will not do all these commandments;
text.KJV: 26:14 ¶ But if ye will not hearken vnto me, and will not doe all these Commandements:
text.NABRE: 26:14 But if you do not heed me and do not keep all these commandments,

text.NRSV: if you spurn my statutes, and abhor my ordinances, so that you will not observe all my commandments, and you break my covenant,
text.Rotherham: And if, my statutes, ye refuse, And, my regulations, your souls shall abhor,—So that ye will not do all my commandments, But shall break my covenant
text.KJV: 26:15 And if ye shall despise my Statutes, or if your soule abhorre my Iudgements, so that ye wil not doe all my Commandements, but that yee breake my Couenant:
text.NABRE: 26:15 if you reject my statutes and loathe my decrees, refusing to obey all my commandments and breaking my covenant,

text.NRSV: I in turn will do this to you: I will bring terror on you; consumption and fever that waste the eyes and cause life to pine away. You shall sow your seed in vain, for your enemies shall eat it.
text.Rotherham: I also, will do this unto you—I will set over you, for terror, consumption and fever, Causing the eyes to fail and the soul to pine away,—And ye shall sow, in vain, your seed, for it shall be eaten by your foes.
text.KJV: 26:16 I also will doe this vnto you, I will euen appoint ouer you terrour, consumption, and the burning ague, that shall consume the eyes, and cause sorrow of heart: and ye shall sow your seede in vaine, for your enemies shall eate it.
text.NABRE: 26:16 then I, in turn, will do this to you: I will bring terror upon you—with consumption and fever to dim the eyes and sap the life. You will sow your seed in vain, for your enemies will consume the crop.

text.NRSV: I will set my face against you, and you shall be struck down by your enemies; your foes shall rule over you, and you shall flee though no one pursues you.
text.Rotherham: And I will set my face against you, And ye shall be smitten before your foes,-And be trodden down by them who hate you, And shall flee when no one is pursuing you.
text.KJV: 26:17 And I will set my face against you, and ye shall be slaine before your enemies: they that hate you shall reigne ouer you, and ye shall flee when none pursueth you.
text.NABRE: 26:17 I will turn against you, and you will be beaten down before your enemies and your foes will lord it over you. You will flee though no one pursues you.

text.NRSV: And if in spite of this you will not obey me, I will continue to punish you sevenfold for your sins.
text.Rotherham: And, if even with these things, ye will not hearken unto me, Then will I yet further correct you seven times, for your sins.
text.KJV: 26:18 And if ye will not yet for all this hearken vnto me, then I will punish you seuen times more for your sinnes.
text.NABRE: 26:18 If even after this you do not obey me, I will increase the chastisement for your sins sevenfold,

text.NRSV: I will break your proud glory, and I will make your sky like iron and your earth like copper.
text.Rotherham: So will I break your pomp of power, And will set your heavens as iron, and your land as bronze;
text.KJV: 26:19 And I will breake the pride of your power, and I will make your heauen as yron, and your earth as brasse:
text.NABRE: 26:19 to break your proud strength. I will make the sky above you as hard as iron, and your soil as hard as bronze,

text.NRSV: Your strength shall be spent to no purpose: your land shall not yield its produce, and the trees of the land shall not yield their fruit.
text.Rotherham: And your strength shall be spent in vain,—And your land shall not yield her increase, And the trees of the land, shall not yield their fruit.
text.KJV: 26:20 And your strength shall be spent in vaine: for your land shall not yeeld her increase, neither shall the trees of the land yeeld their fruits.
text.NABRE: 26:20 so that your strength will be spent in vain; your land will bear no crops, and its trees no fruit.

text.NRSV: If you continue hostile to me, and will not obey me, I will continue to plague you sevenfold for your sins.
text.Rotherham: If therefore, ye will go in opposition to me, And not be willing to hearken unto me, Then will I yet further plague you seven times according to your sins;
text.KJV: 26:21 ¶ And if ye walke contrary vnto me, and will not hearken vnto mee, I will bring seuen times moe plagues vpon you, according to your sinnes.
text.NABRE: 26:21 If then you continue hostile, unwilling to obey me, I will multiply my blows sevenfold, as your sins deserve.

text.NRSV: I will let loose wild animals against you, and they shall bereave you of your children and destroy your livestock; they shall make you few in number, and your roads shall be deserted.
text.Rotherham: And will send among you the wild-beast of the field And it shall rob you of your children, And cut off your cattle, And make you few in number; And your roads shall be silent.
text.KJV: 26:22 I will also send wilde beasts among you, which shall rob you of your children, and destroy your cattell, and make you few in number, and your high wayes shall be desolate.
text.NABRE: 26:22 I will unleash wild beasts against you, to rob you of your children and wipe out your livestock, till your population dwindles away and your roads become deserted.

text.NRSV: If in spite of these punishments you have not turned back to me, but continue hostile to me,
text.Rotherham: And, if, by these things, ye will not be corrected by me,—But will go in opposition to me,
text.KJV: 26:23 And if ye will not be reformed by these things, but will walke contrary vnto me:
text.NABRE: 26:23 If, with all this, you still do not accept my discipline and continue hostile to me,

text.NRSV: then I too will continue hostile to you: I myself will strike you sevenfold for your sins.
text.Rotherham: Then will, I also, go in opposition to you. And, I, even I, will plague you seven times for your sins;
text.KJV: 26:24 Then will I also walke contrary vnto you, and will punish you yet seuen times for your sinnes.
text.NABRE: 26:24 I, too, will continue to be hostile to you and I, for my part, will smite you for your sins sevenfold.

text.NRSV: I will bring the sword against you, executing vengeance for the covenant; and if you withdraw within your cities, I will send pestilence among you, and you shall be delivered into enemy hands.
text.Rotherham: And will bring in upon you a sword that shall inflict the covenanted avenging: So shall ye be gathered into your cities,—Then will I send a pestilence into your midst, And ye shall be delivered into the hand of an enemy.
text.KJV: 26:25 And I will bring a sword vpon you, that shall auenge the quarell of my couenant: and when yee are gathered together within your cities, I wil send the pestilence among you, and ye shalbe deliuered into the hand of the enemie.
text.NABRE: 26:25 I will bring against you the sword, the avenger of my covenant. Though you then huddle together in your cities, I will send pestilence among you, till you are delivered to the enemy.

text.NRSV: When I break your staff of bread, ten women shall bake your bread in a single oven, and they shall dole out your bread by weight; and though you eat, you shall not be satisfied.
text.Rotherham: When I have broken your staff of bread, then shall ten women bake your bread in one oven, And give back your bread by weight,—And ye shall eat and not be filled.
text.KJV: 26:26 And when I haue broken the staffe of your bread, ten women shall bake your bread in one ouen, and they shall deliuer you your bread againe by weight: and ye shall eate, and not bee satisfied.
text.NABRE: 26:26 When I break your staff of bread, ten women will need but one oven for baking your bread, and they shall dole it out to you by weight; and though you eat, you shall not be satisfied.

text.NRSV: But if, despite this, you disobey me, and continue hostile to me,
text.Rotherham: And, if, with this, ye will not hearken to me,—But will go in opposition to me,
text.KJV: 26:27 And if ye wil not for all this hearken vnto me, but walke contrary vnto mee,
text.NABRE: 26:27 If, despite all this, you disobey and continue hostile to me,

text.NRSV: I will continue hostile to you in fury; and I even will chastise you sevenfold for your sins.
text.Rotherham: Then will I go in a rage of opposition to you,—And I, even I, will correct you seven times for your sins;
text.KJV: 26:28 Then I wil walke contrary vnto you also in fury, and I, euen I will chastise you seuen times for your sinnes.
text.NABRE: 26:28 I will continue in my hostile rage toward you, and I myself will discipline you for your sins sevenfold,

text.NRSV: You shall eat the flesh of your sons, and you shall eat the flesh of your daughters.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall eat the flesh of your sons,—Yea, even the flesh of your daughters, shall ye eat.
text.KJV: 26:29 And ye shal eate the flesh of your sonnes, and the flesh of your daughters shall ye eate.
text.NABRE: 26:29 till you begin to eat the flesh of your own sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: I will destroy your high places and cut down your incense altars; I will heap your carcasses on the carcasses of your idols. I will abhor you.
text.Rotherham: And I will destroy your high places And cut down your sun-pillars, And cast your carcases upon the carcases of your manufactured gods,—Thus shall my soul abhor you.
text.KJV: 26:30 And I will destroy your high places, and cut downe your images, and cast your carkeises vpon the carkeises of your idoles, and my soule shall abhorre you.
text.NABRE: 26:30 I will demolish your high places, overthrow your incense stands, and cast your corpses upon the corpses of your idols. In my loathing of you,

text.NRSV: I will lay your cities waste, will make your sanctuaries desolate, and I will not smell your pleasing odors.
text.Rotherham: And I will give your cities unto desolation, And make your holy places dumb,—And will find no fragrance in your satisfying odour;
text.KJV: 26:31 And I wil make your cities waste, and bring your sanctuaries vnto desolation, and I will not smell the sauour of your sweet odours.
text.NABRE: 26:31 I will lay waste your cities and desolate your sanctuaries, refusing your sweet-smelling offerings.

text.NRSV: I will devastate the land, so that your enemies who come to settle in it shall be appalled at it.
text.Rotherham: And, I, will make the land dumb, And your foes that dwell therein shall regard it with dumb amazement:
text.KJV: 26:32 And I will bring the land into desolation: and your enemies which dwel therein, shall be astonished at it.
text.NABRE: 26:32 So devastated will I leave the land that your enemies who come to live there will stand aghast at the sight of it.

text.NRSV: And you I will scatter among the nations, and I will unsheathe the sword against you; your land shall be a desolation, and your cities a waste.
text.Rotherham: When, even you, I scatter among the nations, And make bare after you, a sword, Then shall your land become an astonishment, And your cities become a desolation.
text.KJV: 26:33 And I will scatter you among the heathen, and will draw out a sword after you: and your land shall be desolate, and your cities waste.
text.NABRE: 26:33 And you I will scatter among the nations at the point of my drawn sword, leaving your countryside desolate and your cities deserted.

text.NRSV: Then the land shall enjoy its sabbath years as long as it lies desolate, while you are in the land of your enemies; then the land shall rest, and enjoy its sabbath years.
text.Rotherham: Then, shall the land be paid her sabbaths, All the days she lieth desolate, While, ye, are in the land of your foes,—Then, shall the land keep sabbath, And pay off her sabbaths:
text.KJV: 26:34 Then shall the lande enioy her Sabbaths, as long as it lieth desolate, and yee be in your enemies land, euen then shall the land rest, and enioy her Sabbaths.
text.NABRE: 26:34 Then shall the land, during the time it lies waste, make up its lost sabbaths, while you are in the land of your enemies; then shall the land have rest and make up for its sabbaths

text.NRSV: As long as it lies desolate, it shall have the rest it did not have on your sabbaths when you were living on it.
text.Rotherham: All the days she lieth desolate, shall she keep sabbath,—the which she kept not as your sabbaths,—while ye dwelt thereupon.
text.KJV: 26:35 As long as it lieth desolate, it shall rest: because it did not rest in your Sabbaths, when ye dwelt vpon it.
text.NABRE: 26:35 during all the time that it lies desolate, enjoying the rest that you would not let it have on your sabbaths when you lived there.

text.NRSV: And as for those of you who survive, I will send faintness into their hearts in the lands of their enemies; the sound of a driven leaf shall put them to flight, and they shall flee as one flees from the sword, and they shall fall though no one pursues.
text.Rotherham: And as for such as are left of you, Then will I bring faintness into their heart, in the lands of their foes,—So that the sound of a driven leaf shall chase them, And they shall flee as though fleeing from a sword And they shall fall, when no one is pursuing;
text.KJV: 26:36 And vpon them that are left aliue of you, I will send a faintnesse into their hearts in the lands of their enemies, and the sound of a shaken leafe shall chase them, and they shall flee, as fleeing from a sword: and they shall fall, when none pursueth.
text.NABRE: 26:36 Those of you who survive in the lands of their enemies, I will make so fainthearted that the sound of a driven leaf will pursue them, and they shall run as if from the sword, and fall though no one pursues them;

text.NRSV: They shall stumble over one another, as if to escape a sword, though no one pursues; and you shall have no power to stand against your enemies.
text.Rotherham: And they shall stumble one upon another as from before a sword, when, pursuer there is none; And ye shall not have wherewith to stand before your foes;
text.KJV: 26:37 And they shall fall one vpon another, as it were before a sword, when none pursueth: and yee shall haue no power to stand before your enemies.
text.NABRE: 26:37 stumbling over one another as if to escape a sword, while no one is after them—so helpless will you be to take a stand against your foes!

text.NRSV: You shall perish among the nations, and the land of your enemies shall devour you.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall perish among the nations,—And the land of your foes shall eat you up;
text.KJV: 26:38 And yee shall perish among the Heathen, and the land of your enemies shall eate you vp.
text.NABRE: 26:38 You shall perish among the nations, swallowed up in your enemies’ country.

text.NRSV: And those of you who survive shall languish in the land of your enemies because of their iniquities; also they shall languish because of the iniquities of their ancestors.
text.Rotherham: And they who are left of you, shall melt away in their iniquity, in the lands of your foes; Yea also, in the iniquity of their fathers with them, shall they melt away.
text.KJV: 26:39 And they that are left of you shall pine away in their iniquitie in your enemies lands, and also in the iniquities of their fathers shall they pine away with them.
text.NABRE: 26:39 Those of you who survive will waste away in the lands of their enemies, for their own and their ancestors’ guilt.

text.NRSV: But if they confess their iniquity and the iniquity of their ancestors, in that they committed treachery against me and, moreover, that they continued hostile to me—
text.Rotherham: Then shall they confess their iniquity, And the iniquity of their fathers, In their unfaithfulness wherewith they had been unfaithful towards me; Yea moreover, because they had gone in opposition to me,
text.KJV: 26:40 If they shall confesse the iniquitie of their fathers, with their trespasse which they trespassed against me, and that also they haue walked contrary vnto me:
text.NABRE: 26:40 They will confess their iniquity and the iniquity of their ancestors in their treachery against me and in their continued hostility toward me,

text.NRSV: so that I, in turn, continued hostile to them and brought them into the land of their enemies—if then their uncircumcised heart is humbled and they make amends for their iniquity,
text.Rotherham: I also, must needs go in opposition to them, and bring them into the land of their foes,—Save only that, if, even then, their uncircumcised heart shall be humbled, And, even then, they shall accept as a payment the punishment of their iniquity,
text.KJV: 26:41 And that I also haue walked contrary vnto them, and haue brought them into the land of their enemies: if then their vncircumcised hearts bee humbled, and they then accept of the punishment of their iniquitie:
text.NABRE: 26:41 so that I, too, had to be hostile to them and bring them into their enemies’ land. Then, when their uncircumcised hearts are humbled and they make amends for their iniquity,

text.NRSV: then will I remember my covenant with Jacob; I will remember also my covenant with Isaac and also my covenant with Abraham, and I will remember the land.
text.Rotherham: Then will I remember my covenant with Jacob Yea moreover, my covenant with Isaac, Yea moreover, my covenant with Abraham, will I remember; And the land, will I remember.
text.KJV: 26:42 Then will I remember my couenant with Iacob, and also my couenant with Isaac, and also my couenant with Abraham will I remember, and I will remember the land.
text.NABRE: 26:42 I will remember my covenant with Jacob, and also my covenant with Isaac; and also my covenant with Abraham I will remember. The land, too, I will remember.

text.NRSV: For the land shall be deserted by them, and enjoy its sabbath years by lying desolate without them, while they shall make amends for their iniquity, because they dared to spurn my ordinances, and they abhorred my statutes.
text.Rotherham: For, the land, shall be left of them, And shall be paid her sabbaths, while she lieth desolate without them, They also, accepting, as a payment, the punishment of their iniquity, Because, yea because, my regulations, they refused, And my statutes, their soul abhorred.
text.KJV: 26:43 The land also shalbe left of them, and shall enioy her Sabbaths, while she lieth desolate without them: and they shall accept of the punishment of their iniquitie: because, euen because they despised my Iudgements, and because their soule abhorred my Statutes
text.NABRE: 26:43 The land will be forsaken by them, that in its desolation without them, it may make up its sabbaths, and that they, too, may make good the debt of their guilt for having spurned my decrees and loathed my statutes.

text.NRSV: Yet for all that, when they are in the land of their enemies, I will not spurn them, or abhor them so as to destroy them utterly and break my covenant with them; for I am the Lord their God;
text.Rotherham: And yet even so when they are in the land of their foes I have not refused them Neither have I abhorred them To make an end of them, To break my covenant with them,—For, I—Yahweh, am their God.
text.KJV: 26:44 And yet for all that, when they be in the land of their enemies, I will not cast them away, neither will I abhorre them, to destroy them vtterly, and to breake my couenant with them: for I am the Lord their God.
text.NABRE: 26:44 Yet even so, even while they are in their enemies’ land, I will not reject or loathe them to the point of wiping them out, thus making void my covenant with them; for I, the LORD, am their God.

text.NRSV: but I will remember in their favor the covenant with their ancestors whom I brought out of the land of Egypt in the sight of the nations, to be their God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore will I remember in their behalf the covenant of their ancestors,—Whom, I brought forth out of the land of Egypt in the sight of the nations, that I might be their God—I—Yahweh.
text.KJV: 26:45 But I wil for their sakes remember the couenant of their Ancestours, whom I brought forth out of the land of Egypt, in the sight of the Heathen, that I might be their God: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: 26:45 I will remember for them the covenant I made with their forebears, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt before the eyes of the nations, that I might be their God. I am the LORD.

text.NRSV: These are the statutes and ordinances and laws that the Lord established between himself and the people of Israel on Mount Sinai through Moses.
text.Rotherham: These are the statutes and the regulations and the laws, which Yahweh granted between himself, and the sons of Israel,—in Mount Sinai by the mediation of Moses.
text.KJV: 26:46 These are the Statutes, and Iudgements, and Lawes which the Lord made betweene him and the children of Israel, in mount Sinai, by the hand of Moses.
text.NABRE: 26:46 These are the statutes, decrees and laws which the LORD established between himself and the Israelites through Moses on Mount Sinai.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 27:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: 27:1 V. Redemption of Offerings Chapter 27 - Votive Offerings and Dedications. The LORD said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When a person makes an explicit vow to the Lord concerning the equivalent for a human being,
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel and thou shall say unto them—When any man would consecrate and pay a vow by thine estimate of lives unto Yahweh,
text.KJV: 27:2 Speake vnto the children of Israel, and say vnto them, When a man shal make a singular vow, the persons shall be for the Lord, by thy estimation.
text.NABRE: 27:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When anyone makes a vow to the LORD with respect to the value of a human being,

text.NRSV: the equivalent for a male shall be: from twenty to sixty years of age the equivalent shall be fifty shekels of silver by the sanctuary shekel.
text.Rotherham: then shall, thine estimate be for the male from twenty years of age even to sixty years of age, yea thine estimate shall be—fifty shekels of silver by the shekel of the sanctuary;
text.KJV: 27:3 And thy estimation shall be: Of the male from twentie yeeres old, euen vnto sixtie yeeres old: euen thy estimation shall be fiftie shekels of siluer, after the shekel of the Sanctuary.
text.NABRE: 27:3 the value for males between the ages of twenty and sixty shall be fifty silver shekels, by the sanctuary shekel;

text.NRSV: If the person is a female, the equivalent is thirty shekels.
text.Rotherham: but if it be, a female, then shall thine estimate be thirty shekels.
text.KJV: 27:4 And if it be a female, then thy estimation shall be thirtie shekels.
text.NABRE: 27:4 and for a female, the value shall be thirty shekels.

text.NRSV: If the age is from five to twenty years of age, the equivalent is twenty shekels for a male and ten shekels for a female.
text.Rotherham: And, if it be from five years of age even unto twenty years of age, then shall thine estimate be for the male, twenty shekels,—and, for the female, ten shekels.
text.KJV: 27:5 And if it be from fiue yeeres olde, euen vnto twentie yeeres old, then thy estimation shall be of the male twentie shekels, and for the female ten shekels.
text.NABRE: 27:5 For persons between the ages of five and twenty, the value for a male shall be twenty shekels, and for a female, ten shekels.

text.NRSV: If the age is from one month to five years, the equivalent for a male is five shekels of silver, and for a female the equivalent is three shekels of silver.
text.Rotherham: And, if it be from a month old even unto five years old, then shall thine estimate be—for the male, five shekels of silver,—and, for the female, thine estimate [shall be] three shekels of silver.
text.KJV: 27:6 And if it be from a moneth old, euen vnto fiue yeeres old, then thy estimation shall be of the male, fiue shekels of siluer, and for the female, thy estimation shall be three shekels of siluer.
text.NABRE: 27:6 For persons between the ages of one month and five years, the value for a male shall be five silver shekels, and for a female, three shekels.

text.NRSV: And if the person is sixty years old or over, then the equivalent for a male is fifteen shekels, and for a female ten shekels.
text.Rotherham: And if it be from sixty years of age and upwards, if a male, then shall thine estimate be fifteen shekels,—and, for the female, ten shekels.
text.KJV: 27:7 And if it be from sixtie yeeres old, and aboue, if it be a male, then thy estimation shall be fifteene shekels, and for the female ten shekels.
text.NABRE: 27:7 For persons of sixty or more, for a male the value shall be fifteen shekels, and ten shekels for a female.

text.NRSV: If any cannot afford the equivalent, they shall be brought before the priest and the priest shall assess them; the priest shall assess them according to what each one making a vow can afford.
text.Rotherham: But if he be, too poor, for thine estimate, then shall he present himself before the priest, and the priest shall estimate him,—according to that which the hand of him who would vow can attain to, shall the priest estimate him.
text.KJV: 27:8 But if he bee poorer then thy estimation, then he shall present himselfe before the Priest, and the Priest shall value him: according to his abilitie that vowed, shall the Priest value him.
text.NABRE: 27:8 However, if the one who made the vow is too poor to meet the sum, the person must be set before the priest, who shall determine a value; the priest will do this in keeping with the means of the one who made the vow.

text.NRSV: If it concerns an animal that may be brought as an offering to the Lord, any such that may be given to the Lord shall be holy.
text.Rotherham: And if it be a beast whereof men may bring near an oblation unto Yahweh, all that whereof aught is given to Yahweh, shall be holy.
text.KJV: 27:9 And if it be a beast whereof men bring an offering vnto the Lord, all that any man giueth of such vnto the Lord, shall be holy.
text.NABRE: 27:9 If the offering vowed to the LORD is an animal that may be sacrificed, every such animal given to the LORD becomes sacred.

text.NRSV: Another shall not be exchanged or substituted for it, either good for bad or bad for good; and if one animal is substituted for another, both that one and its substitute shall be holy.
text.Rotherham: He shall not alter it, nor change it—good for bad or bad for good,—but if he, do, change beast for beast, then shall, both it and what was given in exchange for it be holy.
text.KJV: 27:10 He shall not alter it, nor change it, a good for a bad, or a bad for a good: And if hee shall at all change beast for beast, then it, and the exchange thereof shall be holy.
text.NABRE: 27:10 The offerer shall not substitute or exchange another for it, either a worse or a better one. If the offerer exchanges one animal in place of another, both the original and its substitute shall become sacred.

text.NRSV: If it concerns any unclean animal that may not be brought as an offering to the Lord, the animal shall be presented before the priest.
text.Rotherham: And, if it be any unclean beast whereof men may not bring near an oblation to Yahweh, then shall he present the beast before the priest:
text.KJV: 27:11 And if it be any vncleane beast, of which they doe not offer a sacrifice vnto the Lord, then he shall present the beast before the Priest:
text.NABRE: 27:11 If any unclean animal which is unfit for sacrifice to the LORD is vowed, it must be set before the priest,

text.NRSV: The priest shall assess it: whether good or bad, according to the assessment of the priest, so it shall be.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall estimate it, whether it is good or bad,—according to thine estimate, O priest, so, shall it be.
text.KJV: 27:12 And the Priest shall value it, whether it be good or bad: as thou valuest it who art the Priest: so shall it be.
text.NABRE: 27:12 who shall determine its value in keeping with its good or bad qualities, and the value set by the priest shall stand.

text.NRSV: But if it is to be redeemed, one-fifth must be added to the assessment.
text.Rotherham: But if he should please to redeem, it, then shall he add the fifth part thereof unto thine estimate.
text.KJV: 27:13 But if hee will at all redeeme it, then he shall adde a fift part thereof vnto thy estimation.
text.NABRE: 27:13 If the offerer wishes to redeem the animal, the person shall pay one fifth more than this valuation.

text.NRSV: If a person consecrates a house to the Lord, the priest shall assess it: whether good or bad, as the priest assesses it, so it shall stand.
text.Rotherham: And, when, any man, would hallow his house to be holy unto Yahweh, then shall the priest estimate it, whether it is good or bad,—as the priest shall estimate it, so, shall it stand.
text.KJV: 27:14 ¶ And when a man shall sanctifie his house to be holy vnto the Lord, then the Priest shal estimate it, whether it be good or bad: as the Priest shall estimate it, so shall it stand.
text.NABRE: 27:14 When someone dedicates a house as sacred to the LORD, the priest shall determine its value in keeping with its good or bad qualities, and the value set by the priest shall stand.

text.NRSV: And if the one who consecrates the house wishes to redeem it, one-fifth shall be added to its assessed value, and it shall revert to the original owner.
text.Rotherham: But, if he that halloweth it, would redeem his house, then shall he add the fifth part of the silver of thine estimate thereunto and it shall be his.
text.KJV: 27:15 And if he that sanctified it, will redeeme his house, then he shall adde the fift part of the money of thy estimation vnto it, and it shall be his.
text.NABRE: 27:15 A person dedicating a house who then wishes to redeem it shall pay one fifth more than the price thus established, and then it will again belong to that individual.

text.NRSV: If a person consecrates to the Lord any inherited landholding, its assessment shall be in accordance with its seed requirements: fifty shekels of silver to a homer of barley seed.
text.Rotherham: And if of the field of his possession any man would hallow unto Yahweh, then shall thine estimate be according to the seed thereof,—the seed of a homer of barley, at fifty shekels of silver.
text.KJV: 27:16 And if a man shall sanctifie vnto the Lord some part of a field of his possession, then thy estimation shall be according to the seed thereof: An Homer of barley seed shall be valued at fiftie shekels of siluer.
text.NABRE: 27:16 If someone dedicates to the LORD a portion of hereditary land, its valuation shall be made according to the amount of seed required to sow it, the acreage sown with a homer of barley seed being valued at fifty silver shekels.

text.NRSV: If the person consecrates the field as of the year of jubilee, that assessment shall stand;
text.Rotherham: If, from the year of jubilee, he would hallow his field, according to thine estimate, shall it stand.
text.KJV: 27:17 If hee sanctifie his field from the yeere of Iubile, according to thy estimation it shall stand.
text.NABRE: 27:17 If the dedication of a field is made at the beginning of a jubilee period, the full valuation shall hold;

text.NRSV: but if the field is consecrated after the jubilee, the priest shall compute the price for it according to the years that remain until the year of jubilee, and the assessment shall be reduced.
text.Rotherham: But if after the jubilee he would hallow his field, then shall the priest reckon to him the silver, according to the years that remain, until the year of the jubilee,—and it shall be abated from thine estimate.
text.KJV: 27:18 But if hee sanctifie his field after the Iubile, then the Priest shall reckon vnto him the money, according to the yeeres that remaine, euen vnto the yeere of the Iubile, and it shall be abated from thy estimation.
text.NABRE: 27:18 but if it is some time after this, the priest shall estimate its money value according to the number of years left until the next jubilee year, with a corresponding reduction on the valuation.

text.NRSV: And if the one who consecrates the field wishes to redeem it, then one-fifth shall be added to its assessed value, and it shall revert to the original owner;
text.Rotherham: But, if he that hath hallowed it should be pleased to redeem, the field, then shall he add the fifth part of the silver of thine estimate thereunto and it shall be assured to him.
text.KJV: 27:19 And if he that sanctified the field, will in any wise redeeme it, then he shal adde the fift part of the money of thy estimation vnto it, and it shall be assured to him.
text.NABRE: 27:19 A person dedicating a field who then wishes to redeem it shall pay one fifth more than the price thus established, and so reclaim it.

text.NRSV: but if the field is not redeemed, or if it has been sold to someone else, it shall no longer be redeemable.
text.Rotherham: But if he will not redeem the field, but have sold the field to another man, it shall be redeemable no longer;
text.KJV: 27:20 And if hee will not redeeme the field, or if he haue sold the field to another man, it shall not be redeemed any more.
text.NABRE: 27:20 If, instead of redeeming such a field, one sells it to another, it may no longer be redeemed;

text.NRSV: But when the field is released in the jubilee, it shall be holy to the Lord as a devoted field; it becomes the priest’s holding.
text.Rotherham: so shall the field, when it goeth out in the jubilee, be holy unto Yahweh as a devoted, field,—to the priest, shall belong the possession thereof.
text.KJV: 27:21 But the field, when it goeth out in the Iubile, shall be holy vnto the Lord, as a field deuoted: the possession thereof shalbe the Priests.
text.NABRE: 27:21 but at the jubilee it shall be released as sacred to the LORD; like a field that is put under the ban, it shall become priestly property.

text.NRSV: If someone consecrates to the Lord a field that has been purchased, which is not a part of the ancestral holding,
text.Rotherham: If, however, a field that he hath bought, which is not of the fields of his possession, he would hallow unto Yahweh,
text.KJV: 27:22 And if a man sanctifie vnto the Lord a field which he hath bought, which is not of the fieldes of his possession:
text.NABRE: 27:22 If someone dedicates to the LORD a field that was purchased and was not part of hereditary property,

text.NRSV: the priest shall compute for it the proportionate assessment up to the year of jubilee, and the person shall pay the assessment as of that day, as a holy thing to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest reckon to him the amount of thine estimate until the year of the jubilee,—and he shall give thine estimate, in that day, as holy unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 27:23 Then the Priest shall reckon vnto him the worth of thy estimation, euen vnto the yeere of the Iubile, and hee shall giue thine estimation in that day, as a holy thing vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 27:23 the priest shall compute its value in proportion to the number of years until the next jubilee, and on the same day the person shall pay the price thus established, a sacred donation to the LORD;

text.NRSV: In the year of jubilee the field shall return to the one from whom it was bought, whose holding the land is.
text.Rotherham: In the year of the jubilee, shall the field return unto him from whom he bought it to him whose it was as a possession in the land.
text.KJV: 27:24 In the yeere of the Iubile, the field shall returne vnto him of whom it was bought, euen to him to whom the possession of the land did belong.
text.NABRE: 27:24 at the jubilee the field shall revert to the hereditary owner of this land from whom it had been purchased.

text.NRSV: All assessments shall be by the sanctuary shekel: twenty gerahs shall make a shekel.
text.Rotherham: And, every estimate of thine, shall be by the holy shekel,—twenty gerahs, make the shekel.
text.KJV: 27:25 And all thy estimations shall be according to the shekel of the Sanctuarie: twentie Gerahs shall bee the shekel.
text.NABRE: 27:25 Every valuation shall be made according to the standard of the sanctuary shekel. There are twenty gerahs to the shekel.

text.NRSV: A firstling of animals, however, which as a firstling belongs to the Lord, cannot be consecrated by anyone; whether ox or sheep, it is the Lord’s.
text.Rotherham: Only the firstling which is born a firstling to Yahweh, among beasts, no man shall hallow it,—whether ox or sheep, unto Yahweh, it belongeth.
text.KJV: 27:26 ¶ Onely the firstling of the beasts which should be the Lords firstling, no man shall sanctifie it, whether it bee oxe, or sheepe: It is the Lords.
text.NABRE: 27:26 Irredeemable Offerings. Note that a firstborn animal, which as such already belongs to the LORD, may not be dedicated. Whether an ox or a sheep, it is the LORD’s.

text.NRSV: If it is an unclean animal, it shall be ransomed at its assessment, with one-fifth added; if it is not redeemed, it shall be sold at its assessment.
text.Rotherham: But, if it be among the beasts that are unclean, then shall he ransom it by thine estimate, and add the fifth part of it thereunto,—but, if it is not redeemed, then shall it be sold by thine estimate.
text.KJV: 27:27 And if it be of an vncleane beast, then hee shall redeeme it according to thine estimation, and shall adde a fifth part of it thereto: Or if it be not redeemed, then it shalbe sold according to thy estimation.
text.NABRE: 27:27 But if it is an unclean animal, it may be redeemed by paying one fifth more than its value. If it is not redeemed, it shall be sold at its value.

text.NRSV: Nothing that a person owns that has been devoted to destruction for the Lord, be it human or animal, or inherited landholding, may be sold or redeemed; every devoted thing is most holy to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Only no devoted thing which any man shall devote unto Yahweh, of all that belongeth to him,—of man or beast, or of the field of his possession, shall be either sold or redeemed,—as to every devoted thing, most holy, it is unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 27:28 Notwithstanding, no deuoted thing that a man shall deuote vnto the Lord, of all that he hath, both of man and beast, and of the field of his possession, shall be sold or redeemed: euery deuoted thing is most holy vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 27:28 Note, also, that any possession which someone puts under the ban for the LORD, whether it is a human being, an animal, or a hereditary field, shall be neither sold nor redeemed; everything that is put under the ban becomes most holy to the LORD.

text.NRSV: No human beings who have been devoted to destruction can be ransomed; they shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: As touching any one devoted, who may be devoted from among men he shall not be ransomed,—he must be, surely put to death.
text.KJV: 27:29 None deuoted, which shalbe deuoted of men, shall be redeemed: but shall surely be put to death.
text.NABRE: 27:29 All human beings that are put under the ban cannot be redeemed; they must be put to death.

text.NRSV: All tithes from the land, whether the seed from the ground or the fruit from the tree, are the Lord’s; they are holy to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, as for all the tithe of the land whether of the seed of the land, [or] of the fruit of the trees, unto Yahweh, it belongeth,—as something holy unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 27:30 And all the tithe of the land, whether of the seed of the land, or of the fruit of the tree, is the Lords: it is holy vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 27:30 All tithes of the land, whether in grain from the fields or in fruit from the trees, belong to the LORD; they are sacred to the LORD.

text.NRSV: If persons wish to redeem any of their tithes, they must add one-fifth to them.
text.Rotherham: But, if any man should be pleased, to redeem, aught of his tithes, the fifth part thereof, shall he add thereunto.
text.KJV: 27:31 And if a man will at all redeeme ought of his tithes, he shall adde thereto the fifth part thereof.
text.NABRE: 27:31 If someone wishes to redeem any of the tithes, the person shall pay one fifth more than their value.

text.NRSV: All tithes of herd and flock, every tenth one that passes under the shepherd’s staff, shall be holy to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, as for all the tithe of herd and flock, all that passeth under the rod, the tenth, shall be holy unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 27:32 And concerning the tithe of the herde, or of the flocke, euen of whatsoeuer passeth vnder the rod, the tenth shalbe holy vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: 27:32 The tithes of the herd and the flock, every tenth animal that passes under the herdsman’s rod, shall be sacred to the LORD.

text.NRSV: Let no one inquire whether it is good or bad, or make substitution for it; if one makes substitution for it, then both it and the substitute shall be holy and cannot be redeemed.
text.Rotherham: He shall not examine whether it be good or bad neither shall he change it,—or, if he do in anywise change, it, then shall both, it and what is given in exchange for it, be holy, it shall not be redeemed.
text.KJV: 27:33 He shall not search whether it be good or bad, neither shall he change it: and if he change it at all, then both it, and the change thereof, shall be holy; it shall not be redeemed.
text.NABRE: 27:33 It shall not matter whether good ones or bad ones are thus chosen, and no exchange may be made. If any exchange is made, both the original animal and its substitute become sacred and cannot be redeemed.

text.NRSV: These are the commandments that the Lord gave to Moses for the people of Israel on Mount Sinai.
text.Rotherham: These are the commandments which Yahweh commanded Moses for the sons of Israel,—in Mount Sinai.
text.KJV: 27:34 These are the Commandements which the Lord commanded Moses, for the children of Israel in mount Sinai.
text.NABRE: 27:34 These are the commandments which the LORD gave Moses on Mount Sinai for the Israelites.
